Conversely, a single vendor (e.g., a welding contractor) performs work under multiple permits across different locations, time periods, and activity types throughout the port facility. The port authority must track vendor-specific authorizations, competencies, safety briefing completion, and supervision levels for each vendor under each permit to maintain ISO 45001 compliance and contractor safety management.'
}

Table safety.risk_assessment_participant {
  risk_assessment_participant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this risk assessment participant involvement record. Primary key for the association.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the port community participant involved in or affected by the assessed risk']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the risk assessment record for the port operation or hazardous activity']
  acceptance_status varchar [note: 'The participants formal acceptance status of the residual risk level and proposed controls. For shared operations, each participant must formally accept their portion of the residual risk. Rejected status triggers escalation to port operations management and may result in operational restrictions or permit denial.']
  commitment_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the participant must implement their committed mitigation actions. Used by port safety officers to track compliance with risk mitigation commitments and trigger follow-up audits.']
  commitment_status varchar [note: 'Current implementation status of the participants mitigation commitment. Verified status indicates port safety has audited and confirmed implementation. Overdue status may trigger operational restrictions or ISPS accreditation review.']
  consultation_date date [note: 'Date when this participant was formally consulted during the risk assessment process. ISO 45001 requires documented evidence of stakeholder consultation for shared operational risks. This date represents when the participant reviewed the risk assessment and provided input.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this participant involvement record was created in the risk assessment system. Audit trail for consultation process.']
  mitigation_commitment varchar [note: 'Specific mitigation actions, additional controls, or procedural changes that this participant has committed to implement as a condition of risk acceptance. For example, a shipping line may commit to providing certified hot work operators, or a freight forwarder may commit to enhanced IMDG pre-notification. These commitments are tracked and audited by port safety management.']
  notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this participant must be notified before the assessed activity commences. True for participants with direct control responsibilities or high exposure levels. Used to generate pre-operational safety notifications.']
  participant_control_responsibility varchar [note: 'Description of the specific risk control measures and safeguards that this participant is responsible for implementing and maintaining. For shared operations like vessel bunkering or multi-party cargo handling, different participants have distinct control responsibilities (e.g., shipping line responsible for vessel hot work permits, port operator responsible for shore-side fire watch, freight forwarder responsible for IMDG documentation).']
  participant_exposure_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the participants exposure level to the identified hazard. Direct exposure indicates the participant\'s personnel or assets are directly involved in the hazardous activity. Indirect exposure indicates proximity or downstream impact. Required for multi-party risk assessments under ISO 45001 consultation requirements.']
  participant_feedback varchar [note: 'Free-text record of feedback, concerns, or additional hazard identification provided by the participant during consultation. Captures participant-specific operational knowledge and risk perspectives that may not be visible to port safety officers.']
  participant_role_in_operation varchar [note: 'The specific operational role this participant plays in the assessed activity (e.g., Vessel Operator, Shore-side Service Provider, Cargo Owner, Stevedore, Customs Inspector). Clarifies why this participant is involved in this specific risk assessment.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the safety officer or assessor who added this participant to the risk assessment. Audit trail for consultation process.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal involvement of port community participants in operational risk assessments for shared port operations. It captures participant-specific risk exposure levels, control responsibilities, consultation records, and mitigation commitments for multi-party operations such as vessel-shore interface activities, dangerous goods handling involving multiple stakeholders, and shared operational areas. Each record links one risk assessment to one port community participant with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific involvement relationship.. Existence Justification: In maritime port operations, risk assessments for shared operational areas and multi-party activities (vessel-shore interface, dangerous goods handling, bunkering operations) genuinely involve multiple port community participants simultaneously, each with distinct exposure levels and control responsibilities. A single risk assessment for a vessel bunkering operation may involve the shipping line, bunkering service provider, port operator, and potentially customs/inspection authorities, each requiring formal consultation, risk acceptance, and mitigation commitments under ISO 45001. Conversely, a single participant like a major shipping line is involved in dozens of different risk assessments across different operational areas, vessel types, and hazardous activities throughout the year.'
}

Table customer.port_community_participant {
  port_community_participant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each port community participant record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this master entity. Entity role: MASTER_PARTY.']
  account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Primary account ownership at participant level. Essential for relationship management, strategic account planning, and revenue attribution. account_manager_name is denormalized employee reference.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Country of incorporation determines trade compliance requirements, sanctions screening protocols, customs procedures, WCO membership status, and regulatory jurisdiction for all port community particip']
  flag_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.flag_state. Business justification: Vessel operators and shipping lines are registered under flag states; essential for PSC targeting, sanctions screening, regulatory compliance, and maritime authority jurisdiction determination in port']
  aeo_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the participant\'s AEO certification. Customs compliance teams must monitor this date to ensure continued eligibility for expedited customs clearance. Null if AEO certification is not held.']
  aeo_certification_number varchar [note: 'Authorised Economic Operator (AEO) certification number issued by the national customs authority. AEO status indicates a trusted trader with streamlined customs clearance privileges. Applicable to importers, exporters, freight forwarders, and customs brokers.']
  annual_teu_volume_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated annual container throughput volume in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) for this participant. Used for capacity planning, berth scheduling, equipment allocation, and commercial negotiations. Sourced from CRM account profiling.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address designated for receipt of invoices, Terminal Handling Charges (THC), wharfage (WHR), demurrage (DMG), and detention (DET) billing communications. May differ from the primary operational contact.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) or Bureau International des Containers (BIC) operator code assigned to shipping lines and transport operators. Used in BAPLIE stowage plans, COPARN pre-announcements, and IFTMIN transport instructions for EDI interoperability.']
  credit_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit limit and financial transactions with this participant (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD). Determines the currency basis for invoicing and accounts receivable.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount extended to the participant for port services before prepayment or guarantee is required. Expressed in the port\'s base operating currency. Managed in SAP S/4HANA FI and enforced by NAVIS N4 billing controls.']
  crm_account_reference varchar [note: 'Source system identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM Account entity. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the CRM and the port community master data. Enables traceability back to the originating CRM record.']
  customer_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial segmentation tier assigned to the participant based on volume, revenue contribution, and strategic importance to the port. Determines applicable service level agreements (SLA), tariff schedules, and priority berth allocation. Managed in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  customs_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official customs authority registration or accreditation number assigned to the participant (e.g., Authorised Economic Operator number, customs broker licence number, or importer/exporter code). Required for customs clearance and trade compliance operations.']
  dangerous_goods_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the participant is approved to handle, declare, or transport International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) classified cargo through the port. Approval requires IMDG certification and compliance with SOLAS Chapter VII.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant\'s account was deactivated or suspended. Null for currently active participants. Used for historical reporting, regulatory audit trails, and reactivation workflows.']
  freight_forwarder_licence_number varchar [note: 'National freight forwarding licence or accreditation number issued by the relevant transport authority. Required for participants classified as freight forwarders to legally arrange cargo transport and issue House Bills of Lading (BOL). Null for non-freight-forwarder participant types.']
  imo_company_number varchar [note: 'Unique seven-digit IMO company identification number assigned by IHS Markit on behalf of the IMO. Mandatory for shipping companies and ship operators under IMO Resolution A.1078(28). Provides a permanent, globally unique identifier for maritime entities.']
  isps_accreditation_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant\'s ISPS accreditation expires. Port security management must be notified prior to expiry to ensure uninterrupted access to secure port areas. Null if ISPS accreditation is not applicable.']
  isps_accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current ISPS Code accreditation status of the participant. Determines eligibility for access to restricted port security zones and handling of security-sensitive cargo. Mandatory for shipping lines and port agents operating under SOLAS Chapter XI-2.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent operational activity recorded for this participant (e.g., vessel call, container gate-in/out, cargo manifest submission). Used to identify dormant participants and support account review processes.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the port community participant as recorded in the official business registry of the country of incorporation. Used for all formal documentation, contracts, and regulatory filings.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant was formally onboarded and approved to conduct operations within the port community. Marks the start of the participant\'s active relationship with the port for SLA measurement and tenure reporting.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the participant\'s account within the port community system. Controls access to port services, gate entry, and TOS operations. \'suspended\' indicates temporary restriction; \'blacklisted\' indicates permanent exclusion pending regulatory review.']
  p_and_i_club_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Protection and Indemnity (P&I) insurance club providing liability coverage to the participant, particularly relevant for shipping lines and port agents. Used in claims management and incident response coordination.']
  p_and_i_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of the participant\'s P&I insurance coverage. Required for port entry of vessels and for processing liability claims arising from cargo damage, pollution incidents, or port infrastructure damage.']
  participant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the participant\'s primary role within the port community ecosystem. Determines applicable workflows, tariff schedules, and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipping_line|freight_forwarder|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent|cargo_owner|consignee|importer|exporter — promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code applicable to this participant for port service invoices (e.g., NET30, NET60, PREPAID, COD). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI customer master and governs invoice due date calculation.']
  pcs_participant_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the participant within the Port Community System (PCS) for EDI messaging, cargo manifest submissions, and stakeholder communications. Used as the primary operational identifier in PCS integrations.']
  preferred_edi_format varchar [note: 'Preferred EDI message format used by the participant for electronic communications with the port, including cargo manifests, COPARN pre-announcements, BAPLIE stowage plans, and IFTMIN transport instructions. Drives PCS integration configuration.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary operational contact at the participant organisation. Used for EDI notifications, vessel arrival alerts, cargo status updates, and billing communications from the Port Community System.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary operational contact person at the participant organisation. Used for day-to-day port operations communications, notifications, and service coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'International format telephone number of the primary operational contact at the participant organisation. Used for urgent operational communications including vessel scheduling, berth changes, and security incidents.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the participant record was first created in the port community master data system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and GDPR data retention policy enforcement.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the participant record in the port community master data system. Used for change data capture (CDC), data quality monitoring, and audit trail compliance.']
  registered_address_city varchar [note: 'City of the participant\'s official registered business address. Used in conjunction with registered_address_line1 and country_of_incorporation for complete address resolution.']
  registered_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the participant\'s official registered business address as recorded in the company registry. Used for formal correspondence, legal notices, and regulatory filings.']
  registered_address_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the participant\'s official registered business address. Required for formal correspondence and regulatory submissions.']
  sanctions_last_screened_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant was most recently screened against international sanctions lists. Compliance policy typically requires periodic re-screening (e.g., quarterly or upon each vessel call). Supports audit trail for trade compliance reporting.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the participant\'s screening against international sanctions lists (OFAC SDN, UN Security Council, EU Consolidated List). Flagged participants are blocked from port services pending compliance review. Critical for trade compliance and anti-money laundering obligations.']
  sap_customer_account_number varchar [note: 'Customer account number assigned in SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting (FI) module. Used for invoicing, accounts receivable management, and financial reconciliation of port charges including THC, wharfage, and pilotage fees.']
  sla_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the applicable Service Level Agreement (SLA) tier governing turnaround times, berth priority, equipment availability, and dispute resolution timelines for this participant. Links to the SLA master reference for KPI monitoring.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number (TIN) or VAT registration number of the participant in their country of incorporation. Required for customs declarations, invoicing, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  tos_account_code varchar [note: 'Account code assigned to the participant in the Terminal Operating System (NAVIS N4 or TOPS Expert). Used for container tracking, equipment dispatch authorisation, yard management, and billing within the TOS. Links participant master data to TOS operational records.']
  trading_name varchar [note: 'Commercial or operating name used by the participant in day-to-day port operations, which may differ from the registered legal name. Used in operational communications and TOS displays.']
  trucking_licence_number varchar [note: 'Road transport operator licence number issued by the national transport authority. Required for trucking companies accessing the port gate for container pickup and delivery. Used in gate management within NAVIS N4 for vehicle pre-registration.']
  vessel_operator_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the participant operates vessels calling at the port. When true, the participant is subject to vessel scheduling, berth allocation, and VTMS coordination requirements. Drives integration with the Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS).']

  Note: 'Master entity representing all port community participants — shipping lines, freight forwarders, cargo owners, consignees, importers, exporters, customs brokers, trucking companies, and port agents. This is the SSOT for all legal entities and organisations that interact with the port. Captures entity type, legal registration, IMO company number, ISPS accreditation status, tax identifiers, country of incorporation, and operational status. Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM (Account entity) and Port Community System (PCS) stakeholder registry.'
}

Table customer.participant_account {
  participant_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the participant account record in the port community system. Primary key for the participant_account data product.']
  account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Account management structure requires linking accounts to responsible port employees. Drives performance metrics, commission calculations, and customer escalation routing. account_manager_name is deno']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Multi-entity port operators manage participant accounts under specific legal entities for financial reporting, tax compliance, and regulatory segregation. Essential for consolidated financial statemen']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent participant (port community member) who holds this account. One participant may hold multiple accounts across terminals or service lines.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Accounts are often terminal-specific or berth-specific; required for location-based tariff application, access control enforcement, billing reconciliation, and operational capacity planning at specifi']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Participant accounts are managed by specific profit centres (container terminal, bulk cargo, RoRo, cruise) for revenue attribution, P&L reporting, and business segment performance analysis. Essential ']
  account_close_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the participant account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts. Used for lifecycle reporting and regulatory record retention compliance.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Commercial trading name of the participant account as registered with the port. May differ from the legal entity name of the participant (e.g., a shipping line may have a separate account name per service line).']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric account number assigned to the participant account. Used on invoices, billing statements, and Terminal Handling Charge (THC) documentation. Sourced from NAVIS N4 billing module and Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  account_open_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the participant account was formally opened and activated for trading with the port. Marks the effective start of the billing and commercial relationship.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the participant account governing whether the account may transact with the port. \'suspended\' indicates temporary restriction (e.g., overdue balance); \'on_hold\' indicates pending credit review; \'blacklisted\' indicates regulatory or compliance-driven prohibition.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial tier classification of the participant account reflecting volume, strategic importance, and service priority. Determines priority berthing, dedicated account management, and preferential Terminal Handling Charge (THC) rates.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the participant account by the type of port community role. Determines applicable tariff schedules, service entitlements, and regulatory obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipping_line|freight_forwarder|cargo_owner|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent|consignee|importer|exporter — promote to reference product]']
  annual_teu_volume_commitment varchar [note: 'Contracted annual Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit (TEU) throughput volume committed by the participant under their service agreement. Basis for volume-based discount tiers and tariff schedule assignment.']
  auto_suspension_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is configured for automatic suspension when credit utilisation exceeds the defined threshold or invoices become overdue beyond the permitted days. Controlled by NAVIS N4 billing credit management rules.']
  billing_address varchar [note: 'Full postal billing address for invoice delivery to this participant account. Used for physical invoice dispatch and regulatory correspondence. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  billing_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated billing contact person at the participant organisation responsible for invoice receipt, query resolution, and payment authorisation for this account.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are generated and issued to this participant account. \'per_vessel_call\' is common for shipping lines where charges are consolidated per port call.']
  billing_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for electronic invoice delivery and billing notifications for this account. Used for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) invoice transmission and automated dunning communications.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the master service agreement or port access contract governing this account. Null for open-ended agreements. Triggers renewal workflow in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM when approaching.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the master service agreement or port access contract governing this account\'s commercial relationship with the port. Links to the contract management module in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the master service agreement or port access contract governing this account. Determines when contracted tariff rates, SLA commitments, and volume discounts become applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this participant account record was first created in the system. Audit trail field aligned with ISO/IEC 27001 data governance requirements.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding balance (in account currency) the port will extend to this participant account before requiring payment or suspending services. Used by NAVIS N4 billing module to enforce credit controls on Terminal Handling Charge (THC) and other port dues.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal or external credit rating assigned to this participant account reflecting financial risk. Influences credit limit setting, payment terms, and deposit requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|unrated — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled or last-completed date for periodic credit limit review of this account. Ensures credit limits remain aligned with participant trading volumes and financial standing.']
  credit_utilisation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the credit limit currently consumed by the outstanding balance (outstanding_balance / credit_limit × 100). Used for credit risk monitoring and automated suspension triggers in NAVIS N4 billing.']
  crm_account_reference varchar [note: 'Source system account identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM corresponding to this participant account. Used for data lineage, cross-system reconciliation, and CRM integration.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which this account is denominated for billing, invoicing, and credit limit management (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD). Supports multi-currency port operations and Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF) and Currency Adjustment Factor (CAF) calculations.']
  customs_broker_licence varchar [note: 'Regulatory licence number issued by the national customs authority to the participant where the account type is \'customs_broker\'. Required for Port Community System (PCS) customs integration and trade compliance validation.']
  days_sales_outstanding varchar [note: 'Average number of days taken by this account to settle invoices, calculated over a rolling period. Key accounts receivable Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for port commercial and finance teams.']
  deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash security deposit (in account currency) held by the port against this account to cover potential non-payment of port dues, Demurrage (DMG), Detention (DET), or damage liabilities.']
  discount_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Negotiated percentage discount applied to standard tariff charges for this account. Reflects volume commitments, strategic partnerships, or long-term service agreements. Commercially sensitive.']
  edi_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is configured for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) message exchange via the Port Community System (PCS) for automated cargo manifests, container pre-announcements (COPARN), and delivery orders (D/O).']
  edi_participant_code varchar [note: 'Unique Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) identifier assigned to this account for automated message exchange via the Port Community System (PCS). Used in COPARN, IFTMIN, and BAPLIE message sets for container pre-announcement and vessel planning.']
  isps_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Accreditation number issued under the International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code confirming the participant\'s security compliance for port access. Required for shipping lines and port agents operating under SOLAS Chapter XI-2.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this participant account record was most recently updated. Used for change data capture, audit trails, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value (in account currency) of the most recent payment received against this account. Supports cash flow monitoring and reconciliation with SAP S/4HANA accounts receivable.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent payment was received and applied to this account. Used for overdue monitoring, Demurrage (DMG) escalation, and credit risk assessment.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent financial transaction (invoice, payment, or credit note) posted against this account. Used for dormancy detection and account review scheduling.']
  navis_account_code varchar [note: 'Account code as configured in the NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System billing module. Used for charge posting, invoice generation, and Terminal Handling Charge (THC) allocation in terminal operations.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current unpaid balance (in account currency) owed by the participant to the port across all invoices. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI accounts receivable sub-ledger and reconciled with NAVIS N4 billing module.']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount (in account currency) of invoices past their due date on this account. Triggers automated dunning, service suspension, or escalation workflows in SAP S/4HANA and NAVIS N4.']
  overdue_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the oldest unpaid invoice on this account has been overdue. Used for dunning level determination and credit suspension escalation.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred or contracted payment method for settling port dues, Terminal Handling Charges (THC), Wharfage (WHR), and other tariff charges on this account.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code applicable to this account (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD, PREPAID). Governs invoice due dates, Demurrage (DMG) and Detention (DET) billing cycles, and late payment penalty calculations. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA payment terms configuration.']
  service_line varchar [note: 'Port service line under which this account operates, determining applicable operational procedures, equipment allocation, and tariff structures. Roll-on Roll-off (RoRo) and container accounts have distinct billing and operational profiles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: container|bulk|roro|general_cargo|liquid_bulk|cruise|offshore — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Service Level Agreement (SLA) tier applicable to this account, governing guaranteed berth turnaround times, crane productivity rates, and gate processing commitments.']
  tariff_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the applicable port tariff schedule for this account, governing Terminal Handling Charges (THC), Wharfage (WHR), Port Infrastructure Levy (PIL), and other dues. Linked to the port\'s published tariff book.']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax registration or VAT number for the participant account entity. Required for invoice compliance, customs documentation, and World Customs Organization (WCO) trade reporting.']
  ytd_teu_volume varchar [note: 'Actual Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit (TEU) throughput processed under this account in the current calendar or fiscal year. Used to track progress against annual volume commitments and trigger tier reclassification.']

  Note: 'Commercial account record for each port community participant, representing the billing and trading relationship between the participant and the port. Tracks account number, credit limit, payment terms, account status, currency, outstanding balance, and account tier. Distinct from the participant master — one participant may hold multiple accounts (e.g., separate accounts per terminal or service line). Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM and NAVIS N4 billing module.'
}

Table customer.contact_person {
  contact_person_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the contact person record in the port community participant registry. Primary key for the contact_person data product.']
  parent_contact_contact_person_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier supporting contact hierarchy structures within a participant organisation. Points to the supervising or parent contact person (e.g., an operations manager who supervises multiple shipping agents). Enables hierarchical escalation routing for SLA breaches and operational exceptions.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, port agent, etc.) that this contact person is associated with. Foreign key candidate to the customer/participant entity.']
  accreditation_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact person\'s port access accreditation or security clearance expires. Triggers renewal workflow notifications to the participant and port security team. Null if accreditation is not required for this contact type.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the contact person for port facility access and operational authorisations. Accredited contacts have passed ISPS security screening and hold valid port access credentials. Expired or revoked contacts must be re-screened before port access is reinstated.']
  contact_person_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact person record. Active contacts are eligible to receive port notifications and access PCS services. Inactive contacts have been deactivated (e.g., staff departure). Suspended contacts are temporarily blocked pending re-verification. Pending_verification contacts are awaiting identity or accreditation confirmation.']
  contact_role_type varchar [note: 'Functional role classification of the contact person within the port community context. Drives role-based notification routing: operations contacts receive ETA alerts and vessel call confirmations; finance contacts receive invoices and THC statements; customs contacts receive cargo manifest and trade compliance notifications; emergency contacts receive ISPS and safety alerts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operations|finance|customs|emergency|commercial|technical|legal|environmental|security|management — promote to reference product]']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact person\'s standing within the participant account hierarchy. Primary contacts are the main account representatives; billing contacts receive financial documents (THC invoices, demurrage statements); emergency contacts are notified for ISPS security incidents and safety events; technical contacts handle operational system integrations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact person record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the audit trail creation event sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM (createdon field) or the ingestion timestamp for manually entered records.']
  crm_contact_reference varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this contact person in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM (Contact entity). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM source of record.']
  customs_broker_licence_number varchar [note: 'Official customs broker licence or registration number issued by the national customs authority for contact persons acting as licensed customs brokers. Required for trade compliance validation and customs clearance authorisation within the Port Community System.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department or functional unit within the participant organisation to which this contact person belongs (e.g., Operations, Finance, Customs, Logistics). Supports departmental routing of port notifications and invoices.']
  do_not_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person has opted out of all non-essential communications. When True, the contact is excluded from marketing, commercial outreach, and non-operational notifications. Mandatory for GDPR and privacy regulation compliance.']
  do_not_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person has opted out of email communications specifically. When True, the contact is excluded from email-based notifications and marketing campaigns. Supports granular GDPR consent management at the channel level.']
  do_not_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person has opted out of SMS/text message communications. When True, the contact is excluded from SMS-based vessel arrival alerts and operational notifications. Supports granular GDPR consent management.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this contact person record became operationally effective and eligible to receive port notifications and access port community services. Supports temporal validity tracking for contact hierarchy management.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this contact person record ceases to be operationally effective (e.g., upon staff departure or role change). Null for currently active contacts. Used for temporal filtering in reporting and notification suppression after departure.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the contact person. Used as the principal digital channel for ETA/ETD alerts, vessel call confirmations, invoice delivery, EDI notifications, and Port Community System (PCS) messaging.']
  email_secondary varchar [note: 'Secondary or alternate business email address for the contact person. Used as a failover channel when the primary email is undeliverable, or for routing specific notification categories (e.g., invoice copies to a shared finance mailbox).']
  fax varchar [note: 'Facsimile number for the contact person. Retained for legacy trade documentation workflows where certain customs authorities and port state control bodies still require fax-based submission of manifests and declarations.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the contact person as recorded in the CRM system. Used for personalised communications including ETA alerts, vessel call confirmations, and invoice delivery.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Full display name of the contact person as it appears in official port communications, vessel call notifications, and trade documentation. May include salutation or professional title prefix.']
  gdpr_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact person provided explicit consent for the port operator to process their personal data for specified purposes. Required for GDPR Article 7 compliance documentation and data subject rights management.']
  is_edi_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is designated as the EDI technical contact for the participant organisation. EDI contacts receive BAPLIE, COPARN, IFTMIN, and other UN/EDIFACT message acknowledgements and error notifications from the Port Community System.']
  is_isps_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is designated as the ISPS security contact for the participant organisation. ISPS contacts receive security level change notifications, port facility security alerts, and are included in emergency communication trees as required by the ISPS Code.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is designated as the primary representative for the associated port community participant. Only one contact per participant should hold this flag as True. Used to determine the default recipient for vessel call confirmations and account-level communications.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or designation of the contact person within their organisation (e.g., Operations Manager, Shipping Agent, Customs Broker, Finance Controller, Port Agent). Used for role-based notification routing and SLA escalation.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the contact person as recorded in the CRM system. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for correspondence and notification routing.']
  linkedin_profile varchar [note: 'LinkedIn professional profile URL for the contact person. Used by the commercial team for relationship management, stakeholder engagement tracking, and business development activities within Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  mobile varchar [note: 'Mobile or cellular phone number of the contact person in E.164 format. Primary channel for urgent SMS/push notifications including vessel arrival alerts, berth allocation changes, and emergency ISPS security notifications.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact person record was last updated in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM (modifiedon field). Used for change data capture (CDC) and incremental lakehouse ingestion.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the nationality of the contact person. Relevant for port access control, ISPS security screening, and compliance with national maritime security regulations governing port facility access.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or remarks about the contact person recorded by port staff or account managers. May include special communication instructions, availability constraints, or contextual information relevant to port operations coordination.']
  notification_eta_alert boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is subscribed to receive automated ETA (Estimated Time of Arrival) alert notifications when a vessel associated with their participant organisation is approaching the port. Sourced from CRM notification preference settings.']
  notification_invoice boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is designated to receive invoice delivery notifications including Terminal Handling Charges (THC), wharfage (WHR), demurrage (DMG), and detention (DET) billing statements generated by the NAVIS N4 billing module.']
  notification_vessel_call boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is subscribed to receive vessel call confirmation notifications including berth allocation, ETB (Estimated Time of Berthing), and vessel call number assignments from the Port Management Information System (PMIS).']
  pcs_contact_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned to this contact person within the Port Community System (PCS) for stakeholder messaging, cargo manifest communications, and trade documentation routing.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary business telephone number of the contact person including country and area code in E.164 format. Used for voice communications regarding vessel operations, berth scheduling, and cargo handling coordination.']
  port_access_badge_number varchar [note: 'Physical or RFID-enabled port access badge number issued to the contact person for entry into controlled port facility zones. Links to the port access control system for audit trail and ISPS compliance reporting.']
  preferred_channel varchar [note: 'The contact person\'s preferred channel for receiving port notifications and operational communications. Drives the notification dispatch logic in the Port Management Information System (PMIS) and PCS messaging engine. Valid values: email (electronic mail), sms (short message service), phone (voice call), fax (facsimile), pcs_portal (Port Community System web portal), edi (Electronic Data Interchange automated message).']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or BCP-47 language code representing the contact person\'s preferred language for receiving communications, documents, and notifications (e.g., \'en\' for English, \'zh-CN\' for Simplified Chinese, \'ar\' for Arabic). Supports multilingual port community operations.']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Formal salutation or honorific prefix for the contact person (e.g., Mr, Ms, Dr, Capt). Used in formal port correspondence, bill of lading communications, and official maritime documentation where professional titles are required.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this contact person record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution during master data management. Primary source is Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM; secondary sources include the Port Community System (PCS) and manual entry.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the contact person\'s operational location (e.g., \'Asia/Singapore\', \'Europe/Rotterdam\', \'America/Los_Angeles\'). Used to schedule notifications and communications at appropriate local times, particularly for ETA/ETD alerts across international shipping routes.']

  Note: 'Individual contact persons associated with port community participants — operations managers, shipping agents, customs brokers, finance contacts, and emergency contacts. Captures full name, job title, department, email, phone, mobile, preferred communication channel, language, and active status. Supports contact hierarchy and role-based routing for notifications (ETA alerts, vessel call confirmations, invoice delivery). Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM (Contact entity).'
}

Table customer.participant_address {
  participant_address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each participant address record in the port community system. Primary key for the participant_address data product.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, port agent, etc.) to whom this address belongs.']
  address_format_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the structural format of the address. Structured addresses follow standard street/city/postal hierarchy; unstructured addresses are free-form (common in some Asian and Middle Eastern jurisdictions); po_box and private_bag are postal delivery formats used for correspondence addresses.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line including building number, street name, or PO Box. Used in trade documentation, customs declarations, and invoice delivery per WCO data model requirements.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, floor, unit, building name, or additional location detail. Supplements address_line_1 for complete postal addressing on customs declarations and trade documents.']
  address_line_3 varchar [note: 'Tertiary address line for district, suburb, or additional locality detail. Used for complex international addresses where two lines are insufficient for complete postal addressing on BOL and customs documentation.']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record. Active addresses are used in trade documentation and invoice delivery. Pending_verification addresses are awaiting confirmation from the participant or customs authority. Superseded addresses have been replaced by a newer record but retained for historical audit.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the address purpose within port community operations. Registered office is the legal entity address; operational is the day-to-day working address; billing is used for invoice delivery and THC/WHR/DMG charge correspondence; correspondence is for general trade documentation and BOL/D/O dispatch; port_agent is the local port agent\'s operational address.']
  attention_to varchar [note: 'Name of the specific person, department, or role to whom correspondence should be directed at this address. Used on trade documents, BOL, D/O, and invoice delivery to ensure correct routing within large organisations such as shipping lines or freight forwarders.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the address. Required field for customs declarations, trade documentation, and invoice delivery. Used in Port Community System (PCS) for cargo manifest address validation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the address country. Mandatory for customs declarations, BOL preparation, and trade compliance screening. Used by PCS for country-of-origin and destination validation in cargo manifests.']
  country_name varchar [note: 'Full English name of the country corresponding to the ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country_code. Stored for display purposes on trade documents, invoices, and customs declarations without requiring a lookup join.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this address record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under ISPS Code, ISO 28000 supply chain security, and port community data governance policies.']
  customs_registered_address_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the officially registered address with the national customs authority for the participant. Customs-registered addresses are used on import/export declarations and must match the address held in the customs authority\'s trader registration system.']
  customs_registration_number varchar [note: 'Customs authority registration or trader identification number associated with this address. Required for customs declarations and trade compliance submissions. Corresponds to the Economic Operator Registration and Identification (EORI) number or equivalent national customs trader ID.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department or business unit within the participant organisation at this address. Used for routing trade documentation, customs correspondence, and invoices to the correct operational unit (e.g., Import Operations, Customs Compliance, Accounts Payable).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this address record is valid and in use for the participant. Supports temporal address management for participants who change registered office or operational addresses. Used in trade documentation to ensure the correct address is applied for a given transaction date.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this address record is valid. Null indicates the address is currently active with no planned expiry. When populated, the address is superseded after this date and a new address record should be active. Supports validity period management for port agent addresses and seasonal operational addresses.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Email address associated with this specific address record. Used for electronic delivery of invoices, D/O, trade documentation, and customs correspondence to the correct department or location within the participant organisation.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number associated with this address. Retained for legacy trade documentation workflows and customs authorities that still require fax-based submission of cargo manifests and customs declarations in certain jurisdictions.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary address for the given address type for the participant. Only one address per participant per address_type should be flagged as primary at any point in time. Used to determine the default address for invoice generation and trade documentation.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address has been verified against an authoritative postal or customs registry. Verified addresses are required for customs declarations and official trade documentation submissions to national customs authorities.']
  isps_facility_code varchar [note: 'ISPS facility code assigned to the address if it is a port facility or secure logistics facility. Required for ISPS Code compliance and port security declarations. Applicable to port agent addresses and operational addresses at ISPS-designated facilities.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language in which the address is recorded. Relevant for multilingual port communities where addresses may be recorded in local script (e.g., Arabic, Chinese, Japanese) as well as in Latin script for international trade documentation.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for geospatial analytics, port logistics routing, and intermodal transport coordination. Particularly relevant for container yard (CY), ICD, and CFS addresses.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for geospatial analytics, port logistics routing, and intermodal transport coordination. Particularly relevant for container yard (CY), ICD, and CFS addresses.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions associated with this address. Used to capture delivery instructions, access restrictions, security clearance requirements, or other operational notes relevant to port logistics and trade documentation delivery.']
  pcs_address_reference varchar [note: 'Address reference code assigned by the Port Community System for use in EDI messaging, cargo manifests, and customs integration. Used in COPARN, IFTMIN, and other EDI message types to identify the participant\'s address without transmitting full address details.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number associated with this address location. Used for operational contact at the specific address, particularly for port agent addresses and operational addresses. Formatted in E.164 international format where possible.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the address. Used for invoice delivery, trade documentation, and customs declarations. Format varies by country; validated against country-specific postal code standards in the Port Community System.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this address record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when the same participant address is maintained in multiple systems. Dynamics_365_CRM is the primary CRM source; PCS is the Port Community System; SAP S/4HANA is the ERP billing address source.']
  source_system_address_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this address record in the originating source system (e.g., Dynamics 365 CRM address GUID, SAP Business Partner address sequence number, PCS address reference). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and back-integration with operational systems.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the address. Captured as ISO 3166-2 subdivision code where applicable. Required for customs declarations in jurisdictions with state-level trade compliance requirements.']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (VAT, GST, or equivalent) registered at this address. Used for invoice generation, THC billing, and financial reporting. Required for cross-border trade documentation and compliance with national tax authority requirements.']
  un_locode varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE for the city or port location associated with this address. Particularly relevant for port agent addresses and operational addresses at seaport locations. Used in EDI messaging (IFTMIN, COPARN) and PCS cargo manifest submissions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this address record was last modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for audit trail compliance under port community data governance and trade compliance requirements.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the address was last verified. Used to determine whether re-verification is required per port security and trade compliance policies. Addresses older than the configured re-verification threshold may be flagged for review.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the address was verified. Manual verification is performed by port operations staff; postal_registry uses national postal authority data; customs_registry uses WCO or national customs authority data; third_party uses a commercial address validation service; self_declared is provided by the participant without independent verification.']

  Note: 'Physical and postal address records for port community participants, supporting multiple address types per participant: registered office, operational address, billing address, correspondence address, and port agent address. Captures street, city, state, postal code, country (ISO 3166), address type, and validity period. Used for trade documentation, customs declarations, and invoice delivery.'
}

Table customer.org_hierarchy {
  org_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each parent-child corporate hierarchy relationship record between port community participants in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  child_org_port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the child or subsidiary organisation entity in the hierarchy (e.g., regional subsidiary, local freight forwarder branch, subsidiary cargo owner). Used to traverse downward in the corporate tree.']
  primary_root_org_port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ultimate parent (root) organisation at the top of the corporate hierarchy tree. Enables direct group-level consolidation for billing, credit management, and enterprise reporting without full tree traversal.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for the creation or modification of this hierarchy relationship. \'approved\' = validated and authorised by the port commercial team; \'pending_approval\' = submitted and awaiting review; \'rejected\' = not approved; \'under_review\' = currently being assessed.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the port authority commercial officer or system user who approved this hierarchy relationship. Used for audit trail and governance compliance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy relationship was formally approved by the authorised port commercial officer. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  billing_consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether charges and invoices for the child organisation are consolidated and billed to the parent organisation under this hierarchy relationship. True = consolidated billing applies (e.g., THC, WHR, DMG charges rolled up to parent); False = child is billed independently.']
  contract_inheritance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child organisation inherits master contract terms (tariff schedules, service agreements, credit terms) from the parent organisation. True = contract terms cascade; False = child operates under its own independently negotiated contracts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy relationship record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker for data lineage, compliance, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  credit_consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credit limit and credit exposure of the child organisation is managed at the parent group level under this hierarchy. True = group credit management applies; False = child has independent credit management.']
  crm_account_hierarchy_reference varchar [note: 'The source system identifier for this hierarchy relationship as recorded in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM Account Hierarchy module. Enables traceability and reconciliation between the CRM system of record and the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this parent-child hierarchy relationship becomes valid and binding. Used for temporal validity tracking, consolidated billing activation, and group credit management effective dates.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date on which this parent-child hierarchy relationship ceases to be valid. Null indicates an open-ended, currently active relationship. Used for historical hierarchy reconstruction and audit trails.']
  governance_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary legal jurisdiction governing this hierarchy relationship. Relevant for regulatory compliance, ISPS Code administration, and Port State Control (PSC) reporting.']
  hierarchy_category varchar [note: 'Classifies the port community participant category to which this hierarchy relationship belongs. Enables segment-specific analytics and reporting (e.g., shipping line parent-subsidiary networks, freight forwarder global networks, conglomerate cargo owner structures). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipping_line|freight_forwarder|cargo_owner|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent — promote to reference product if additional values required]']
  hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business code identifying this specific hierarchy relationship, used for cross-system referencing in Port Community System (PCS) and Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM. Follows the format ORG-HIER-[alphanumeric].']
  hierarchy_depth_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth level of this relationship within the overall corporate hierarchy tree, where level 1 represents the ultimate parent (root node). Enables efficient tree traversal, roll-up reporting, and enterprise-level consolidated analytics across related port community participants.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialised path string representing the full ancestry chain from root to this node (e.g., \'/1001/1045/1078\'), enabling efficient subtree queries and hierarchy traversal without recursive joins. Populated and maintained by the TOS/CRM integration pipeline.']
  hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hierarchy relationship. \'active\' = currently valid and in use; \'inactive\' = relationship exists but not currently operative; \'pending\' = awaiting approval or activation; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted (e.g., credit hold); \'terminated\' = permanently ended.']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the parent-child relationship. \'corporate\' = legal ownership/shareholding structure; \'operational\' = operational reporting line; \'billing\' = consolidated billing and invoicing group; \'regulatory\' = regulatory compliance grouping (e.g., ISPS, IMO); \'commercial\' = commercial/contractual grouping for tariff and SLA management.']
  imo_company_number varchar [note: 'The IMO Company Identification Number assigned to the parent organisation in this hierarchy by the International Maritime Organization. Provides a unique, permanent identifier for shipping companies and operators for regulatory compliance and vessel ownership tracking.']
  is_primary_hierarchy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchy relationship is the primary (canonical) hierarchy for the child organisation when multiple hierarchy types exist. True = primary hierarchy used for default roll-up reporting and consolidated billing; False = secondary or supplementary hierarchy.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy relationship record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, commercial notes, or operational remarks about this hierarchy relationship that are not captured in structured fields. Used by port commercial and customer service teams.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of equity ownership the parent organisation holds in the child organisation for corporate hierarchy types. Relevant for regulatory reporting, group credit exposure assessment, and consolidated financial reporting. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00).']
  pcs_hierarchy_ref varchar [note: 'The external reference identifier for this hierarchy relationship as recorded in the Port Community System (PCS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and the PCS for customs integration, cargo manifest, and trade documentation purposes.']
  regulatory_group_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory grouping code assigned by the port authority or national maritime safety authority to identify this hierarchy for compliance reporting purposes (e.g., ISPS security group, PSC inspection group, IMO company identification grouping).']
  relationship_role varchar [note: 'Describes the specific role of the child organisation relative to the parent within this hierarchy link. For example, a shipping line regional office may be a \'subsidiary\', while a port agent acting on behalf of a shipping line is an \'agent\'.']
  sla_inheritance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child organisation inherits the Service Level Agreement (SLA) terms and conditions from the parent organisation under this hierarchy relationship. True = SLA terms cascade from parent to child; False = child has independently negotiated SLA terms.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this hierarchy relationship was terminated or deactivated. Supports audit compliance, regulatory reporting, and historical analysis of port community participant corporate changes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate_restructure|acquisition|divestiture|contract_expiry|regulatory_action|voluntary_exit|other — promote to reference product if additional values required]']

  Note: 'Captures parent-child corporate hierarchy relationships between port community participants — shipping line parent companies and their regional subsidiaries, freight forwarder networks, and conglomerate cargo owners. Tracks hierarchy type (corporate, operational, billing), effective dates, and relationship depth. Enables consolidated billing, group credit management, and enterprise-level reporting across related entities.'
}

Table customer.accreditation {
  accreditation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each accreditation, certification, permit, or official document record held by a port community participant or their personnel/vehicle. Primary key for the accreditation data product.']
  compliance_hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.hs_code. Business justification: Dangerous goods handling accreditations (IMDG permits) must specify authorized HS code scope. Port operators require this link to validate that a participant\'s DG accreditation covers the specific com']
  contractor_safety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.contractor_safety. Business justification: Port access accreditations (ISPS clearance, dangerous goods handling permits) must link to contractor safety qualification records. Accreditation issuance depends on safety performance verification. E']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Accreditation processing costs (ISPS verification, dangerous goods certification, port access permits) are allocated to security/compliance cost centres for regulatory compliance cost tracking and fee']
  imdg_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.imdg_class. Business justification: Dangerous goods handling accreditations specify which IMDG classes the holder is certified for; mandatory for ISPS compliance, cargo acceptance gate checks, and segregation enforcement in port operati']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant entity (shipping line, freight forwarder, customs broker, stevedoring company, trucking company, port agent, cargo owner, etc.) that holds this accreditation at the entity level. Null when the accreditation is held at individual or vehicle level only.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Port access permits and facility-specific accreditations are tied to specific terminals, berths, or ISPS security zones; required for physical access control enforcement and security zone management.']
  verified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Compliance gate process requires tracking which port compliance officer verified each accreditation. Critical for audit trails, quality control, and regulatory defense. verified_by is bare string.']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Accreditations grant access to specific security zones under ISPS Code. Links permit scope to physical zone definitions for access control enforcement. Existing security_zone_access_level is denormali']
  accreditation_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping for the accreditation. Security covers ISPS and access control documents. Trade Compliance covers customs, freight forwarder, and broker licences. Operational covers stevedoring, dangerous goods, and vessel operator certifications. Insurance covers P&I and liability certificates. Environmental covers ISO 14001 and MARPOL compliance certificates. Health and Safety covers OHS and ISO 45001 certifications.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accreditation or permit. Active indicates the document is valid and in force. Expired indicates the document has passed its expiry date without renewal. Suspended indicates temporary withdrawal of privileges pending investigation or compliance action. Revoked indicates permanent cancellation by the issuing authority. Pending indicates the application has been submitted and is awaiting approval. Cancelled indicates the holder voluntarily withdrew the application.']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the accreditation or document type. Covers entity-level accreditations (e.g., ISPS facility security certificate, customs broker licence, freight forwarder registration, dangerous goods handling certification, stevedoring company accreditation), official documents (trade licence, P&I insurance certificate, vessel operator certificate), and individual/vehicle-level permits (port access permit, ISPS security zone authorisation, biometric access credential, contractor access pass). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: isps_facility_certificate|customs_broker_licence|freight_forwarder_registration|dangerous_goods_certification|stevedoring_accreditation|trade_licence|pi_insurance_certificate|vessel_operator_certificate|port_access_permit|security_zone_authorisation|biometric_credential|contractor_access_pass — promote to reference product]']
  application_reference_number varchar [note: 'The internal or external application reference number assigned when the accreditation application was lodged with the issuing authority or the port. Used to track the application through the approval workflow and to correspond with the issuing authority during the renewal or initial application process.']
  biometric_reference varchar [note: 'Tokenised reference identifier linking this access credential to the holder\'s biometric record stored in the port\'s biometric access control system. This is a non-reversible token, not raw biometric data. Populated only for biometric-linked access credentials. Classified as restricted PII biometric data.']
  coverage_scope_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific operational scope, geographic coverage, or service categories covered by this accreditation (e.g., \'Authorised for FCL and LCL customs clearance at Port of Melbourne\', \'Stevedoring operations limited to container terminal berths 1-6\'). Provides context beyond the accreditation type classification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this accreditation record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation point for data lineage, compliance reporting, and Silver layer ingestion tracking. Aligns with MASTER_AGREEMENT RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  crm_record_reference varchar [note: 'The record identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM corresponding to this accreditation. Enables bidirectional synchronisation between the accreditation data product and the CRM system for customer-facing compliance status visibility and service request management.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The official externally-assigned certificate, licence, permit, or document number as issued by the relevant authority (e.g., customs broker licence number, ISPS facility security certificate number, port access permit number). This is the primary business identifier used in compliance gating and audit trails.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the accreditation, certificate, or permit ceases to be valid. Null for open-ended or permanent accreditations. Used as the primary trigger for renewal workflows, compliance gating in PCS and VTMS, and automated expiry alerts. Aligns with MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  file_reference varchar [note: 'Storage path, URL, or document management system reference to the scanned or digital copy of the original accreditation document or certificate. Enables direct retrieval of source documents for audit, compliance verification, and Port State Control inspections.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum indemnity coverage amount specified in the insurance certificate, expressed in the currency indicated by insurance_currency_code. Populated only for insurance certificate accreditation types. Used to verify that minimum coverage thresholds required by port bylaws are met. Classified as confidential financial data.']
  insurance_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the insurance coverage amount (e.g., USD, EUR, AUD). Populated only when insurance_coverage_amount is present.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this accreditation is a mandatory regulatory or port bylaw requirement for the participant to conduct operations. True indicates the accreditation is legally or contractually required. False indicates it is voluntary or supplementary. Used in compliance gating logic to determine whether a missing or expired accreditation blocks operations.']
  is_revoked boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this accreditation has been formally revoked by the issuing authority or the port. True indicates the accreditation is revoked and must not be used for compliance gating. Provides a fast-path check for access control systems independent of the full status field.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the accreditation, certificate, or permit was officially issued by the issuing authority. Establishes the effective start of the document\'s validity period and is used in compliance age calculations.']
  issuing_authority_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction under which the accreditation was issued. Critical for cross-border trade compliance, mutual recognition agreements, and Port State Control verification.']
  issuing_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the government body, regulatory authority, classification society, or port authority that issued the accreditation, certificate, or permit (e.g., National Maritime Safety Authority, Customs Authority, Port State Control, IMO-recognised organisation). Used for verification and audit purposes.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this accreditation record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail completeness in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the accreditation was verified against the issuing authority\'s records or through a formal audit process. Used to determine whether re-verification is required and to support Port State Control inspection readiness.']
  linked_compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Reference code or description of the specific regulatory or contractual compliance requirement that mandates this accreditation (e.g., \'ISPS Code Section 2.1.4\', \'Customs Act Section 183\', \'Port Bylaws Clause 12.3\'). Links the accreditation to the governing regulatory framework for audit readiness.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, conditions, restrictions, or remarks associated with this accreditation record. May include special conditions imposed by the issuing authority, port-specific operational restrictions, or compliance officer observations.']
  pcs_document_reference varchar [note: 'The document identifier assigned by the Port Community System (PCS) to this accreditation record. Enables direct cross-reference between the accreditation data product and the PCS for customs integration, cargo manifest compliance gating, and stakeholder messaging workflows.']
  permit_holder_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued identity document number (passport, national ID, seafarer\'s identity document) of the individual permit holder. Populated only when scope_level is \'individual\'. Used for identity verification at port access control points and ISPS security zone entry. Classified as restricted PII.']
  permit_holder_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual person (personnel, contractor, driver) who holds this permit or access credential when scope_level is \'individual\'. Null for entity-level or vehicle-level accreditations. Classified as restricted PII as it directly identifies a natural person.']
  pi_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'The Protection and Indemnity (P&I) Club insurance policy number associated with this insurance certificate accreditation. Populated only when accreditation_type is \'pi_insurance_certificate\'. P&I cover is a mandatory requirement for vessel operators and stevedoring companies operating within the port. Classified as confidential business data.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the renewal application must be submitted to the issuing authority to avoid a lapse in accreditation. Typically calculated as a fixed lead-time before the expiry_date (e.g., 30 or 60 days prior). Drives automated renewal reminder workflows and compliance monitoring dashboards.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the current state of the renewal process for this accreditation. Not Due indicates the document is current and renewal is not yet required. Renewal Initiated indicates internal renewal preparation has begun. Renewal Submitted indicates the renewal application has been lodged with the issuing authority. Renewal Approved indicates the renewed document has been received. Renewal Overdue indicates the document has expired and renewal has not been completed.']
