  revocation_date date [note: 'The date on which the accreditation was formally revoked by the issuing authority or the port authority. Null when the accreditation has not been revoked. Used in audit trails and compliance investigations.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for revocation of the accreditation, as recorded by the revoking authority or port compliance officer. Null when not revoked. Critical for audit readiness and ISPS Code enforcement documentation.']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: 'The Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tag number embedded in the physical access card or vehicle transponder associated with this accreditation. Used for automated gate access control and security zone entry tracking in NAVIS N4 gate operations.']
  scope_level varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this accreditation applies at the entity (organisation/company) level, individual (person/personnel) level, or vehicle level. Drives compliance gating logic in PCS and VTMS workflows and determines which permit holder reference fields are populated.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'The date on which the accreditation was suspended by the issuing authority or the port. Null when the accreditation has not been suspended. Used in compliance investigations and audit trails to track the duration of suspension periods.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for suspension of the accreditation, as recorded by the suspending authority or port compliance officer. Null when not suspended. Required for ISPS Code enforcement documentation and Port State Control audit readiness.']
  vehicle_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official vehicle registration plate number for vehicle-level access permits. Populated only when scope_level is \'vehicle\'. Used for automated gate access control, RFID matching, and port security zone vehicle authorisation in NAVIS N4 gate operations.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle for which the access permit is issued. Populated only when scope_level is \'vehicle\'. Determines applicable port zones, weight restrictions, and operational areas within the terminal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: truck|trailer|forklift|reach_stacker|agv|private_vehicle|motorcycle|other — promote to reference product]']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the accreditation or document has been independently verified against the issuing authority\'s records. Unverified indicates the document has been received but not yet checked. Verified indicates successful confirmation with the issuing authority. Verification Failed indicates the document could not be confirmed as authentic. Pending Verification indicates verification has been initiated and is in progress.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all accreditations, certifications, permits, official documents, and access credentials held by port community participants and their personnel/vehicles. Covers entity-level accreditations (ISPS facility security, customs broker licensing, freight forwarder registration, dangerous goods handling certification, stevedoring company accreditation), official documents (trade licences, insurance certificates including P&I cover, vessel operator certificates), and individual-level permits (port access permits for personnel and vehicles, ISPS security zone access authorisations, biometric-linked access credentials, contractor access passes). Captures document/certificate type, scope level (entity, individual, vehicle), permit holder reference, issuing authority, certificate/document number, issue date, expiry date, renewal status, verification status, file reference, security zone access level, biometric reference, revocation status, and linked compliance requirements. This product subsumes what were previously separate participant_document and port_access_permit concepts — all official documents and access permits are managed here as a single SSOT. Critical for compliance gating in PCS and VTMS workflows, ISPS Code enforcement, port zone access control, and audit readiness.'
}

Table customer.sla_profile {
  sla_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the SLA profile record. Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, or port agent) to whom this SLA profile is assigned. Links to the customer master in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM and Port Community System (PCS).']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: SLAs are often berth-specific or terminal-specific (container terminal vs bulk terminal); performance measurement, crane availability commitments, and turnaround time targets require precise location ']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: SLA targets vary fundamentally by vessel category (container, bulk, tanker); turnaround time commitments, pilotage requirements, and berth allocation priorities differ by vessel type in port service a']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The measured actual performance value for the metric_type during the measurement period. For example, actual vessel turnaround time of 26.5 hours, actual container dwell time of 4.2 days, or actual gate processing time of 12 minutes. Expressed in unit_of_measure. Sourced from NAVIS N4 TOS, VTMS, or PCS operational data.']
  bol_reference varchar [note: 'The Bill of Lading reference number associated with the cargo or container for which this SLA measurement applies. Relevant for cargo handling, documentation processing, and customs clearance SLA types. Links to PCS cargo manifest and trade documentation records.']
  breach_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of SLA breach events recorded within the current measurement_window period. Used to determine if escalating penalty tiers apply (e.g., first breach = warning, third breach = critical escalation) and to track chronic underperformance patterns.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual_value exceeded the breach_threshold for this measurement period, constituting a formal SLA breach. When True, triggers penalty/credit calculation workflows in SAP S/4HANA FI and escalation notifications in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of the severity level when a breach of the SLA threshold occurs. Critical breaches trigger immediate escalation and maximum penalty/credit terms; warning-level breaches initiate remediation planning without financial penalty. Used in SLA breach alerting and escalation workflows.']
  breach_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The value at which an SLA breach is formally triggered. May differ from target_value to allow a tolerance band (e.g., target vessel turnaround = 24 hours, breach threshold = 28 hours). Expressed in the same unit_of_measure as target_value. Used for automated breach alerting in NAVIS N4 and PCS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this SLA profile record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data governance, lineage tracking, and compliance reporting in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  credit_cap_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum cumulative credit or penalty amount that can be applied within a single measurement_window period, expressed in penalty_currency_code. Protects the port operator from unlimited financial exposure under a single SLA agreement. Confidential commercial contract term.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this SLA profile definition ceases to be binding. Nullable for open-ended profiles. When populated, triggers profile_status transition to \'expired\'. Aligns with contract expiry management in SAP S/4HANA and Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this SLA profile definition becomes binding and applicable to assigned service agreements. Aligns with contract commencement dates in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM and SAP S/4HANA contract management.']
  escalation_contact varchar [note: 'Name or role designation of the primary escalation contact responsible for managing SLA breach resolution on behalf of the port operator. Typically a Port Operations Manager or Commercial Manager. Confidential internal business contact information.']
  force_majeure_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether force majeure clauses apply to this SLA profile, suspending breach penalties during declared force majeure events (e.g., severe weather, port closures, ISPS security incidents). When True, breach measurements are paused during force majeure periods.']
  imo_number varchar [note: 'The IMO vessel identification number associated with the vessel call for vessel-operations SLA measurements. Provides a globally unique, permanent vessel identifier for cross-referencing with VTMS, NAVIS N4, and international maritime databases. Nullable for non-vessel SLA types.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this SLA profile record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer, change data capture (CDC) processing, and audit trail requirements.']
  measurement_period_end timestamp [note: 'The precise end timestamp of the performance measurement period for this SLA record. For vessel-call SLAs, this is the Actual Time of Departure (ATD); for gate processing SLAs, this is the gate-out timestamp. Captured from NAVIS N4 TOS or VTMS event feeds.']
  measurement_period_start timestamp [note: 'The precise start timestamp of the performance measurement period for this SLA record. For vessel-call SLAs, this is the Actual Time of Arrival (ATA); for gate processing SLAs, this is the gate-in timestamp. Captured from NAVIS N4 TOS or VTMS event feeds.']
  measurement_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which the actual_value measurement was sourced. Ensures data lineage traceability for SLA disputes and audit purposes. NAVIS N4 is the primary source for container terminal metrics; VTMS for vessel operations; PCS for documentation processing times. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NAVIS_N4|VTMS|PCS|TOPS|SAP|DYNAMICS_CRM|MANUAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp at which the SLA performance measurement was recorded or calculated. Provides the exact point-in-time reference for the actual_value observation. Sourced from NAVIS N4 TOS event timestamps, VTMS vessel event feeds, or PCS document processing logs.']
  measurement_window varchar [note: 'The time window over which SLA performance is measured and evaluated. \'per_call\' applies to individual vessel calls or gate transactions; \'daily\', \'weekly\', \'monthly\' apply to aggregated operational periods. Determines the frequency of SLA reporting cycles.']
  metric_type varchar [note: 'The specific performance metric being governed by this SLA profile (e.g., \'vessel_turnaround_time\', \'container_dwell_time\', \'gate_in_processing_time\', \'documentation_processing_window\', \'billing_dispute_resolution_time\', \'crane_productivity_moves_per_hour\', \'customs_clearance_time\'). Aligns with IAPH KPI framework and NAVIS N4 performance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vessel_turnaround_time|container_dwell_time|gate_processing_time|documentation_processing_window|billing_dispute_resolution_time|crane_productivity|customs_clearance_time|berth_occupancy_time — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational context, commercial commentary, or exception notes related to this SLA profile or measurement record. Used by port commercial managers and operations teams to document special circumstances, dispute context, or improvement initiatives.']
  penalty_amount_applied decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual financial penalty or credit amount applied for this specific breach event, expressed in penalty_currency_code. May differ from the standard penalty_value if partial waivers, caps, or commercial adjustments were applied. Feeds into SAP S/4HANA FI credit memo or debit note processing.']
  penalty_basis varchar [note: 'The basis on which the penalty_value is calculated when an SLA breach occurs. \'per_event\' applies a flat fee per breach; \'per_hour\' or \'per_day\' applies a rate for each unit of time in breach; \'per_teu\' applies to container volume; \'percentage_of_invoice\' applies a percentage of the relevant THC or service charge.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the penalty_value (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD, AUD). Ensures correct financial processing in SAP S/4HANA FI for multi-currency port operations.']
  penalty_type varchar [note: 'The type of financial remedy applied when an SLA breach occurs. \'credit\' reduces future invoices; \'rebate\' is a cash payment back to the participant; \'liquidated_damages\' are contractually pre-agreed fixed amounts per breach event; \'none\' indicates a non-financial SLA with reporting obligations only.']
  penalty_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The financial penalty or credit amount applied per SLA breach event, expressed in the currency defined by penalty_currency_code. For percentage-based penalties, this represents the percentage of the applicable invoice or THC charge. Confidential as it reflects commercial contract terms.']
  penalty_waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text justification for waiving or reducing the standard penalty_value for a breach event. Common reasons include force majeure, port congestion beyond operator control, mutual agreement, or first-time breach goodwill waiver. Required for audit trail in SAP S/4HANA and compliance reporting.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the SLA profile, used in Port Community System (PCS) messaging, EDI exchanges, and customer-facing documentation. Follows the format SLA-[ALPHANUMERIC].']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SLA profile (e.g., \'Premium Shipping Line Tier 1\', \'Standard Freight Forwarder SLA\'). Used in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM service agreement records and customer-facing communications.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA profile definition. \'draft\' indicates the profile is under review; \'active\' means it is currently governing service agreements; \'suspended\' means temporarily paused (e.g., during force majeure); \'expired\' means the profile has passed its effective_end_date.']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA profile by operational domain. Determines which port business process the SLA governs: vessel operations (turnaround, berthing), container terminal (dwell time, crane productivity), gate processing, documentation (customs, manifests), billing dispute resolution, or cargo handling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vessel_operations|container_terminal|gate_processing|documentation|billing_dispute|cargo_handling — promote to reference product]']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective or remediation action taken in response to an SLA breach or near-breach event. Examples include \'Additional quay crane deployed\', \'Gate lane capacity increased\', \'Vessel scheduling adjusted\'. Captured in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM service request records.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'The target date by which the remediation_action must be completed and verified. Drives service request SLA tracking in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM and ensures timely resolution of performance issues.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation action for an SLA breach event. \'open\' indicates no action taken yet; \'in_progress\' means corrective measures are underway; \'completed\' means the issue is resolved; \'waived\' means the breach penalty has been waived by commercial agreement; \'escalated\' means the issue has been raised to senior management.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Identifies which party bears primary responsibility for meeting the SLA target. In maritime logistics, responsibility may rest with the port operator (crane productivity, berth allocation), the shipping line (vessel arrival punctuality), or be shared between parties. Determines penalty applicability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: port_operator|shipping_line|freight_forwarder|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent|shared — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'The scheduled frequency at which this SLA profile is formally reviewed with the port community participant for performance assessment and potential renegotiation. Drives SLA governance calendar in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial tier classification of the SLA profile reflecting the service commitment level offered to the port community participant. Platinum and Gold tiers typically apply to major shipping lines with high TEU volumes; Bronze and Standard apply to smaller freight forwarders or occasional users.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The committed performance target value for the metric defined in metric_type. For example, a vessel turnaround time target of 24 (hours), a container dwell time limit of 5 (days), or a gate processing time of 15 (minutes). Interpreted in conjunction with unit_of_measure.']
  teu_volume decimal(10,2) [note: 'The TEU volume of containers associated with this SLA measurement event. Used for per-TEU penalty calculations, throughput-based SLA assessments, and volume-weighted performance reporting. Sourced from NAVIS N4 TOS container movement records.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure applicable to target_value, breach_threshold, and actual_value fields. Standard maritime logistics units include hours (vessel turnaround), minutes (gate processing), days (container dwell time), moves_per_hour (crane productivity), TEU per day (terminal throughput), percentage (utilisation), or count (number of incidents). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours|minutes|days|moves_per_hour|teu_per_day|percentage|count — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variance_from_target decimal(18,4) [note: 'The arithmetic difference between actual_value and target_value (actual_value minus target_value). A positive variance indicates underperformance (actual exceeded target); a negative variance indicates overperformance. Expressed in unit_of_measure. Used for SLA performance trending and continuous improvement analysis.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for Service Level Agreement definitions, targets, and measured performance outcomes for port community participants. Defines performance commitments including vessel turnaround time targets, container dwell time limits, gate processing time SLAs, documentation processing windows, and billing dispute resolution timeframes. Captures SLA tier name, metric type, target value, unit of measure, measurement window, breach threshold, and penalty/credit terms. Also stores all operational performance measurement records: measurement period, actual value, variance from target, breach flag, breach severity, remediation action taken, responsible party, and measurement timestamp. This product subsumes what was previously a separate sla_performance concept — both SLA definitions and their measured outcomes are managed here as a single SSOT. Assigned to participants via service agreements. Enables SLA reporting, penalty/credit calculation, performance trending, breach alerting, and continuous improvement tracking.'
}

Table customer.sla_performance {
  sla_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SLA performance measurement record. Primary key for the sla_performance data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service agreement or contract under which this SLA performance record is measured. Links to the customer service agreement governing the committed targets.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: SLA performance measurement and penalty calculation require cost centre attribution for management reporting, operational efficiency analysis, and cost recovery. Essential for terminal-level P&L and s']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Business justification: SLA metrics for customs clearance time (submission to release) must reference the specific declaration. Port operators measure and report customs processing performance as a key service level indicato']
  kpi_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_kpi. Business justification: SLA performance metrics for safety-related service commitments (incident response time, inspection turnaround, permit processing) should reference underlying safety KPIs for integrated performance rep']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: SLA measurement, penalty calculation, and credit application are tracked at account level for participants with multiple service accounts. Enables account-specific performance reporting and penalty in']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, port agent, etc.) whose SLA performance is being tracked.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: SLA performance is measured at specific terminals or berths; root cause analysis, remediation planning, and capacity constraint identification require location-level granularity for operational improv']
  sla_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SLA metric definition record that specifies the metric name, unit of measure, and measurement methodology being evaluated in this performance record.']
  vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vessel port call associated with this SLA measurement, where applicable (e.g., for vessel turnaround time, berth waiting time, or crane productivity metrics). Null for non-vessel-related SLA metrics such as documentation or billing.']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Performance measurement and breach analysis must segment by vessel category; operational benchmarks, turnaround time standards, and penalty calculations differ significantly across container, bulk, an']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual measured performance value achieved during the measurement period for this SLA metric. Sourced from operational systems such as NAVIS N4 TOS, VTMS, or Port Community System. Compared against target_value to determine compliance.']
  breach_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the actual value exceeded or fell below the SLA target. Critical for metrics where both over and under performance may be contractually significant (e.g., crane productivity above target is positive, but vessel turnaround time above target is a breach).']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA measurement event constitutes a breach of the committed target. True when actual performance fails to meet the contractual SLA threshold. Drives penalty/credit calculation and escalation workflows in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the SLA breach when breach_flag is True. Drives escalation priority, penalty tier, and remediation urgency. \'none\' applies when no breach occurred. Severity tiers are defined in the service agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this SLA performance measurement record was first created in the system. Represents the audit creation timestamp, distinct from the measurement_event_timestamp which captures the real-world business event time.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the port community participant was formally notified of this SLA breach or performance event. Populated only when customer_notified_flag is True. Supports contractual notification timeline compliance.']
  customer_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port community participant has been formally notified of this SLA breach event. Notification may be via Port Community System (PCS) messaging, EDI, or Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM email. Required for contractual transparency obligations.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Classification of the port community participant type for this SLA performance record. Enables segment-level SLA reporting and benchmarking. Denormalized from the customer profile for analytical efficiency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipping_line|freight_forwarder|cargo_owner|consignee|importer|exporter|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent — promote to reference product]']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which the actual_value for this SLA measurement was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and auditability. \'MANUAL\' indicates the value was entered manually by port operations staff. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NAVIS_N4|VTMS|PCS|TOPS|SAP_S4HANA|DYNAMICS_365|MAXIMO|MANUAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has formally disputed this SLA measurement or breach determination. When True, the measurement_status should be \'disputed\' and the record is under review. Drives dispute resolution workflow in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of the formal dispute case raised by the customer against this SLA measurement. Links to the dispute case in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM. Populated only when dispute_flag is True.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA breach has been escalated to senior management, the commercial team, or the customer relationship manager. Triggered by critical breach severity or repeated breaches within a measurement period.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this SLA breach was formally escalated. Populated only when escalation_flag is True. Used for tracking escalation response times and SLA governance compliance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this SLA performance measurement record was most recently modified. Tracks updates such as status changes, dispute flags, waiver grants, or remediation action updates. Supports Silver Layer incremental processing.']
  measurement_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise real-world timestamp at which this SLA performance measurement was captured or triggered. Represents the principal business event time distinct from audit timestamps. For example, the moment a vessel berth operation completed and the metric was evaluated.']
  measurement_period_end timestamp [note: 'The end date and time of the measurement period over which SLA performance is evaluated. Defines the closing boundary of the observation window.']
  measurement_period_start timestamp [note: 'The start date and time of the measurement period over which SLA performance is evaluated. Defines the beginning boundary of the observation window (e.g., start of a vessel call, start of a billing cycle, start of a gate operation window).']
  measurement_period_type varchar [note: 'Defines the granularity and type of the measurement period. \'vessel_call\' — measured per individual vessel port call; \'per_transaction\' — measured per individual cargo or gate transaction; \'daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly\' — calendar-based aggregation periods as defined in the service agreement.']
  measurement_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this SLA performance measurement event, used for cross-referencing with CRM service records, billing disputes, and customer communications. Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM SLA KPI Instance ID.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this SLA performance measurement record. \'pending\' — awaiting data capture; \'measured\' — raw value recorded; \'validated\' — confirmed by operations; \'disputed\' — customer has raised a dispute; \'waived\' — breach waived per agreement terms; \'cancelled\' — measurement voided.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the SLA metric target and actual values. Examples include \'hours\', \'minutes\', \'moves_per_hour\', \'TEU_per_day\', \'percentage\', \'days\', \'count\'. Ensures consistent interpretation of target_value and actual_value fields.']
  metric_category varchar [note: 'Operational category classifying the SLA metric into a port business domain. Enables domain-level SLA reporting and benchmarking across vessel operations, terminal operations, cargo handling, documentation, billing, and marine services.']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SLA metric being measured, such as \'Vessel Turnaround Time\', \'Container Gate-In Processing Time\', \'Berth Waiting Time\', \'Crane Productivity (moves/hour)\', \'Documentation Processing Time\', or \'Cargo Availability Notification Lead Time\'.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial penalty or service credit amount applicable for this SLA breach, expressed in the contract currency. Calculated per the penalty schedule in the service agreement. Zero when no penalty applies. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity of contractual penalty terms.']
  penalty_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a financial penalty or service credit is contractually applicable for this SLA breach event. Determined by the service agreement penalty clauses. Drives the penalty/credit calculation workflow in SAP S/4HANA FI and billing in NAVIS N4.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the penalty_amount (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD, AUD). Ensures correct financial processing in SAP S/4HANA FI when multi-currency contracts are in place.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective or remediation action taken in response to the SLA breach or performance deviation. Examples include \'Additional crane deployed\', \'Priority berth allocation granted\', \'Expedited customs clearance arranged\', \'Service credit issued\'. Supports continuous improvement tracking.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation action for this SLA breach event. \'not_required\' — no breach or waived; \'pending\' — action identified but not started; \'in_progress\' — action underway; \'completed\' — action resolved; \'escalated\' — escalated to senior management or commercial team.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the root cause of an SLA breach or near-miss. Enables systemic analysis and continuous improvement tracking. \'port_operations\' — internal operational failure; \'equipment_failure\' — crane, RTG, AGV, or STS breakdown; \'weather\' — adverse weather or sea conditions; \'third_party\' — customs, pilotage, or towage delay; \'customer_delay\' — cargo or documentation delay by the customer; \'regulatory\' — PSC inspection or customs hold; \'it_system\' — TOS, VTMS, or PCS system outage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: port_operations|equipment_failure|weather|third_party|customer_delay|regulatory|it_system|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the specific root cause of the SLA breach or performance deviation. Provides operational context beyond the categorical root_cause_category for incident investigation and continuous improvement reporting.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The specific port service type to which this SLA measurement applies, such as \'Berth Allocation\', \'Container Discharge\', \'Container Loading\', \'Gate Processing\', \'Pilotage\', \'Towage\', \'Documentation\', \'Customs Clearance\', \'Intermodal Transfer\'. Aligns with the service catalogue in the service agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: berth_allocation|container_discharge|container_loading|gate_processing|pilotage|towage|documentation|customs_clearance|intermodal_transfer|yard_management — promote to reference product]']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The transaction or record identifier from the source operational system (e.g., NAVIS N4 vessel visit ID, VTMS port call number, PCS cargo manifest reference) that was used to derive the actual_value for this SLA measurement. Supports data lineage and audit trail.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The committed SLA target value as defined in the service agreement for this metric and measurement period. Represents the performance threshold the port has contractually committed to deliver (e.g., vessel turnaround ≤ 24 hours, crane productivity ≥ 25 moves/hour).']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the port operations staff member or system process that validated this SLA measurement record. Populated when measurement_status transitions to \'validated\'. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this SLA measurement record was formally validated by port operations staff or an automated validation process. Populated when measurement_status transitions to \'validated\'.']
  variance_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'The variance expressed as a percentage of the target value ((actual - target) / target * 100). Enables proportional comparison of SLA deviations across metrics with different units and scales. Stored as a raw operational measurement field captured at the time of evaluation.']
  variance_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The arithmetic difference between actual_value and target_value (actual minus target). A positive variance may indicate overperformance or underperformance depending on the metric direction (e.g., for time-based metrics, positive variance means slower than target). Stored as a raw operational field, not a derived KPI.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA breach for this measurement event has been formally waived by the port authority. Waivers may be granted for force majeure events, weather disruptions, or commercial goodwill. When True, penalty_applicable_flag should be False.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of the reason a breach waiver was granted. Common reasons include force majeure (weather, natural disaster), equipment breakdown, third-party delay (customs, pilotage), or commercial agreement. Populated only when waiver_flag is True.']

  Note: 'Operational records tracking actual SLA performance against committed targets for each port community participant and service agreement. Captures measurement period, SLA metric, target value, actual value, variance, breach flag, breach severity, and remediation action taken. Enables SLA reporting, penalty/credit calculation, and continuous improvement tracking. Distinct from analytics — this is the raw operational performance record per measurement event.'
}

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: portal|email|phone|in_person|pcs|edi|fax — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was formally closed after resolution confirmation or customer acceptance. Distinct from resolved_timestamp — a case may be resolved but remain open pending customer confirmation before closure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this service request record was first created in the data platform. Distinct from lodgement_timestamp which captures the business event time. Used for data lineage and ETL audit trail.']
  crm_case_reference varchar [note: 'The native case identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM, the system of record for service requests. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CRM system for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the dispute_amount field. Required for ports operating in multiple currencies or handling international billing disputes involving BAF, CAF, or foreign-currency THC charges.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-resolution customer satisfaction rating provided by the customer, typically on a scale of 1 to 5. Captured via automated survey sent after case closure. Used for port service quality KPI reporting and continuous improvement programmes.']
  dispute_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value under dispute for billing disputes, demurrage (DMG) claims, detention (DET) charges, Terminal Handling Charge (THC) disputes, or wharfage (WHR) fee challenges. Null for non-financial service requests. Expressed in the port\'s operating currency.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request has been formally escalated to a higher authority or management tier due to complexity, SLA breach, customer dissatisfaction, or regulatory implications (e.g., PSC complaint, ISPS security concern).']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for escalation of the service request. Supports root cause analysis of escalation patterns and identification of systemic operational issues.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was formally escalated. Null if escalation_flag is false. Used to track escalation response times and management intervention patterns.']
  imdg_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request involves dangerous goods classified under the IMDG Code, including misdeclaration complaints, handling incidents, documentation disputes, or storage compliance issues for hazardous cargo.']
  imo_number varchar [note: 'The IMO vessel identification number associated with this service request, where applicable. Provides a globally unique, permanent vessel identifier for cross-system correlation with VTMS, NAVIS N4, and Port Community System records.']
  isps_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request relates to an ISPS Code security matter, including access control breaches, security level changes, security incidents, or ISPS compliance complaints. Triggers mandatory security officer review and potential regulatory notification.']
  lodgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the service request was formally lodged by the customer or captured in the system. This is the principal business event timestamp and the anchor point for all SLA response time calculations.']
  pcs_message_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the originating Port Community System (PCS) message (e.g., COPARN, IFTMIN, or stakeholder message) that triggered or is associated with this service request. Enables traceability back to the PCS transaction for EDI reconciliation.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the service request, determining SLA response and resolution targets. Critical priority applies to vessel safety, ISPS security incidents, or cargo at imminent risk. High applies to operational disruptions affecting vessel or terminal schedules.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the service request at lodgement. Used in all customer-facing communications, Port Community System (PCS) messaging, and EDI exchanges. Format: SR-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority to which this service request must be or has been notified (e.g., IMO, National Maritime Safety Authority, Port State Control, WCO, ILO). Populated when regulatory_notification_required is true.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request requires formal notification to a regulatory authority such as the National Maritime Safety Authority, Port State Control (PSC), IMO, or customs authority. Triggered for ISPS security incidents, MARPOL environmental complaints, SOLAS safety violations, or customs fraud allegations.']
  related_vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel associated with this service request, where the request relates to vessel operations, berth allocation, pilotage, towage, or vessel-related billing disputes. Supports cross-referencing with VTMS and NAVIS N4 vessel records.']
  reopen_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this service request has been reopened after a previous resolution or closure. A high reopen count indicates unresolved root causes or customer dissatisfaction with the initial resolution. Used in quality and recurrence analysis.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service request from lodgement through investigation to resolution. Drives workflow routing and SLA monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|open|in_progress|pending_customer|resolved|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the customer interaction. Distinguishes between general enquiries, formal complaints, billing disputes, operational service requests, incident reports, permit applications, and documentation requests. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: enquiry|complaint|dispute|service_request|incident_report|permit_application|documentation_request — promote to reference product]']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardised outcome code applied at case closure indicating how the service request was resolved (e.g., resolved_in_favour_of_customer, resolved_in_favour_of_port, partial_credit_issued, no_action_required, referred_to_regulator, withdrawn_by_customer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: resolved_customer_favour|resolved_port_favour|partial_credit|no_action|referred_regulator|withdrawn|information_provided — promote to reference product]']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the resolution actions taken, findings of investigation, decisions made, and any remedial actions or credits applied. Captured by the assigned case handler at resolution. Forms part of the audit trail for regulatory and compliance purposes.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was formally resolved and a resolution communicated to the customer. Used to calculate total resolution time and SLA compliance for resolution targets.']
  service_request_category varchar [note: 'Primary business domain category of the service request (e.g., Vessel Operations, Container Handling, Billing and Tariffs, Customs Clearance, Gate Operations, Yard Management, Berth Allocation, Marine Services). Aligns with port operational domains for routing and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vessel_operations|container_handling|billing_tariffs|customs_clearance|gate_operations|yard_management|berth_allocation|marine_services|infrastructure|safety — promote to reference product]']
  service_request_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the service request, complaint, dispute, or enquiry as provided by the customer. Captures the complete context of the issue including relevant operational details such as vessel name, container numbers, BOL references, or terminal locations.']
  sla_resolution_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA resolution target was breached for this service request. True if the resolved_timestamp exceeded sla_resolution_target or if the case remains unresolved past the target. Used for SLA compliance reporting and penalty calculations.']
  sla_resolution_target timestamp [note: 'The contractual deadline by which the port must fully resolve this service request, calculated from lodgement_timestamp based on the applicable SLA tier, request type, and priority level.']
  sla_response_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA first-response target was breached for this service request. True if the acknowledged_timestamp exceeded sla_response_target or if no acknowledgement was sent before the target. Used for SLA compliance reporting and KPI dashboards.']
  sla_response_target timestamp [note: 'The contractual deadline by which the port must send an initial response to the customer for this service request, calculated from lodgement_timestamp based on the applicable SLA tier and priority level.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing granular context within the primary category. Examples include: THC dispute, demurrage claim, container damage, vessel delay, ISPS access, dangerous goods documentation, BOL amendment. Supports detailed analytics and specialist routing.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief, descriptive title summarising the nature of the service request as provided by the customer or captured by the port agent at lodgement. Used in case lists, dashboards, and customer communications.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this service request record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all customer-initiated service requests, enquiries, formal complaints, disputes, and case management records raised by port community participants. Covers the full case lifecycle from lodgement through investigation to resolution.'
}

Table customer.communication_log {
  communication_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each communication log record in the port community system. Primary key for the communication_log data product.']

  container_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.container. Business justification: Communication logs reference container_number as text. Direct FK enables message threading by container (gate notifications, reefer alerts, damage reports), operational event correlation, and customer']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Customer communication activities (service desk, regulatory correspondence, EDI support) consume resources tracked by cost centres for operational cost allocation, service costing, and customer servic']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Business justification: Communications about declaration status updates, amendment requests, or clearance notifications must reference the specific declaration. Port community systems require this link for audit trails and r']
  customs_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_hold. Business justification: Communications about hold notifications, examination scheduling, or release confirmations must reference the specific hold. Port operators need this link to track all stakeholder communications regard']
  parent_communication_communication_log_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier linking this communication to a parent communication record, enabling threading of reply chains and follow-up correspondence. Null for original (non-reply) communications.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, port agent, etc.) involved in this communication. Links to the customer master record.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Communications are often location-specific (berth allocation confirmations, gate appointment notifications, terminal access instructions); operational coordination and message routing require precise ']
  related_incident_ohs_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ohs_incident. Business justification: Communications with participants about safety incidents (incident notification letters, investigation updates, corrective action requests) require formal linkage for regulatory compliance, audit trail']
  sender_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Audit trail for port-initiated communications. Required for dispute resolution, regulatory compliance reporting, and employee performance tracking. No denormalized column exists to remove.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service request or CRM case linked to this communication. Populated for service alerts, complaint responses, and operational service correspondence managed in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel call business event linked to this communication. Populated for vessel call notifications, ETA/ETB/ETD advisories, and berth-related communications. Null when the communication is not associated with a specific vessel call.']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Vessel-related communications (ETA updates, berth requests, pilotage coordination) are vessel-type-specific; message routing, SLA tracking, and operational planning require vessel type context for app']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the recipient formally acknowledged receipt of the communication. Null if acknowledgement has not been received. Critical for ISPS security communication compliance and SLA measurement.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of documents or files attached to this communication. Supports audit completeness checks and document management workflows. Zero for communications with no attachments.']
  body_reference varchar [note: 'Reference pointer (document store path, object key, or external document ID) to the full body content of the communication. The actual message body is stored externally (e.g., document management system or object storage) to avoid large text in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The medium or interface through which the communication was transmitted. Includes Electronic Data Interchange (EDI), email, Port Community System (PCS) message, SMS, physical letter, or fax. Supports channel-level analytics and SLA tracking.']
  communication_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the communication record. Tracks progression from draft through sent, delivered, failed, acknowledged, or rejected. Supports SLA monitoring and dispute resolution.']
  communication_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the communication by its business purpose. Covers vessel call notifications, ETA/ETB/ETD advisories, documentation requests, compliance notices, billing notifications, service alerts, regulatory correspondence, and ISPS security notices. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vessel_call_notification|eta_etb_etd_advisory|documentation_request|compliance_notice|billing_notification|service_alert|regulatory_correspondence|security_notice — promote to reference product]']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific contact person at the port community participant organisation who is the addressee or originator of this communication. Business-confidential as it identifies a named individual within a commercial counterparty.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this communication log record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and regulatory compliance purposes.']
  crm_activity_reference varchar [note: 'The activity or interaction record identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM corresponding to this communication. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM system of record for customer-facing communications.']
  delivery_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason for delivery failure when delivery_status is \'failed\', \'bounced\', or \'undeliverable\'. Captures error codes or descriptions from the transmission channel (e.g., invalid email address, EDI partner not reachable, SMS gateway error). Null when delivery is successful.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Technical delivery outcome of the communication transmission. Distinct from communication_status (which tracks the business lifecycle), delivery_status reflects the channel-level transmission result (e.g., email bounce, EDI acknowledgement failure, SMS undeliverable).']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the communication was sent by the port to a port community participant (outbound) or received by the port from a participant (inbound). Critical for audit trail and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  edi_interchange_reference varchar [note: 'The unique interchange control reference number from the EDI transmission envelope (UNB segment in UN/EDIFACT). Used to correlate EDI messages with their functional acknowledgements (CONTRL) and for EDI error resolution. Null for non-EDI channels.']
  edi_message_type varchar [note: 'UN/EDIFACT message type identifier for EDI-channel communications. Applicable when channel is \'EDI\'. Common types include COPARN (Container Pre-Announcement), BAPLIE (Bayplan/Stowage Plan), IFTMIN (Instruction Message for Transport), COARRI (Container Discharge/Load Report), CODECO (Container Gate-In/Out), CUSCAR (Customs Cargo Report). Null for non-EDI channels.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this communication has been escalated due to non-response, SLA breach, or compliance risk. Supports dispute resolution workflows and management reporting on overdue regulatory correspondence.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice or billing document associated with this communication. Populated for billing notifications, Terminal Handling Charge (THC) advisories, demurrage (DMG) notices, and detention (DET) billing correspondence. Null for non-billing communications.']
  is_acknowledged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient has formally acknowledged receipt of this communication. True when acknowledgement has been received; False when pending. Supports ISPS compliance and SLA reporting.']
  is_regulatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this communication is classified as regulatory correspondence subject to mandatory retention and audit requirements. True for ISPS security notices, Port State Control (PSC) correspondence, customs compliance notices, and MARPOL/SOLAS regulatory communications.']
  is_response_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this communication requires a formal response from the recipient. True for documentation requests, compliance notices, and regulatory correspondence. False for informational advisories and one-way notifications.']
  isps_classification varchar [note: 'ISPS Code security classification level applicable to this communication. Security Level 1 is normal operations; Level 2 is heightened security; Level 3 is exceptional threat. Non-ISPS for communications not subject to ISPS requirements. Mandatory for ISPS audit trail.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) indicating the language in which the communication was composed. Supports multilingual port community operations and regulatory correspondence in required languages.']
  pcs_message_reference varchar [note: 'Unique message identifier assigned by the Port Community System (PCS) for communications transmitted via the PCS channel. Enables traceability back to the originating PCS transaction for customs integration, cargo manifest, and trade documentation workflows.']
  port_code varchar [note: 'United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations (UN/LOCODE) identifying the port facility from which or to which this communication pertains. Supports multi-port operations and regulatory reporting by port authority.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the communication, determining processing urgency and SLA response time targets. Urgent priority is used for ISPS security alerts and safety-critical notifications.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the communication was received by the destination system or party. For inbound communications, this is the principal business event timestamp. Null for outbound communications not yet confirmed received.']
  recipient_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the receiving party for this communication. For EDI, this is the EDI recipient ID (UNB/S003). For email, this is the recipient email address. For PCS messages, this is the PCS participant code. Enables audit trail of communication destination.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique reference number assigned to this communication record, used for tracking and cross-referencing in the Port Community System (PCS) and Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM. Enables parties to cite a specific communication in disputes or audits.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The date by which a response or action is required from the recipient of this communication. Used for compliance notices, documentation requests, and regulatory correspondence to enforce SLA and regulatory deadlines.']
  response_received_date date [note: 'The date on which a response was actually received from the recipient. Compared against response_due_date to determine SLA compliance and identify overdue communications. Null if no response has been received.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this communication record may be purged or archived in accordance with the applicable data retention policy. Calculated based on communication type and regulatory requirements (e.g., ISPS records retained for minimum 5 years, customs records per WCO guidelines).']
  sender_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sending party for this communication. For EDI, this is the EDI sender ID (UNB/S002). For email, this is the sender email address. For PCS messages, this is the PCS participant code. Enables audit trail of communication origin.']
  sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the communication was dispatched or transmitted by the sending party. This is the principal business event timestamp for outbound communications. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this communication log entry. Supports data lineage and reconciliation across Port Community System (PCS), Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM, Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS), NAVIS N4 TOS, TOPS Expert TOS, or manual entry.']
  subject varchar [note: 'The subject line or title of the communication, providing a concise description of the communication\'s topic. Used for search, classification, and audit trail purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this communication log record was last modified. Tracks status updates, delivery confirmations, and acknowledgement events for audit trail purposes.']

  Note: 'Auditable log of all formal communications between the port and port community participants, including vessel call notifications, ETA/ETB/ETD advisories, documentation requests, compliance notices, billing notifications, service alerts, and regulatory correspondence. Captures communication type, channel (EDI, email, PCS message, SMS, letter), direction (inbound/outbound), subject, body reference, timestamp, delivery status, acknowledgement status, and linked business event reference (vessel call ID, invoice number, service request ID). Supports audit trail for regulatory compliance, dispute resolution, and ISPS security communication requirements.'
}

Table customer.credit_assessment {
  credit_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each credit assessment record within the port community participant credit management system.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authorised approver (e.g., Credit Manager, CFO) who formally approved or rejected the credit assessment outcome. Supports segregation of duties and approval authority audit.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: Credit assessments evaluate specific accounts, not just participants. Large shipping lines or freight forwarders maintain multiple accounts with different credit profiles, payment terms, and risk rati']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, or port agent) for whom this credit assessment is conducted.']
  primary_credit_assessor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the port employee or credit analyst who conducted the credit assessment. Used for accountability, audit trail, and workload reporting in Oracle HCM Cloud and SAP S/4HANA.']
  annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'The port community participant\'s most recently reported annual revenue, sourced from submitted financial statements and used as a key input to the credit assessment and limit determination.']
  application_date date [note: 'The date on which the port community participant formally submitted the credit application or the credit assessment was initiated by the commercial team.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the credit assessment was formally approved by the authorised approver, marking the point from which the credit limit becomes effective.']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'The organisational approval authority level at which the credit assessment was approved, reflecting the port\'s delegated authority framework based on credit limit thresholds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_officer|credit_manager|finance_director|cfo|board — promote to reference product]']
  approved_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final credit limit formally approved by the authorised approver, which may differ from the assessed limit following management review or escalation. This is the enforceable credit ceiling for the participant.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorised approver who approved or rejected the credit assessment, captured at the time of decision for audit trail and governance reporting.']
  assessed_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The credit limit amount determined by the assessor following evaluation of the participant\'s financial standing, payment history, and risk profile. This is the operative limit used by the billing domain for credit control.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the credit assessor formally conducted and completed the credit evaluation, distinct from the application submission date.']
  assessment_methodology varchar [note: 'The methodology or combination of methods used to conduct the credit assessment, such as financial statement analysis, external credit bureau reports, trade references, or the port\'s internal scoring model. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial_statement_analysis|credit_bureau_report|trade_reference|internal_scoring_model|combined|external_agency — promote to reference product]']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative notes recorded by the assessor documenting key findings, risk factors, qualitative observations, and rationale for the credit decision. Supports audit trail and future review context.']
  assessment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric reference number assigned to this credit assessment, used in correspondence with the port community participant and referenced in billing and commercial onboarding workflows.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit assessment record, tracking its progression from initial submission through review, approval or rejection, and eventual expiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|expired|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credit assessment indicating whether it is an initial assessment for new account onboarding, a scheduled periodic review, a triggered review due to payment behaviour changes, or a credit limit adjustment request. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial|periodic_review|triggered_review|limit_increase|limit_decrease|onboarding — promote to reference product]']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the credit assessor who conducted the evaluation, captured at the time of assessment for audit and reporting purposes. Complements the assessor_employee_id for human-readable audit trails.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this credit assessment record was first created in the system, used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting purposes.']
  credit_bureau_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or report identifier from an external credit bureau or trade credit agency report obtained as part of the assessment process. Enables traceability back to the source credit report.']
  credit_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of any trade credit insurance arrangement covering the port community participant\'s credit exposure, where the port has obtained insurance to mitigate default risk. Null if no insurance is in place.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal credit rating assigned to the port community participant following assessment, using a standardised rating scale aligned with international credit rating conventions. Drives review frequency and credit terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D — promote to reference product]']
  credit_score decimal(6,2) [note: 'Numeric credit score derived from the assessment methodology, representing the port community participant\'s creditworthiness on the port\'s internal scoring scale. Used to determine credit rating tier and approved limit.']
  crm_opportunity_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM opportunity or account onboarding record that initiated this credit assessment, enabling traceability between commercial pipeline activities and credit decisions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the credit limits and financial assessments are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, AUD). Aligns with the port\'s billing currency configuration in SAP S/4HANA.']
  days_past_due_count varchar [note: 'The number of days the port community participant\'s outstanding invoices were past due at the time of assessment, sourced from SAP S/4HANA accounts receivable. A key input to the credit scoring model.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which the assessed credit limit and terms become binding and active for the port community participant, used by the billing domain for credit control enforcement.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'The date on which the current credit assessment expires and a review must be conducted. Nullable for open-ended credit arrangements. Triggers automated review workflow in the commercial team.']
  financial_statement_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year of the financial statements submitted by the port community participant as supporting documentation for the credit assessment (e.g., 2023). Used to assess recency and relevance of financial data.']
  is_watch_list boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port community participant has been placed on the credit watch list due to deteriorating payment behaviour, adverse financial news, or elevated risk signals, triggering enhanced monitoring.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this credit assessment record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail, change tracking, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic credit review of the port community participant, set based on credit rating tier and risk policy. Used to trigger review workflows in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total outstanding accounts receivable balance owed by the port community participant at the time of the credit assessment, sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI. Used as a key input to limit utilisation and risk evaluation.']
  payment_history_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the port community participant\'s historical payment behaviour based on analysis of invoice settlement patterns in SAP S/4HANA accounts receivable. A primary input to the credit scoring model.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms code assigned to the port community participant based on the credit assessment outcome, defining the number of days allowed for invoice settlement (e.g., NET30, NET60, IMMEDIATE). Feeds directly into NAVIS N4 billing configuration.']
  previous_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The credit limit that was in effect prior to this assessment, enabling tracking of credit limit changes over time and trend analysis of the participant\'s credit standing.']
  previous_credit_rating varchar [note: 'The credit rating assigned in the immediately preceding assessment, enabling tracking of rating migrations and trend analysis. Null for initial assessments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D — promote to reference product]']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Structured reason code or narrative explanation for the rejection of a credit application or reduction of a requested credit limit. Populated only when assessment_status is \'rejected\'. Used for participant communication and regulatory reporting.']
  requested_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The credit limit amount requested by the port community participant in their credit application, expressed in the port\'s operating currency. Used as the baseline for the assessor\'s evaluation.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Broad risk classification assigned to the port community participant based on the credit assessment outcome, used to determine credit control measures, payment terms, and security deposit requirements.']
  sap_credit_account_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA customer credit account number associated with this assessment, enabling direct linkage between the credit assessment record and the operative credit master data in the ERP system.']
  security_deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the security deposit required from the port community participant as a condition of credit approval, denominated in the currency specified by currency_code. Null if no deposit is required.']
  security_deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credit assessment outcome requires the port community participant to lodge a security deposit (cash, bank guarantee, or letter of credit) before credit facilities are activated.']
  security_deposit_type varchar [note: 'The form of security instrument lodged or required from the port community participant as collateral for the credit facility, such as cash deposit, bank guarantee, or letter of credit.']
  supporting_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document management system path for the supporting financial documents submitted with the credit application (e.g., audited financial statements, bank references, trade references). Enables document retrieval for audit.']
  watch_list_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason the port community participant was placed on the credit watch list. Populated only when is_watch_list is True. Used for enhanced monitoring and escalation decisions.']

  Note: 'Credit assessment records for port community participants, capturing credit application details, assessed credit limit, credit rating, assessment methodology, supporting financial documents, assessor identity, assessment date, approval status, and next review date. Tracks credit history and limit changes over time. Used by the billing domain for credit control and by commercial teams for account onboarding decisions.'
}

Table customer.onboarding_application {
  onboarding_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each port community participant onboarding application record. Primary key for this entity.']
  assigned_relationship_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Customer onboarding workflow assigns account managers to new applications. Essential for workload distribution, SLA tracking, and handover management. assigned_relationship_manager is denormalized emp']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Customer onboarding costs (background checks, system setup, ISPS screening, training) are allocated to sales/marketing cost centres for customer acquisition cost analysis and marketing ROI measurement']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Applicant country of incorporation determines regulatory requirements, documentation standards, sanctions screening protocols, AEO eligibility, and customs procedures during port community participant']
  isps_screening_security_personnel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_personnel. Business justification: Security personnel conduct ISPS background screening during participant onboarding. Tracks which security officer performed screening for audit trail and accountability. Role prefix distinguishes from']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the resulting port_community_participant master record created upon successful activation of this onboarding application. Null until the application reaches \'activated\' status.']
  prior_application_onboarding_application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous onboarding application record if this is a resubmission (is_resubmission = true). Enables lineage tracking across multiple application attempts by the same entity.']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Onboarding applications require sanctions screening before participant activation. Port compliance teams need this link to track screening results and prevent sanctioned entities from accessing port f']
  activation_date date [note: 'The date on which the applicant was formally activated as a port community participant, granting access to port services, the Port Community System (PCS), and Terminal Operating System (TOS) integrations. Populated upon successful completion of the onboarding lifecycle.']
  applicant_address varchar [note: 'Full registered business address of the applicant entity, including street, city, state/province, and postal code. Required for legal correspondence, sanctions screening, and regulatory compliance.']
  applicant_legal_name varchar [note: 'The full registered legal name of the applying entity as it appears on official incorporation or registration documents. Used for identity verification, contract formation, and regulatory compliance checks.']
  applicant_trading_name varchar [note: 'The trading or doing-business-as (DBA) name of the applicant entity, if different from the legal name. Used in operational communications and Port Community System (PCS) messaging.']
  application_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the onboarding application was submitted by the applicant. Supports channel analytics and process improvement initiatives.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the onboarding application at submission (e.g., OBA-2024-000123). Used in all correspondence with the applicant and referenced in the Port Community System (PCS).']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the onboarding application within the registration workflow. Tracks progression from initial submission through document verification, security screening, credit assessment, and final activation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|pending_documents|approved|rejected|activated|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the onboarding application received final approval from the authorised port authority officer, marking the transition to the activation phase.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name or username of the port authority officer or manager who approved or last actioned the onboarding application at the current or final workflow stage. Provides audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the background check conducted on the applicant entity, including sanctions list screening, adverse media checks, and anti-money laundering (AML) verification. Distinct from ISPS screening which is port-facility-specific.']
  business_registration_number varchar [note: 'The official government-issued business registration or company number of the applicant entity, as issued by the relevant national authority. Required for identity verification and customs compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the onboarding application record was first created in the platform, which may precede formal submission if the applicant saved a draft.']
  credit_assessment_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the credit assessment team or third-party credit bureau for the pre-assessment conducted on this applicant. Links to the detailed credit assessment report in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  credit_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the credit pre-assessment conducted to evaluate the financial standing of the applicant entity prior to granting access to port services on credit terms. Informs the credit limit and payment terms assigned upon activation.']
  crm_lead_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the corresponding lead or opportunity record in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM from which this onboarding application originated. Enables traceability between the sales pipeline and the registration lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the proposed credit limit and any financial terms associated with this onboarding application (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD).']
  customs_broker_licence_number varchar [note: 'The official customs broker licence number issued by the national customs authority, applicable to applicants of participant type \'customs_broker\'. Required for customs clearance and trade compliance operations at the port.']
  document_checklist_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the submitted documents checklist for this onboarding application, indicating whether all required documents have been received and verified. Drives workflow progression to the security screening stage.']
  freight_forwarder_licence_number varchar [note: 'The official freight forwarder licence or accreditation number issued by the relevant national transport authority, applicable to applicants of participant type \'freight_forwarder\'.']
