  imo_company_number varchar [note: 'The IMO-assigned unique company identification number for shipping line applicants, as issued under the IMO Ship and Company Identification Number Scheme. Applicable only to shipping line participant types; null for other participant types.']
  is_resubmission boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this onboarding application is a resubmission following a prior rejection or withdrawal. True if the applicant has previously submitted an application that was rejected or withdrawn.']
  isps_screening_date date [note: 'Date on which the ISPS security screening was completed for the applicant entity. Used for compliance audit trails and screening validity period tracking.']
  isps_screening_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or case identifier assigned by the ISPS screening authority for the security check conducted on this applicant. Enables cross-referencing with the port security management system.']
  isps_screening_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the ISPS security screening conducted on the applicant entity as part of the port community onboarding process. Mandatory for all participant types under the ISPS Code to ensure port facility security compliance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent modification to the onboarding application record, capturing any status change, document update, or workflow progression event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes recorded by port authority staff during the processing of the onboarding application. May include clarifications, follow-up actions, or observations from document verification and screening stages.']
  participant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the applicant entity by their role in the port community. Determines the applicable onboarding workflow, required documents, and service entitlements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipping_line|freight_forwarder|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent|cargo_owner|consignee — promote to reference product]']
  pcs_entity_code varchar [note: 'The entity code assigned within the Port Community System (PCS) upon activation, used for EDI messaging, cargo manifest submissions, and stakeholder communications. Populated after activation.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person at the applicant organisation. Used for all official onboarding correspondence, document requests, and status notifications via the Port Community System (PCS).']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the applicant organisation responsible for managing the onboarding application and subsequent port community participation.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct telephone number of the primary contact person at the applicant organisation, including international dialling code. Used for urgent onboarding communications and verification callbacks.']
  proposed_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The credit limit (in the port\'s base currency) proposed by the credit assessment team for the applicant entity upon activation. Determines the maximum outstanding balance permitted before pre-payment is required for port services.']
  rejection_category varchar [note: 'Structured categorical classification of the rejection reason, enabling reporting and trend analysis on onboarding failure modes. Populated only when application_status is \'rejected\'.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason(s) for rejection of the onboarding application, recorded by the approving officer. Populated only when application_status is \'rejected\'. Used for applicant feedback and compliance audit trails.']
  sanctions_screening_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the sanctions list screening conducted against international sanctions databases (e.g., OFAC, UN, EU) for the applicant entity. A \'confirmed_match\' outcome results in automatic rejection of the application.']
  sla_target_completion_date date [note: 'The target date by which the onboarding application should be fully processed and the applicant activated, as defined by the port\'s SLA for participant registration. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and KPI reporting.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the applicant formally submitted the onboarding application to the port authority. This is the principal business event timestamp marking the start of the registration lifecycle.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'The tax identification number (TIN) or VAT registration number of the applicant entity, as issued by the relevant tax authority. Required for billing, tariff management, and trade compliance reporting.']
  total_documents_received varchar [note: 'Count of documents that have been physically or electronically received from the applicant against the required document checklist. Compared against total_documents_required to assess completeness.']
  total_documents_required varchar [note: 'Total number of documents required for this onboarding application based on the participant type and applicable regulatory requirements. Used to track document submission completeness.']
  total_documents_verified varchar [note: 'Count of received documents that have been reviewed and verified as authentic and compliant by the port authority\'s document verification team.']
  vehicle_operator_licence_number varchar [note: 'The road transport or vehicle operator licence number issued by the relevant national authority, applicable to applicants of participant type \'trucking_company\'. Required for gate access authorisation and intermodal transport coordination.']
  workflow_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage within the multi-step onboarding approval workflow. Tracks the sequential progression of the application through internal review gates: initial review, document verification, ISPS security screening, credit pre-assessment, final approval, and activation.']

  Note: 'End-to-end onboarding application records for new port community participants seeking to register with the port. Captures applicant entity details, application type (shipping line, freight forwarder, customs broker, trucking company, port agent, cargo owner, consignee), submitted documents checklist, verification status per document, background check status, ISPS security screening outcome, credit pre-assessment reference, approval workflow stage, approver identity, rejection reason (if applicable), and activation date. Manages the complete participant registration lifecycle from initial application through document verification, security screening, credit assessment, and final activation. Links to the resulting port_community_participant record upon successful completion.'
}

Table customer.relationship_manager {
  relationship_manager_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each relationship manager assignment record linking a port community participant to a designated port organisation staff member.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master service agreement or commercial contract governing the relationship between the port and the customer under this assignment. Links to the contract management product for tariff and SLA terms.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, port agent, etc.) assigned to this relationship manager.']
  primary_relationship_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal port organisation employee (account manager, commercial manager, or port agent liaison) assigned to manage the customer relationship. Sourced from Oracle HCM Cloud workforce records.']
  annual_revenue_target decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annual revenue target in the port\'s base currency assigned to this relationship manager for the customer account. Supports commercial performance management and EBITDA contribution tracking.']
  annual_teu_volume_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'The agreed annual container volume target in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) for this customer under the current relationship manager assignment. Used for commercial planning, KPI tracking, and revenue forecasting.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the relationship manager assignment is scheduled to end or was terminated. Null for open-ended active assignments. Used for succession planning and historical reporting.']
  assignment_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for creating or modifying this relationship manager assignment. Supports audit trail, workforce planning, and CRM change management reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_customer|reassignment|staff_departure|restructure|volume_growth|escalation|rotation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assignment_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business reference code for this assignment record, used in CRM workflow routing, correspondence, and escalation management. Format: RM-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the relationship manager assignment became effective. Used for tenure tracking, SLA accountability, and CRM reporting.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the relationship manager assignment. Active indicates the assignment is current and operational; pending indicates awaiting formal activation; suspended indicates temporarily paused; terminated indicates the assignment has ended.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the relationship manager assignment. Permanent indicates a long-term designated assignment; interim indicates a temporary cover arrangement; acting indicates a stand-in during absence; backup indicates a secondary escalation contact; co_managed indicates shared responsibility.']
  churn_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been flagged as at risk of reducing or ceasing port service usage. Set by the relationship manager or CRM system based on engagement signals, volume trends, or competitor activity. Triggers escalation workflows.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Defines whether the relationship manager assignment covers all port services or is restricted to specific service lines such as container terminal operations, vessel operations, cargo handling, intermodal logistics, marine services, or trade compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all_services|container_terminal|vessel_operations|cargo_handling|intermodal|marine_services|trade_compliance — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this relationship manager assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.']
  crm_owner_team varchar [note: 'The name of the CRM team or business unit within the port organisation responsible for this relationship assignment. Used for CRM workflow routing, queue management, and team-level performance reporting.']
  crm_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM for this relationship assignment. Used for system integration, data lineage, and bi-directional synchronisation between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM source system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the revenue target and commercial targets for this assignment (e.g., USD, EUR, AUD, SGD).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The commercial segment classification of the port community participant for this assignment. Used for portfolio management, revenue reporting, and targeted service delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1_shipping_line|tier_2_shipping_line|freight_forwarder|cargo_owner|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent|nvocc|government_entity — promote to reference product]']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has provided consent for their relationship data to be shared with third-party port community system participants or regulatory bodies. Supports privacy compliance and data governance obligations.']
  escalation_tier varchar [note: 'Numeric tier indicating the escalation level of this assignment within the CRM workflow routing hierarchy. Tier 1 is the primary point of contact; higher tiers represent escalation paths to senior management. Used for automated CRM escalation routing.']
  handover_date date [note: 'The date on which the relationship manager assignment was formally handed over from the previous staff member to the current assignee. Null if this is an original assignment with no prior holder.']
  handover_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting key customer context, open issues, relationship history, and action items transferred during the assignment handover. Classified as confidential due to commercially sensitive customer relationship intelligence.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary relationship manager assignment for the customer. A customer may have multiple assigned managers across different service lines, but only one should be flagged as primary for default CRM routing and escalation.']
  isps_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned relationship manager requires International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code clearance to access restricted port areas when meeting with this customer. Supports security compliance and access management.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent formal account review meeting conducted by the relationship manager with the customer. Used to track engagement cadence and identify overdue reviews.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The date of the next scheduled formal account review meeting between the relationship manager and the customer. Drives CRM calendar scheduling and proactive engagement workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the relationship manager to record contextual notes about the customer relationship, special instructions, or account-specific considerations not captured in structured fields. Classified as confidential due to commercially sensitive content.']
  pcs_participant_code varchar [note: 'The participant code assigned to the customer within the Port Community System (PCS). Used to correlate the relationship manager assignment with PCS messaging, cargo manifests, and trade documentation workflows.']
  port_code varchar [note: 'The UN/LOCODE identifying the specific port or terminal at which this relationship manager assignment is applicable. Supports multi-port organisations where a customer may have different assigned managers at different port locations.']
  preferred_contact_channel varchar [note: 'The customer\'s preferred communication channel for engagement with their assigned relationship manager. Supports CRM workflow configuration and communication preference management. EDI and PCS messaging are standard channels in maritime logistics.']
  preferred_contact_language varchar [note: 'The ISO 639-1 language code representing the customer\'s preferred language for communication with their relationship manager (e.g., en, zh-CN, ar, fr). Supports multilingual port community engagement.']
  relationship_health_score varchar [note: 'A manually assessed or CRM-system-generated integer score (typically 1-100) reflecting the overall health and strength of the customer relationship at the time of last review. Used for portfolio risk management and proactive engagement prioritisation.']
  response_time_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractually or operationally agreed maximum response time in hours for the assigned relationship manager to respond to customer enquiries or service requests under this assignment. Supports SLA compliance monitoring.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'The agreed frequency at which the relationship manager conducts formal account review meetings with the customer. Supports scheduling of Key Performance Indicator (KPI) reviews and service performance discussions.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assigned staff member\'s role in managing the customer relationship. Defines the nature of engagement and authority level within the port organisation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: key_account_manager|commercial_manager|port_agent_liaison|customer_success_manager|trade_development_manager|operations_liaison — promote to reference product]']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'The SLA tier applicable to this customer relationship assignment, determining response time commitments, service priority, and escalation thresholds. Platinum and gold tiers typically apply to major shipping lines and key accounts.']
  strategic_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is classified as a strategic account requiring executive-level engagement and enhanced service delivery. Strategic accounts typically represent major shipping lines or high-volume cargo owners critical to port revenue.']
  terminal_code varchar [note: 'The specific terminal identifier within the port where this relationship manager assignment applies. Relevant for multi-terminal ports where customer relationships may be scoped to a specific container terminal, RoRo terminal, or bulk terminal.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for terminating this relationship manager assignment. Populated when assignment_status transitions to terminated. Supports workforce analytics and customer retention reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this relationship manager assignment record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data loading in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Assignment records linking port community participants to their designated account managers, relationship managers, and port agents within the port organisation. Captures assigned staff member, role type (key account manager, commercial manager, port agent liaison), assignment start date, end date, primary flag, and coverage scope (all services or specific service lines). Supports CRM workflow routing and escalation management.'
}

Table customer.participant_document {
  participant_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each participant document record in the port community system. Primary key for the participant_document data product.']
  accreditation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the linked accreditation record that this document supports or satisfies as a compliance requirement. Enables compliance gating by associating documents to specific accreditation milestones.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Agreements require specific participant documents (insurance certificates, performance bonds, P&I club certificates, dangerous goods licenses, customs broker licenses). Port operators must track which']
  commodity_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.commodity_code. Business justification: Import/export licenses, phytosanitary certificates, and customs permits reference specific HS codes; regulatory gate checks, cargo release authorization, and compliance verification require this link ']
  compliance_hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.hs_code. Business justification: Commodity-specific permits (phytosanitary, veterinary, CITES) must reference the HS code they authorize. Port security validates that participant documents cover the specific commodity being imported/']
  imdg_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.imdg_class. Business justification: Dangerous goods certificates and training records specify which IMDG classes are covered; cargo acceptance gate checks, ISPS compliance verification, and dangerous goods handling authorization depend ']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, customs broker, cargo owner, trucking company, port agent, etc.) who submitted or to whom this document was issued.']
  primary_superseded_by_document_participant_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer participant_document record that replaces this document upon renewal or reissuance. Enables document version chain tracking and ensures compliance systems reference the current valid document.']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Security incidents trigger document submission requirements (insurance updates after breach, revised security plans, corrective action evidence). Links document compliance tracking to incident remedia']
  trade_restriction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_restriction. Business justification: Import/export permits must reference the specific trade restriction they satisfy (sanctions, embargoes, license requirements). Port compliance officers validate permit-restriction alignment before car']
  verified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Document verification workflow tracks which port staff member verified compliance documents. Essential for ISPS/customs audits and quality assurance. verified_by is bare string.']
  certified_copy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document on file is a certified true copy (True) or an original (False). Certified copies may have different evidentiary weight for regulatory compliance purposes.']
  compliance_gate_blocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document is currently blocking the participant from accessing port services or completing accreditation (True = blocked). Set to True when a mandatory document is expired, rejected, or pending verification. Drives real-time compliance gating in the Port Community System.']
  coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of insurance coverage or financial guarantee stated in the document (e.g., P&I cover limit, cargo insurance sum insured). Applicable primarily to insurance certificates. Used for risk assessment and minimum coverage compliance checks.']
  coverage_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the coverage amount stated in the document (e.g., USD, EUR, AUD). Required to normalise coverage values across multi-currency insurance instruments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this participant document record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the document lifecycle.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The official externally-issued or internally-assigned document number as printed on the physical or digital document (e.g., licence number, certificate serial, permit reference). This is the business identifier used for cross-referencing with issuing authorities and regulatory bodies.']
  document_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates the operational scope to which the document applies — whether it covers the entire participant entity, a specific vessel, a cargo consignment, a port facility, or an individual person (e.g., a named customs broker). Determines how the document is applied during compliance gating.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'The full descriptive title of the document as stated on the official instrument (e.g., \'Protection and Indemnity Insurance Certificate\', \'Dangerous Goods Handling Authorisation\'). Provides human-readable identification beyond the document type code.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the official document submitted by or issued to the port community participant. Drives compliance gating rules and determines which regulatory framework applies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trade_licence|insurance_certificate|customs_broker_licence|dangerous_goods_certificate|vessel_operator_certificate|port_access_permit|isps_certificate|marpol_certificate|solas_certificate|p_and_i_cover_letter|customs_clearance_authority|imdg_handling_certificate — promote to reference product]']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the document as issued by the authority (e.g., \'v2\', \'Amendment 3\', \'2024 Edition\'). Tracks amendments and reissues of the same document number without full replacement.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the document ceases to be valid. Null indicates the document has no expiry (e.g., certain trade registrations). Used for compliance gating — expired documents block port access or service provision until renewed.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'The digital file format of the stored document copy (e.g., PDF, JPEG, XML for EDI-transmitted certificates). Determines rendering and processing requirements for document management systems.']
  file_reference varchar [note: 'The storage path, URI, or document management system reference pointing to the digital copy of the document (e.g., cloud object storage path, DMS document ID). Enables retrieval of the original scanned or electronic document for audit and compliance review.']
  imo_number varchar [note: 'The IMO vessel identification number (7-digit) associated with the document when the document scope is vessel-level (e.g., vessel operator certificate, vessel-specific ISPS certificate). Provides unambiguous vessel linkage for maritime regulatory compliance.']
  insurer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance underwriter or P&I Club that issued the insurance certificate (e.g., \'UK P&I Club\', \'Gard P&I\', \'Skuld\'). Applicable to insurance-type documents. Used for risk management and claims processing.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document is a mandatory regulatory or port authority requirement (True) for the participant\'s accreditation or service access, or an optional supplementary document (False). Mandatory documents block service provision if not verified.']
  isps_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document is relevant to ISPS Code compliance requirements (True), such as port facility security certificates, vessel security plans, or security officer designations. Enables targeted ISPS compliance reporting.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the document was officially issued or certified by the issuing authority. Used to determine document age, validity windows, and renewal scheduling.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'The name of the regulatory body, government agency, classification society, or accredited organisation that issued or certified the document (e.g., \'International Maritime Organization\', \'National Maritime Safety Authority\', \'Lloyd\'s Register\', \'Australian Border Force\'). Critical for verifying document authenticity and regulatory standing.']
  issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the jurisdiction in which the document was issued (e.g., AUS, SGP, GBR). Used for cross-border trade compliance and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or 639-2 language code of the document\'s primary language (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'zh\'). Used to determine whether a certified translation is required for port authority review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to record additional observations, conditions, endorsements, or special instructions associated with the document (e.g., \'Conditional approval pending physical inspection\', \'Covers vessels under IMO 1234567 only\').']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'The insurance policy or P&I cover entry number associated with the insurance certificate document. Enables cross-referencing with insurer records for claims and coverage verification.']
  psc_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Port State Control authorities have been notified of a document deficiency or non-compliance finding related to this document (True = notified). Supports PSC inspection management and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the document was rejected during verification (e.g., \'Document expired prior to submission\', \'Issuing authority not recognised\', \'Signature mismatch\'). Populated only when verification_status is \'rejected\'. Guides participant re-submission.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the expiry date at which the port authority should trigger a renewal reminder notification to the participant. Configurable per document type to align with regulatory lead times (e.g., 90 days for P&I certificates, 30 days for port access permits).']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document requires periodic renewal (True) or is a one-time permanent issuance (False). Drives automated renewal reminder workflows and compliance calendar management.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the document was submitted to the port authority (e.g., Port Community System portal, EDI message, email, manual counter submission, API integration). Supports process analytics and EDI compliance tracking.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the participant submitted the document to the port authority for review and verification. Used to measure processing turnaround times and SLA compliance for document verification.']
  translation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a certified translation of this document is required for port authority compliance review (True). Triggered when the document language is not an accepted official language of the port jurisdiction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this participant document record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any attribute, supporting audit readiness and data lineage in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which the document was last verified or reviewed by port authority compliance staff. Supports audit trails and tracks when the verification status was last confirmed.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document verification process. Controls compliance gating: only \'verified\' documents unlock accreditation milestones and port access. \'Rejected\' triggers re-submission workflow. \'Expired\' triggers renewal notification.']

  Note: 'Repository of official documents submitted by or issued to port community participants, including trade licences, insurance certificates (P&I cover), customs broker licences, dangerous goods handling certificates, vessel operator certificates, and port access permits. Captures document type, document number, issuing authority, issue date, expiry date, file reference, verification status, and linked accreditation record. Supports compliance gating and audit readiness.'
}

Table customer.edi_subscription {
  edi_subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each EDI subscription configuration record within the Port Community System. Primary key for this entity.']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'The EDI interchange sender identifier (ISA06 in ANSI X12, UNB sender in UN/EDIFACT, or AS2-From header) that uniquely identifies the sending party in the EDI message envelope. Used for message routing and validation.']
  pcs_channel_id varchar [note: 'The internal channel or queue identifier assigned by the Port Community System to route messages for this subscription. Used for operational monitoring and message tracing within the PCS.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, or port agent) who owns this EDI subscription.']
  receiver_edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'The EDI interchange receiver identifier (ISA08 in ANSI X12, UNB receiver in UN/EDIFACT, or AS2-To header) that uniquely identifies the receiving party in the EDI message envelope. Used for message routing and delivery confirmation.']
  service_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service agreement or contract under which this EDI subscription is provisioned, linking the technical configuration to the commercial terms and SLA obligations.']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: EDI message subscriptions are often vessel-type-specific (BAPLIE for container vessels, IFTDGN for dangerous goods); message routing, validation rules, and subscription eligibility depend on vessel ty']
  acknowledgement_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a functional acknowledgement (e.g., CONTRL in UN/EDIFACT, 997/999 in ANSI X12, MDN in AS2) is required from the receiving party upon successful receipt and processing of each EDI message.']
  acknowledgement_timeout_minutes varchar [note: 'The maximum number of minutes the Port Community System will wait for a functional acknowledgement before flagging the transmission as unacknowledged and triggering an alert or retry. Applicable when acknowledgement_required is true.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiry date of the digital certificate associated with this EDI subscription. Used to proactively manage certificate renewals and prevent service disruptions due to expired certificates.']
  certificate_thumbprint varchar [note: 'The SHA-1 or SHA-256 thumbprint of the digital certificate used for AS2 message signing or SFTP public key authentication. Used to validate the identity of the communicating party and ensure message integrity.']
  character_set varchar [note: 'The character set encoding used for EDI message content under this subscription. UTF-8 is the standard for modern integrations; ISO-8859-1 is common in legacy UN/EDIFACT implementations.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'The transport/communication protocol used to exchange EDI messages between the port community participant and the Port Community System. AS2 (Applicability Statement 2) and SFTP are the most common in maritime logistics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AS2|SFTP|FTP|FTPS|API_REST|SMTP — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this EDI subscription configuration record was first created in the system, capturing the audit trail for the subscription\'s inception.']
  crm_subscription_ref varchar [note: 'The corresponding subscription or service agreement reference number in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM, enabling cross-system traceability between the EDI configuration and the commercial relationship record.']
  customs_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EDI messages under this subscription are automatically forwarded to the Customs authority integration layer within the Port Community System. Applicable for CUSCAR (Customs Cargo Report) and related trade compliance message types.']
  edi_standard varchar [note: 'The EDI data interchange standard used for this subscription. UN/EDIFACT is the dominant standard in maritime logistics per IMO and WCO guidelines. XML and JSON are used for modern API-based integrations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UN/EDIFACT|XML|JSON|ANSI X12|TRADACOMS — promote to reference product]']
  edi_version varchar [note: 'The specific version of the EDI standard in use for this subscription (e.g., D96A, D00B for UN/EDIFACT; 4010, 5010 for ANSI X12). Ensures correct message parsing and validation at the Port Community System gateway.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this EDI subscription configuration becomes active and the Port Community System begins routing messages according to this configuration.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this EDI subscription configuration expires or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null indicates an open-ended subscription with no planned termination date.']
  endpoint_address varchar [note: 'The network endpoint address (URL, hostname, IP address, or AS2 station identifier) to which EDI messages are sent or from which they are received. Classified as confidential as it reveals integration infrastructure details.']
  endpoint_port varchar [note: 'The TCP/IP port number used for the EDI communication endpoint (e.g., 22 for SFTP, 443 for HTTPS/AS2). Required for firewall configuration and connectivity management.']
  imo_company_number varchar [note: 'The IMO-assigned unique company identification number for the port community participant, used in EDI message headers for shipping lines and ship operators to ensure regulatory traceability per IMO Resolution A.1078(28).']
  ip_whitelist varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of approved source IP addresses or CIDR ranges permitted to connect to the EDI endpoint for this subscription. Enforced at the Port Community System firewall for ISPS Code and ISO 28000 supply chain security compliance.']
  is_encrypted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EDI messages transmitted under this subscription are encrypted in transit (e.g., using TLS/SSL for SFTP or S/MIME for AS2). Required for ISPS Code compliance and ISO 28000 supply chain security.']
  is_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EDI messages transmitted under this subscription are digitally signed to ensure non-repudiation and message integrity. Relevant for customs cargo reports (CUSCAR) and trade compliance under WCO standards.']
  last_error_code varchar [note: 'The error code returned by the most recent failed EDI transmission or processing attempt under this subscription. Supports operational troubleshooting and incident management.']
  last_error_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent EDI transmission or processing error for this subscription. Used alongside last_error_code for incident tracking and SLA breach analysis.']
  last_message_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recently transmitted or received EDI message under this subscription. Used for monitoring subscription activity, detecting stale connections, and SLA reporting.']
  max_message_size_kb varchar [note: 'The maximum allowable size in kilobytes for a single EDI message transmitted under this subscription. Enforced at the Port Community System gateway to prevent oversized transmissions that could impact system performance.']
  message_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the port community participant is configured to SEND (OUTBOUND) or RECEIVE (INBOUND) this EDI message type via the Port Community System.']
  message_frequency varchar [note: 'The expected frequency or trigger mode for EDI message transmission under this subscription. Real-time = event-driven immediate dispatch; Scheduled = at defined intervals; On-demand = manually triggered; Batch daily/weekly = aggregated periodic transmissions.']
  message_type varchar [note: 'The EDI message type this subscription covers. COPARN = Container Pre-Announcement, IFTMIN = Instruction Message for Transport, BAPLIE = Bayplan/Stowage Plan, CUSCAR = Customs Cargo Report, INVOIC = Invoice, COARRI = Container Arrival/Departure, CODECO = Container Gate-In/Gate-Out, IFTSTA = Transport Status. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: COPARN|IFTMIN|BAPLIE|CUSCAR|INVOIC|COARRI|CODECO|IFTSTA — promote to reference product]']
  notification_email varchar [note: 'The email address to which operational alerts, error notifications, and acknowledgement failures for this EDI subscription are sent. Typically a technical contact or integration team mailbox at the participant organisation.']
  notification_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number (in E.164 international format) for SMS or voice alerts related to critical EDI failures or connectivity issues for this subscription.']
  participant_role varchar [note: 'The operational role of the port community participant in the context of this EDI subscription, determining which message types are applicable and the direction of data flow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipping_line|freight_forwarder|customs_broker|cargo_owner|trucking_company|port_agent|terminal_operator — promote to reference product]']
  retry_attempts_max varchar [note: 'The maximum number of automatic retransmission attempts the Port Community System will make if an EDI message delivery fails or times out. Governs resilience of the EDI channel.']
  retry_interval_minutes varchar [note: 'The number of minutes between successive automatic retransmission attempts when an EDI message delivery fails. Works in conjunction with retry_attempts_max to define the retry schedule.']
  schedule_cron_expression varchar [note: 'The CRON expression defining the scheduled transmission window for batch or scheduled EDI messages (e.g., \'0 6 * * *\' for daily at 06:00 UTC). Applicable when message_frequency is \'scheduled\', \'batch_daily\', or \'batch_weekly\'.']
  sla_response_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The maximum number of minutes within which the Port Community System must process and deliver an inbound EDI message or confirm receipt of an outbound message, as defined in the participant\'s service agreement.']
  subscription_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the EDI subscription, used in operational communications and referenced in the Port Community System configuration. Follows the format EDI-{alphanumeric}.']
  subscription_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the EDI subscription configuration, typically identifying the participant and message type combination (e.g., \'Maersk COPARN Inbound AS2\').']
  subscription_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or remarks about this EDI subscription, capturing special configuration requirements, known issues, or onboarding instructions for the integration team.']
  subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the EDI subscription. Active = operational and processing messages; Inactive = configured but not processing; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., due to connectivity issues or compliance hold); Pending = awaiting activation/testing; Terminated = permanently decommissioned.']
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the EDI subscription tier. Standard = regular production subscription; Premium = high-volume or priority-routed subscription with enhanced SLA; Test = non-production testing environment; Pilot = limited trial configuration for new message types or participants.']
  test_mode boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription is operating in test/sandbox mode, where messages are processed but do not trigger live operational actions in the Terminal Operating System or Customs systems. Used during onboarding and integration testing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this EDI subscription configuration record, supporting audit trail requirements and change management.']

  Note: 'EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) subscription configuration for port community participants, defining which EDI message types they are subscribed to send or receive via the Port Community System. Covers COPARN (container pre-announcement), IFTMIN (transport instruction), BAPLIE (stowage plan), CUSCAR (customs cargo report), and INVOIC (invoice) message types. Captures message type, direction, EDI standard (UN/EDIFACT, XML), communication protocol (AS2, SFTP), endpoint address, and active status.'
}

Table customer.port_access_permit {
  port_access_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each port access permit record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  authorized_access_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_point. Business justification: Permits specify which access points holder may use for entry/exit. Essential for access control system integration and permit validation at gates. Role prefix clarifies this is authorized entry point,']
  contractor_safety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.contractor_safety. Business justification: Access permits for contractor personnel must reference contractor safety qualification status. Permit issuance and renewal depend on current safety clearance, incident history, and training compliance']
  isps_facility_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.isps_facility_record. Business justification: Port access permits must validate against ISPS facility security requirements and current security level. Port security officers require this link to enforce zone access restrictions based on facility']
  issuing_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: ISPS security compliance requires tracking which port security officer issued each access permit. Critical for audit trails, security incident investigations, and regulatory inspections. issuing_offic']
  issuing_security_personnel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_personnel. Business justification: Port access permits are issued by security personnel under ISPS Code requirements. Tracks which security officer issued/approved each permit for audit trail and accountability. Role prefix distinguish']
  pcs_permit_id varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this permit record in the Port Community System (PCS). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and integration between the PCS and the lakehouse silver layer.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant entity (shipping line, freight forwarder, trucking company, port agent, etc.) under whose sponsorship this permit was issued. Links to the customer master.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Access permits grant entry to specific terminals, berths, or ISPS security zones; physical access control enforcement, gate validation, and security zone management require precise location-level auth']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Vendors providing on-site services (equipment maintenance, construction, stevedoring) require ISPS-compliant port access permits for personnel and vehicles entering restricted zones. Real business pro']
  access_time_restriction varchar [note: 'Time-of-day restriction applied to this permit. Unrestricted permits allow 24/7 access; other values limit access to defined operational windows. Enforced at gate entry systems and ISPS checkpoints.']
  application_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the original permit application submitted through the Port Community System (PCS) or port authority portal. Links the issued permit back to the application workflow for audit and traceability.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (with timezone offset) at which the permit application was formally approved by the issuing authority. Distinct from issue_date which records the calendar date of issuance. Supports ISPS audit trail with precise approval timing.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the security background check was completed for this permit holder. Used to determine whether a re-check is required at renewal based on the port\'s ISPS security policy interval.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the security background check conducted on the permit holder prior to issuance. Passed status is a prerequisite for active permit issuance under ISPS requirements. Waived may apply to certain visitor categories under escort.']
  badge_number varchar [note: 'Physical badge or access card number printed on the permit holder\'s port ID badge. Used by gate access control systems, RFID readers, and security checkpoints to validate physical access. May differ from permit_number.']
  biometric_reference_code varchar [note: 'Unique reference token linking this permit to the holder\'s biometric record stored in the port\'s secure biometric identity management system (fingerprint, iris, or facial recognition template). The actual biometric template is NOT stored here — only the reference token. Null for vehicle permits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (with timezone offset) at which this permit record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and lineage tracking in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  crm_contact_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding contact record in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM for the permit holder or their sponsoring organisation. Enables cross-system traceability between the access permit and the CRM customer engagement record.']
  escort_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit holder must be accompanied by an authorised port escort at all times while within restricted ISPS zones. True for visitor and certain contractor permit types; False for credentialed personnel with full unescorted access.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the port access permit ceases to be valid. After this date the permit_status transitions to expired and physical access is denied. Renewal must be initiated before this date to maintain uninterrupted access.']
  holder_date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the individual permit holder in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for identity verification, background screening, and ISPS compliance records. Null for vehicle permits.']
  holder_employer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organisation that employs or sponsors the permit holder (e.g., shipping line, stevedoring company, customs broker, trucking company). Used to link the individual to their sponsoring port community participant for accountability and audit.']
  holder_full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual permit holder (person) as it appears on their government-issued identity document. Populated for personnel, contractor, visitor, crew, and emergency permit types. Null for vehicle-only permits.']
  holder_id_document_number varchar [note: 'The unique number of the government-issued identity document (passport number, national ID number, seafarer book number, etc.) used to verify the permit holder\'s identity during the application process.']
  holder_id_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of government-issued identity document presented by the permit holder at the time of application and verification. Used for ISPS compliance audit and background check records.']
  holder_nationality varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the nationality of the individual permit holder. Required for ISPS background screening and customs/immigration compliance reporting.']
  isps_security_level varchar [note: 'International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code security level at which this permit was issued. Level 1 is normal operations; Level 2 is heightened threat; Level 3 is exceptional threat. Permits issued at higher security levels may carry additional access restrictions.']
  isps_zone_access varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ISPS-designated restricted port zones to which this permit grants access (e.g., berth_side|container_yard|gate_complex|bonded_warehouse|control_room). Defined per the port\'s ISPS Port Facility Security Plan (PFSP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: berth_side|container_yard|gate_complex|bonded_warehouse|control_room|vessel_deck|hazmat_area — promote to reference product for zone management]']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the port access permit was formally issued and became effective. Marks the start of the permit\'s validity period.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the port authority department or designated security officer responsible for issuing this permit (e.g., Port Security Office, ISPS Compliance Unit). Required for audit trail and regulatory accountability.']
  max_entry_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of gate entries permitted under this access permit within its validity period. Null indicates unlimited entries (applicable to permanent and multi-entry permits). Enforced by the gate access control system.']
  permit_category varchar [note: 'Operational category of the permit defining its usage scope. Permanent permits are issued for long-term employees; temporary permits cover short-term contractors or visitors; single-entry permits are for one-time access events; multi-entry permits allow repeated access within the validity period.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique permit reference number assigned by the port authority at issuance. Used on physical badges, gate scanners, and ISPS audit trails. Format: PAP-<PORT_CODE>-<SEQUENCE>.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the port access permit. Active permits grant physical access; suspended permits are temporarily blocked pending investigation; revoked permits are permanently cancelled; expired permits have passed their expiry date without renewal.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the access permit indicating whether it covers a person (personnel, contractor, visitor, crew, emergency responder) or a vehicle. Drives the applicable ISPS security zone rules and badge colour coding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: personnel|vehicle|contractor|visitor|crew|emergency — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']
  previous_permit_number varchar [note: 'Permit number of the immediately preceding permit that this record renews or replaces. Enables lineage tracing across permit renewal cycles for ISPS audit and compliance history.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or special conditions associated with this permit (e.g., restricted to specific berth, requires safety induction completion, hazardous cargo handling certification required). Captured by the issuing security officer.']
  renewal_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this permit has been renewed since its original issuance. Tracks the renewal history for compliance reporting and to identify long-standing permit holders who may require periodic re-vetting under ISPS policy.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the permit was formally revoked prior to its natural expiry. Populated only when permit_status is revoked. Null for active, suspended, or expired permits.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the permit was revoked. Required when permit_status is revoked to support ISPS audit trails and incident reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: security_breach|employment_terminated|document_expired|disciplinary_action|voluntary_surrender|lost_stolen|other — promote to reference product if additional reasons are required]']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) chip embedded in the permit holder\'s access badge or vehicle transponder. Enables automated gate entry/exit tracking and real-time zone monitoring within the port.']
  sponsoring_vessel_imo_number varchar [note: 'IMO vessel identification number of the vessel whose call is the basis for issuing this permit (e.g., for crew shore passes or vessel-specific contractor access). Null for permits not tied to a specific vessel call.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Date on which the permit was placed into suspended status pending investigation or administrative review. Populated only when permit_status is suspended. Null for all other statuses.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for suspending the permit. Populated when permit_status is suspended. Supports security incident investigation and ISPS compliance audit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (with timezone offset) at which this permit record was last modified. Supports change detection, incremental loading in the lakehouse, and audit trail requirements.']
  vehicle_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official government-issued registration plate number of the vehicle for vehicle-type permits. Null for personnel permits. Used at gate entry/exit for automated plate recognition and ISPS vehicle access control.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle covered by a vehicle-type permit. Determines applicable port zone access rules, weight restrictions, and escort requirements. Null for personnel permits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: truck|trailer|forklift|crane|passenger_vehicle|motorcycle|heavy_equipment — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']

  Note: 'Port access permits issued to port community participant personnel and vehicles, granting authorised access to restricted port zones in compliance with ISPS Code requirements. Captures permit holder (person or vehicle), permit type (personnel, vehicle, contractor), ISPS security zone access level, issuing authority, issue date, expiry date, biometric reference, and revocation status. Distinct from accreditation (which is entity-level) — this is individual-level access control.'
}

Table customer.participant_service_agreement {
  participant_service_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each participant-service agreement record. Primary key.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the port community participant who has negotiated this service agreement']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific port service covered by this agreement']
  agreement_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the commercial agreement or contract document that governs this participant-service pricing. Links to contract management system or document repository.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this participant-service agreement. ACTIVE agreements are used in billing calculations. SUSPENDED agreements are temporarily inactive but not terminated.']
  billing_frequency_override varchar [note: 'Service-specific billing frequency that overrides standard billing cycles (e.g., per call, weekly, monthly, quarterly). Used when participants negotiate consolidated billing for specific services.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this participant-service agreement record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSZ']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the negotiated rate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Must align with participant\'s billing currency or be explicitly agreed in contract.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the standard or negotiated rate for this participant-service combination. Range 0.00 to 100.00. Applied before minimum charge validation.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Start date from which this participant-service agreement is valid and should be applied in billing. Part of temporal validity for tariff revision cycles.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'End date until which this participant-service agreement is valid. Null indicates open-ended agreement. Used for contract expiry management and tariff transitions.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this agreement record. Used for audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this participant-service agreement record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSZ. Used for change tracking and audit.']
  minimum_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'Participant-specific minimum charge for this service, overriding the standard minimum charge defined in service_code master. Null indicates standard minimum applies.']
  negotiated_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Participant-specific negotiated rate for this service, overriding the standard tariff rate. Used by billing systems to calculate charges. Null indicates standard rate applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special conditions, exceptions, or commercial terms specific to this participant-service agreement. Used by commercial and billing teams for context.']
  payment_terms_override varchar [note: 'Service-specific payment terms that override the participant\'s default payment terms for this particular service. Used when certain high-value services (e.g., pilotage, towage) require prepayment or shorter payment cycles.']
  volume_tier varchar [note: 'Volume tier classification for this participant-service agreement (e.g., Tier 1: 0-1000 TEU, Tier 2: 1001-5000 TEU, Tier 3: 5000+ TEU). Determines eligibility for volume-based discounts.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the commercial team member who created this agreement record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the commercial agreement between a port community participant and a specific port service. It captures negotiated pricing, discount structures, volume tiers, and payment terms that exist only in the context of this participant-service relationship. Each record links one port community participant to one service code with commercial terms that override standard tariff rates. This is the operational SSOT for participant-specific service pricing used by NAVIS N4 billing module and SAP FI/CO for invoice generation.. Existence Justification: In maritime port operations, participants (shipping lines, freight forwarders, cargo owners) subscribe to multiple port services (pilotage, towage, stevedoring, wharfage, storage), and each service is provided to multiple participants. The business actively manages participant-service agreements with negotiated rates, volume discounts, effective periods, and payment terms that vary by participant-service combination. This is a recognized commercial concept called Service Agreement or Tariff Agreement.'
}

Table customer.location_access_authorization {
  location_access_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each location access authorization record. Primary key for the association.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the port security officer or system administrator who approved this access authorization. Used for audit trail and accountability. References the identity management system user ID.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the port community participant being granted access authorization']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the port location for which access is authorized']
  access_count varchar [note: 'Total number of successful access events by this participant to this location since authorization was granted. Incremented by gate control systems. Used for usage analytics and billing (if applicable). Reset to zero if authorization is revoked and re-issued.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the access permission level granted to the participant for this specific location. Determines operational privileges and security restrictions. Values: FULL_ACCESS (unrestricted operational access), RESTRICTED_ACCESS (limited to specific times/activities), ESCORT_REQUIRED (must be accompanied by port security), NO_ACCESS (authorization revoked but record retained for audit).']
  authorization_approval_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this access authorization was officially approved and became enforceable. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting. Must be on or before authorization_start_date.']
  authorization_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the participants authorization to access this port location expires. Null indicates indefinite authorization subject to ongoing ISPS compliance. Gate systems automatically deny access after this date. Must be on or before the participants ISPS accreditation expiry date.']
  authorization_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the participant is authorized to access this port location. Used by gate control systems to validate time-bound access permissions. Must be on or after the participants ISPS accreditation date.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this access authorization record. ACTIVE = currently valid and enforceable, SUSPENDED = temporarily disabled pending investigation or compliance review, EXPIRED = authorization_end_date has passed, REVOKED = permanently cancelled due to security breach or contract termination, PENDING_APPROVAL = awaiting port security approval. Gate systems only honor ACTIVE authorizations.']
  equipment_access_permitted boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the participant is authorized to operate cargo handling equipment (cranes, reach stackers, RTGs) at this port location. Requires additional operator certification validation. Used by equipment management systems to enforce operational safety and authorization.']
  isps_clearance_level varchar [note: 'ISPS security clearance level required and granted for this participant-location combination. LEVEL_1 = normal operations, LEVEL_2 = heightened security threat, LEVEL_3 = exceptional security threat. Determines access permissions during different MARSEC (Maritime Security) levels. Participant must hold clearance at or above the locations current MARSEC level to gain access.']
  last_access_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful access event by this participant to this location. Updated by gate control systems upon each entry. Used for access pattern analysis and dormant authorization identification. Null if participant has never accessed the location since authorization was granted.']
  operational_hours_restriction varchar [note: 'Time-of-day or day-of-week restrictions on when the participant may access this location. Format: JSON string or structured text defining allowed time windows (e.g., \'MON-FRI 06:00-18:00\', \'24/7\', \'DAYLIGHT_ONLY\'). Null indicates no time restrictions. Used by gate control systems to enforce temporal access policies.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this authorization was revoked or suspended. Null if authorization_status is ACTIVE, EXPIRED, or PENDING_APPROVAL. Required for REVOKED and SUSPENDED statuses. Used for security incident investigation and compliance audits.']
  vehicle_access_permitted boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the participant is authorized to bring vehicles (trucks, forklifts, terminal tractors) into this port location. False indicates pedestrian-only access. Used by gate lane systems to validate vehicle entry permissions.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the security and operational access authorization between port community participants and port locations. It captures ISPS-compliant access control, vehicle and equipment permissions, and time-bound authorization periods for participants to access specific port locations. Each record links one port community participant to one port location with security clearance levels, authorization dates, and operational restrictions that exist only in the context of this access relationship. This is the operational SSOT for port security access control and gate management systems.. Existence Justification: In maritime port operations, port community participants (trucking companies, stevedores, customs brokers, freight forwarders) require access to multiple port locations (berths, container yards, gate lanes, CFS sheds) based on their operational roles and contracts. Simultaneously, each port location is accessed by multiple participants throughout daily operations. Port security actively manages these access authorizations as time-bound, security-cleared permissions with specific operational restrictions per participant-location pair.'
}

Table customer.participant_trade_exemption {
  participant_trade_exemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each participant trade exemption record. Primary key.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the port community participant who holds the exemption or license']
  trade_restriction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the trade restriction for which the exemption or license is granted']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the government agency, regulatory body, or international organization that issued and approved the exemption or license.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the exemption or license was officially approved and granted to the participant.']
  authorized_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum volume, quantity, or value of goods that the participant is authorized to trade under this exemption. Unit depends on the restriction type (e.g., metric tons, TEUs, USD value).']
  authorized_volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the authorized volume (e.g., metric tons, TEUs, currency for value-based limits).']
  conditions varchar [note: 'Detailed conditions, restrictions, and compliance requirements that the participant must meet to maintain the validity of the exemption.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exemption record was created in the port community system.']
  exemption_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number or identifier assigned to the exemption or license by the issuing authority. Used for verification during customs clearance and cargo release processes.']
  exemption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exemption. Determines whether the exemption can be used for customs clearance and cargo release decisions.']
  exemption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the exemption mechanism granted to the participant. Determines the scope and conditions under which the participant may conduct otherwise restricted trade activities.']
  last_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the exemption was last verified or validated by port compliance officers during a cargo clearance process.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exemption record was last updated in the port community system.']
  valid_until date [note: 'Date on which the exemption or license expires. After this date, the participant is no longer authorized to conduct the specified trade activities without renewal.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the exemption or license becomes effective and the participant is authorized to conduct the specified trade activities.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the exemption or license granted to a port community participant for a specific trade restriction. It captures the legal authorization that permits a participant to conduct trade activities that would otherwise be prohibited or controlled under sanctions, embargoes, or trade controls. Each record links one port community participant to one trade restriction with attributes that exist only in the context of this exemption relationship, including exemption type, reference numbers, validity periods, authorized volumes, and approval authority.. Existence Justification: In maritime port operations, a single port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner) can hold exemptions or licenses for multiple trade restrictions (e.g., exemption for sanctioned territory A, license for controlled goods B, humanitarian exemption for embargo C). Conversely, a single trade restriction affects multiple participants, and multiple participants may hold exemptions for the same restriction under different terms. The business actively manages these exemptions as legal compliance artifacts with specific validity periods, authorized volumes, and approval authorities that are essential for customs clearance and cargo release decisions.'
}

Table customer.commodity_handling_authorization {
  commodity_handling_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each commodity handling authorization record. Primary key.']
  commodity_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the commodity code for which handling authorization is granted']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the port authority or trade compliance officer who granted this authorization. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the port community participant who holds this commodity handling authorization']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the commodity handling authorization was granted by port authority or trade compliance team. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity handling authorization. Determines whether the participant is currently permitted to handle this commodity type through the port.']
  certification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the regulatory certification, license, or permit that supports this handling authorization (e.g., dangerous goods handling license, customs broker accreditation, quarantine facility approval). Links to external regulatory authority records.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this authorization record was created in the Port Community System.']
  dangerous_goods_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this authorization includes permission to handle dangerous goods (IMDG class commodities). Requires additional ISPS and IMDG Code compliance certifications.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this commodity handling authorization expires and must be renewed. Port operations system enforces authorization validity before accepting cargo declarations.']
  handling_authorization_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the authorization scope. FULL = unrestricted handling, RESTRICTED = specific conditions apply, CONDITIONAL = requires additional approvals per shipment, TEMPORARY = time-limited trial authorization.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this authorization record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special conditions, restrictions, or compliance requirements specific to this participant-commodity authorization. Examples: \'Requires pre-arrival notification\', \'Limited to containerized cargo only\', \'Subject to quarterly audit\'.']
  volume_limit_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum cargo volume in metric tonnes that the participant is authorized to handle for this commodity type per calendar month. Null indicates no volume restriction. Enforced by Port Community System for capacity planning and risk management.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the regulatory and operational authorization for a port community participant to handle specific commodity types. It captures approval status, certification validity, volume limits, and dangerous goods handling permissions. Each record links one port community participant to one commodity code with attributes that exist only in the context of this authorization relationship. Critical for trade compliance, ISPS security, IMDG dangerous goods management, and customs clearance workflows.. Existence Justification: In maritime port operations, freight forwarders, cargo handlers, customs brokers, and shipping lines require regulatory authorization to handle specific commodity types (HS codes), particularly for dangerous goods (IMDG), quarantine-controlled goods, and restricted commodities. A single participant is authorized to handle multiple commodity types (e.g., a freight forwarder handles both reefer cargo and dry bulk), and each commodity type can be handled by multiple authorized participants. The port authority and trade compliance teams actively manage these authorizations with approval workflows, expiry tracking, volume limits, and dangerous goods certifications.'
}

Table tariff.port_tariff {
  port_tariff_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the port tariff schedule record. Primary key.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Port tariffs require national regulatory approval (approval_authority, regulatory_filing_required_flag). Country context determines jurisdiction, regulatory framework, currency regulations, and intern']
  superseded_by_tariff_port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port_tariff_id of the newer tariff schedule that replaces this one. Null if this is the current active schedule.']
  trade_restriction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_restriction. Business justification: Port tariffs must reference trade restrictions to exclude embargoed goods, sanctioned parties from standard tariff application. Regulatory compliance requirement for port authorities.']
  un_locode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.un_locode. Business justification: Port tariffs are published for specific UN LOCODE locations. Tariff schedules must reference the standardized port location code for regulatory filing, publication, and enforcement. Essential for tari']
  applicable_cargo_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of cargo types to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'FCL\', \'LCL\', \'Breakbulk\', \'Dangerous Goods\', \'Reefer\'). Null if applicable to all cargo types.']
  applicable_container_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of container types to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'20GP\', \'40GP\', \'40HC\', \'45HC\', \'Reefer\', \'Tank\', \'Flat Rack\'). Null if not container-specific.']
  applicable_movement_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of cargo movement types to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'Import\', \'Export\', \'Transshipment\', \'Empty Repositioning\', \'Restow\'). Null if applicable to all movements.']
  applicable_terminal_zones varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of terminal zones, berths, or container yards to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'Zone A\', \'Berth 1-5\', \'CY North\'). Null if applicable port-wide.']
  applicable_trade_lanes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of trade lanes or routes to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'Asia-Europe\', \'Transpacific\', \'Intra-Asia\'). Null if applicable to all lanes.']
  applicable_vessel_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of vessel types or categories to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'Container\', \'Bulk Carrier\', \'Tanker\', \'RoRo\'). Null if applicable to all vessel types.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or internal authority that approved this tariff schedule (e.g., \'Port Authority Board\', \'National Maritime Safety Authority\', \'Finance Committee\').']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the tariff schedule received regulatory or port authority board approval.']
  approval_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference or filing number assigned by the approval authority for audit and compliance tracking.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base rate amount for this tariff schedule. Interpretation depends on charge_type: per TEU for THC, per tonne for WHR, per movement for pilotage, per day for storage/DMG/DET. Null if tariff uses complex multi-tier pricing defined in tariff_item.']
  charge_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator identifying the category of port charge. THC=Terminal Handling Charge, WHR=Wharfage, PILOTAGE=Pilotage fees, TOWAGE=Towage services, MOORING=Mooring services, PORT_DUES=Port dues (light/conservancy/anchorage), STORAGE=Container storage, DMG=Demurrage, DET=Detention, BAF=Bunker Adjustment Factor, CAF=Currency Adjustment Factor, PIL=Port Infrastructure Levy.
