  sla_linked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff is tied to specific service level agreement performance metrics, with potential penalties or credits for non-compliance.']
  sla_response_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Committed maximum time in minutes from service request to mooring gang arrival at vessel, applicable when SLA is linked.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Additional terms, restrictions, or requirements that apply to this mooring tariff, such as weather limitations or equipment prerequisites.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the mooring tariff schedule, used for external reference and billing system integration.']
  tariff_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the mooring tariff schedule for business user identification and reporting purposes.']
  tariff_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the mooring tariff: draft (under development), active (in use), suspended (temporarily inactive), expired (past validity), or superseded (replaced by newer version).']
  tariff_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this tariff schedule in major.minor format, enabling tracking of tariff evolution and changes over time.']
  time_of_day_category varchar [note: 'Time period classification for differential pricing: day (standard business hours), night (after-hours), weekend, or holiday operations.']
  trade_lane varchar [note: 'Specific trade route or shipping lane to which this tariff applies, enabling route-specific pricing strategies.']
  vessel_category varchar [note: 'Classification of vessel type to which this tariff applies: container ship, bulk carrier, tanker, roll-on roll-off (RoRo), general cargo, cruise ship, or naval vessel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: container|bulk|tanker|roro|general_cargo|cruise|naval — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  waiting_time_surcharge_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional charge per hour when mooring gang is on standby waiting for vessel readiness or berth availability beyond the free waiting period.']

  Note: 'Mooring and unmooring service tariff defining charges for line-handling services during vessel arrival and departure. Rates are structured by vessel LOA band, GRT band, mooring type (conventional, buoy, single-point), time of day (day/night/weekend/holiday), and number of mooring gangs deployed. Captures base mooring fee, waiting time surcharge, and cancellation fee. Supports marine services billing in the marine and billing domains.'
}

Table tariff.port_dues_schedule {
  port_dues_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the port dues schedule record. Primary key for this entity.']
  anchorage_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.anchorage_area. Business justification: Anchorage dues are standard maritime charges for vessels at anchorage. Dues vary by anchorage area designation, security zone, and holding duration. Essential for anchorage-specific billing in vessel ']
  berth_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.berth. Business justification: Port dues often calculated based on berth utilization, berth depth, and LOA capacity. Regulatory filings and tariff schedules reference specific berths for dues calculation. Required for berth-specifi']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Port dues are national-level charges subject to country-specific regulations, international maritime conventions (SOLAS, MARPOL), and trade agreements. Country context determines regulatory framework,']
  isps_facility_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.isps_facility_record. Business justification: Port dues schedules include ISPS security levies tied to facility security level. Real cost recovery mechanism for maritime security compliance mandated by SOLAS.']
  masterdata_flag_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.flag_state. Business justification: Port dues often have flag state differentials (flag_state attribute). Bilateral maritime agreements, reciprocity arrangements, and flag state performance affect rates. Links to flag state master for t']
  primary_port_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this port dues schedule record in the system. Required for audit trail and accountability.']
  superseded_by_schedule_port_dues_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port dues schedule that replaces this schedule. Nullable for current active schedules. Maintains version history and audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority officially approved this port dues schedule for implementation.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base monetary amount for the port dues charge before any discounts, surcharges, or adjustments. Represents the standard statutory rate for the defined vessel classification and dues type.']
  call_frequency_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to port dues for vessels meeting the call frequency tier criteria. Incentivizes regular service commitments and port loyalty.']
  call_frequency_tier varchar [note: 'Classification tier based on vessel call frequency at the port. First call applies to vessels making their inaugural visit, regular applies to standard service schedules, frequent applies to high-frequency callers, and premium applies to vessels with committed service agreements. Higher frequency tiers may attract discounted dues rates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this port dues schedule record was first created in the system. Part of mandatory audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the port dues are denominated and payable.']
  dangerous_goods_surcharge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional percentage surcharge applied to port dues for vessels carrying International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) classified cargo. Reflects additional risk management and safety oversight costs.']
  dues_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the statutory port dues charge type levied on vessels. Light dues cover navigational aids, conservancy dues cover channel maintenance, port entry fee is the basic vessel call charge, anchorage dues apply to vessels at anchor, navigation dues cover vessel traffic services, and pilotage dues cover mandatory pilotage services.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this port dues schedule becomes legally binding and applicable to vessel calls. Aligns with regulatory publication requirements.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this port dues schedule remains valid. Nullable for open-ended schedules subject to regulatory review.']
  environmental_levy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage levy added to port dues to fund environmental protection initiatives, emissions monitoring, and compliance with MARPOL (Marine Pollution Convention) requirements.']
  exemption_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the criteria under which vessels may be exempt from port dues under this schedule. Typically includes naval vessels, government vessels on official duty, vessels in distress, and humanitarian mission vessels.']
  exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dues schedule includes provisions for exemptions. True if certain vessel categories (e.g., naval vessels, emergency response vessels, vessels in distress) may be exempt from dues under this schedule.']
  grt_band_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum Gross Registered Tonnage for the vessel size band to which this dues schedule applies. Nullable for open-ended upper bands.']
  grt_band_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum Gross Registered Tonnage for the vessel size band to which this dues schedule applies. GRT is the primary metric for vessel-based port dues calculation.']
  late_payment_penalty_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage penalty applied to overdue port dues payments. Calculated as a percentage of the outstanding amount per period (typically per month or per day).']
  loa_band_max_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel Length Overall in meters for this dues schedule band. Nullable for open-ended upper bands.']
  loa_band_min_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel Length Overall in meters for this dues schedule band. LOA may be used as a supplementary classification criterion for berth allocation and dues calculation.']
  maximum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum port dues charge amount applicable as a cap. Protects large vessel operators from excessive dues and maintains port competitiveness for mega-vessels.']
  measurement_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days over which call frequency is measured for discount eligibility. Typically 30, 90, or 365 days depending on service agreement terms.']
  minimum_calls_per_period varchar [note: 'Minimum number of vessel calls required within the measurement period to qualify for the call frequency discount. Used to enforce service commitment thresholds.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum port dues charge amount applicable regardless of calculated rate. Ensures a floor revenue per vessel call for small vessels where tonnage-based calculation would yield negligible amounts.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this port dues schedule record was last modified in the system. Part of mandatory audit trail for regulatory compliance and version control.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or clarifications regarding the application of this port dues schedule. May include references to specific regulatory provisions or commercial agreements.']
  nrt_band_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum Net Registered Tonnage for the vessel size band. Nullable for open-ended upper bands.']
  nrt_band_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum Net Registered Tonnage for the vessel size band. NRT represents the earning capacity of the vessel and may be used as an alternative or supplementary metric for dues calculation in some jurisdictions.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from vessel departure or invoice date within which port dues must be paid. Standard terms are typically 7, 14, or 30 days depending on customer credit standing.']
  port_dues_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the port dues schedule. Draft schedules are under development, active schedules are in force, suspended schedules are temporarily inactive, expired schedules have passed their validity period, superseded schedules have been replaced by newer versions, and withdrawn schedules have been permanently removed.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date on which this port dues schedule was published in the official gazette or public tariff book. Statutory port dues must be publicly disclosed prior to enforcement.']
  rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the base rate calculation. Per GRT and per NRT are tonnage-based, per call is a flat fee per vessel visit, per meter LOA is length-based, per day applies to anchorage dues, and flat fee is a fixed charge regardless of vessel size.']
  regulatory_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number or gazette notification number issued by the regulatory authority approving this port dues schedule. Required for legal enforceability and audit compliance.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the government or regulatory body that has jurisdiction over this port dues schedule. Port dues are statutory charges requiring regulatory approval and oversight.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the port dues schedule, used for external reference and regulatory reporting.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the port dues schedule for business identification and reporting purposes.']
  security_levy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage levy added to port dues to fund International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code compliance measures and port security infrastructure.']
  trade_type varchar [note: 'Classification of trade type for which this dues schedule applies. International trade involves cross-border cargo, coastal trade is between domestic ports, domestic trade is within national waters, cabotage is domestic cargo carriage reserved for national flag vessels, and transshipment involves cargo transfer without customs clearance.']
  vessel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of vessel type to which this dues schedule applies. Different vessel types may attract different dues rates based on port usage patterns and infrastructure requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: container|bulk_carrier|tanker|roro|general_cargo|passenger|fishing|naval|yacht — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Port dues (port call charges) schedule defining the statutory charges levied on vessels calling at the port. Captures dues type (light dues, conservancy dues, port entry fee, anchorage dues), vessel classification (GRT band, vessel type, flag state), trade type (international, coastal, domestic), call frequency discounts, and regulatory authority reference. Distinct from cargo-based wharfage; port dues are vessel-based charges payable by the shipowner or agent per vessel call.'
}

Table tariff.applicability {
  applicability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tariff applicability rule. Primary key for the tariff applicability matrix.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Applicability rules determine which tariffs apply under which agreement contexts. Agreement-specific applicability rules enable contract-based pricing determination, ensuring correct tariff selection ']
  applicability_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this applicability rule record. Required for audit trail and data governance.']
  applicability_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this applicability rule. Required for audit trail and compliance with tariff governance policies.']
  applicability_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this applicability rule record. Required for audit trail and change tracking.']
  commodity_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.commodity_code. Business justification: Applicability rules filter by cargo category and type. Commodity codes provide standardized cargo classification for tariff rule evaluation. Links denormalized cargo attributes to commodity master for']
  masterdata_shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: Tariff applicability rules are customer-segment specific (customer_segment attribute). Shipping lines represent major customer segments with negotiated rates and service levels. Links to shipping line']
  origin_un_locode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.un_locode. Business justification: Applicability rules specify port_of_origin for trade lane-based tariff filtering. UN LOCODE provides standardized location references for origin/destination routing. Essential for trade lane tariff ap']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Specific customer to which this tariff applies. Null if the rule applies to a segment or all customers. Enables customer-specific negotiated rates.']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port tariff schedule that this applicability rule applies to. Links to the published tariff schedule containing base rates and charge structures.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the negotiated rate card that this applicability rule applies to. Links to customer-specific or segment-specific rate cards with SLA-linked pricing.']
  superseded_by_rule_applicability_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicability rule that supersedes this one. Used for version control and historical tariff tracking.']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: Applicability rules can target specific vessels (vessel_size_category, loa/grt ranges). Vessel master provides actual specifications for rule evaluation. Enables vessel-specific tariff exceptions, pre']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Tariff applicability rules specify which security zones trigger which charges (e.g., \'wharfage applies only in customs-controlled zones\', \'restricted area access fee applies in MARSEC 2+ zones\'). Bill']
  applicability_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the applicability rule. Only active rules are used by the billing engine for tariff selection.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether application of this tariff requires manual approval before billing. True for exceptional or high-value tariffs.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this applicability rule was approved. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance.']
  auto_apply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this applicability rule is automatically applied by the billing engine or requires manual selection. True enables automated billing.']
  conflict_resolution_order varchar [note: 'Secondary ordering criterion for conflict resolution when multiple rules have the same priority. Used in conjunction with rule_priority for deterministic tariff selection.']
  container_size_teu decimal(4,2) [note: 'Container size in TEU to which this tariff applies. Standard values are 1.0 for 20-foot and 2.0 for 40-foot containers. Used for volume-based pricing.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Specific container type to which this tariff applies. Container type impacts handling charges, storage rates, and equipment requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard_20|standard_40|high_cube_40|reefer|tank|open_top|flat_rack|all_types — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this applicability rule record was first created in the system. Required for audit trail and data lineage.']
  dangerous_goods_class varchar [note: 'IMDG dangerous goods classification to which this tariff applies. Dangerous goods require specialized handling and attract premium charges.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this applicability rule becomes active. Used for temporal tariff management and billing period control.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this applicability rule remains active. Null indicates open-ended applicability. Used for tariff expiration and renewal management.']
  exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule defines an exclusion (tariff does NOT apply) rather than an inclusion. Used for negative matching in complex tariff matrices.']
  grt_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum gross registered tonnage for this tariff to apply. Null indicates no upper limit. Enables GRT-based tariff banding.']
  grt_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum gross registered tonnage for this tariff to apply. GRT is used for port dues and regulatory fee calculation.']
  loa_max_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel length overall in meters for this tariff to apply. Null indicates no upper limit. Used for vessel size-based tariff banding.']
  loa_min_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel length overall in meters for this tariff to apply. LOA is a key dimension for berth allocation and wharfage calculation.']
  load_status varchar [note: 'Container load status to which this tariff applies. FCL (Full Container Load) and LCL (Less than Container Load) have different handling and pricing models.']
  maximum_volume_teu decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum volume in TEU for this tariff to apply. Null indicates no upper limit. Used for volume-based tariff banding.']
  minimum_volume_teu decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum committed volume in TEU for this tariff to apply. Used for volume-based discount tiers and contract enforcement.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this applicability rule record was last modified. Required for audit trail and change tracking.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Type of cargo movement to which this tariff applies. Movement type impacts handling complexity, documentation requirements, and pricing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special conditions, or business rationale for this applicability rule.']
  port_of_destination varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the port of discharge to which this tariff applies. Null indicates applicability to all destination ports. Used for origin-destination pricing matrices.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the applicability rule. Used for external reference and billing engine configuration.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the applicability rule, summarizing the combination of dimensions it covers (e.g., \'Premium Container - Asia Trade Lane - Terminal A\').']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Priority order for conflict resolution when multiple tariff applicability rules match a billable event. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by billing engine to deterministically select the correct tariff.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of port service to which this tariff applies. Service type determines the charge calculation method and billing rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: thc|wharfage|pilotage|towage|storage|demurrage|detention|all_services — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'SLA tier to which this tariff applies. SLA tier determines service commitments, performance targets, and premium pricing.']
  terminal_zone varchar [note: 'Specific terminal zone or berth area to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'Terminal A\', \'Container Berth 1-3\', \'Bulk Terminal\'). Terminal zone determines operational costs and service capabilities.']
  trade_lane varchar [note: 'Trade lane or route to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'Asia-Europe\', \'Transpacific\', \'Intra-Asia\'). Trade lane determines service levels, competitive positioning, and pricing strategies.']
  vessel_size_category varchar [note: 'Vessel size classification to which this tariff applies. Size category impacts berth allocation, crane requirements, and handling complexity.']
  vessel_type varchar [note: 'Type of vessel to which this tariff applies. Vessel type determines applicable port services, berth requirements, and handling charges. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: container|bulk_carrier|tanker|roro|general_cargo|cruise|naval|all_types — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Association entity defining the applicability matrix for tariff schedules and rate cards — specifying which tariff applies to which combination of customer segment, vessel type, cargo category, trade lane, terminal zone, and service type. Captures applicability rule priority, effective period, and conflict resolution order when multiple tariffs could apply. Enables the billing engine to deterministically select the correct tariff for any given billable event without ambiguity.'
}

Table tariff.currency_adjustment {
  currency_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the currency adjustment factor record. Primary key for the currency adjustment entity.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: CAF adjustments are tied to currency pairs and exchange rate movements between trading countries (base_currency_code, adjustment_currency_code). Country context determines applicable exchange rates, c']
  primary_currency_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or authority who approved this CAF rate for publication and application. Critical for audit trail and accountability.']
  superseded_by_caf_currency_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer CAF record that replaces this one. Enables time-series tracking of CAF rate evolution and historical rate reconstruction.']
  tertiary_currency_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this CAF rate record. Supports change tracking and accountability.']
  adjustment_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency for which the adjustment factor is being applied to compensate for exchange rate fluctuations.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status of the CAF rate record, indicating whether it has been reviewed and authorized by the appropriate tariff authority.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CAF rate was officially approved. Establishes the point at which the rate became authorized for use.']
  auto_apply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CAF should be automatically applied by the billing system to eligible charges, or requires manual application. True for automatic application.']
  base_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the base currency in which tariff rates are published (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD).']
  billing_system_code varchar [note: 'The charge code or identifier used in the billing system (e.g., NAVIS N4, SAP) to apply this CAF to invoices. Enables system integration and automated billing.']
  caf_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the CAF rate record, used in tariff publications and billing system references.']
  caf_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the CAF rate schedule, typically including currency pair and period reference.']
  caf_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage surcharge or discount applied to base tariff rates to compensate for currency exchange rate fluctuations. Positive values represent surcharges, negative values represent discounts.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the base amount to which the CAF percentage is applied: freight charges only, Terminal Handling Charges (THC) only, total charges, or base tariff rates.']
  cargo_type_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the cargo categories to which this CAF applies. Allows differentiated currency adjustments for different cargo handling operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|containerized|breakbulk|liquid_bulk|dry_bulk|roro|general_cargo — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CAF rate record was first created in the system. Critical for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAF rate record indicating whether it is in draft, awaiting approval, actively applied to billing, temporarily suspended, expired, or superseded by a newer rate.']
  current_exchange_rate decimal(15,6) [note: 'The current market exchange rate at the time of CAF calculation or publication. Used in conjunction with reference rate to determine adjustment percentage.']
  customer_tier_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the customer segments or tiers to which this CAF applies. Allows differentiated currency adjustments based on customer relationship and volume commitments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|premium|standard|spot — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_resolution_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual terms and procedures for resolving disputes related to CAF application, including arbitration clauses and escalation processes.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this CAF rate becomes applicable to vessel calls and cargo movements. Aligns with tariff publication cycles and regulatory filing requirements.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this CAF rate remains valid. Nullable for open-ended rates that remain in effect until superseded.']
  exchange_rate_date date [note: 'The date on which the current exchange rate was captured or fixed for CAF calculation purposes. Critical for audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  exchange_rate_reference varchar [note: 'The source or benchmark exchange rate used to calculate the CAF percentage (e.g., Central Bank rate, OANDA average, Bloomberg fixing). Provides transparency for rate justification.']
  maximum_caf_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum absolute currency adjustment amount that can be charged, capping exposure for large transactions. Provides cost predictability for customers.']
  minimum_caf_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum absolute currency adjustment amount that will be charged, regardless of calculated percentage. Ensures administrative costs are covered for small transactions.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CAF rate record was last modified. Enables change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special conditions, or contextual information about this CAF rate that does not fit structured fields.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days advance notice provided to customers before this CAF becomes effective. Ensures compliance with contractual and regulatory notice requirements.']
  publication_date date [note: 'The date on which this CAF rate was officially published or announced to customers and stakeholders. Establishes notice period compliance.']
  publication_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the official tariff publication, circular, or notice in which this CAF was announced. Critical for regulatory compliance and customer communication.']
  reference_exchange_rate decimal(15,6) [note: 'The baseline exchange rate used as the reference point for calculating currency adjustment. Deviations from this rate trigger CAF adjustments.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which regulatory authorities approved this CAF rate for implementation. Required for jurisdictions with mandatory tariff approval processes.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory filing submission if this CAF required government or port authority approval.']
  regulatory_filing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CAF rate must be filed with port authorities or regulatory bodies before implementation. True if regulatory approval is mandatory.']
  service_type_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies the port services to which this CAF applies, such as container handling, wharfage, pilotage, or all services. Enables selective application of currency adjustments.']
  trade_lane_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the geographic trade lanes or routes to which this CAF applies (e.g., Asia-Europe, Transpacific, Intra-Asia). May be \'ALL\' for global application or specific lane codes.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this CAF rate schedule. Increments with each revision to support change tracking and audit requirements.']
  vessel_category_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies the vessel types or size categories to which this CAF applies (e.g., container vessels, bulk carriers, tankers, all vessels). Enables vessel-specific currency adjustments.']

  Note: 'CAF (Currency Adjustment Factor) rate record defining the periodic currency surcharge applied to base tariff rates to compensate for exchange rate fluctuations. Captures the applicable currency pair, CAF percentage, effective period, calculation base (percentage of freight or THC), trade lane scope, and publication reference. Managed as a time-series of CAF adjustments enabling historical rate reconstruction for billing disputes and audit.'
}

Table tariff.bunker_adjustment {
  bunker_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bunker adjustment factor record. Primary key.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: BAF rates vary by bunker fuel pricing regions. Country/region determines applicable fuel index, pricing zone, and adjustment calculation methodology. Links to country master for region-based BAF appli']
  primary_bunker_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that created this BAF record.']
  superseded_by_baf_bunker_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bunker_adjustment_id of the BAF record that supersedes this one, enabling time-series tracking. Nullable if current.']
  applicable_service_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of port service types to which this BAF applies (e.g., THC, wharfage, pilotage, towage, all services).']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the authority that approved this BAF rate (e.g., Port Director, Tariff Committee, Regulatory Commission).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this BAF rate was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  approval_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number or document identifier associated with the approval of this BAF rate.']
  baf_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the BAF rate record, used for external reference and publication.']
  baf_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the bunker adjustment factor rate, typically including the applicable period and scope.']
  baf_rate_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the BAF surcharge. Interpretation depends on calculation_method (e.g., USD per TEU for flat rates, percentage value for percentage-based).']
  baf_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BAF rate based on its purpose and application scope (standard, emergency, seasonal, trade lane specific, vessel specific, temporary).']
  bunker_adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BAF rate record (draft, pending approval, approved, active, suspended, expired, superseded). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|expired|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the BAF charge: flat rate per TEU, flat rate per FEU, percentage of base tariff rate, tiered structure, or custom formula.']
  cargo_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of cargo types to which this BAF applies (e.g., FCL, LCL, breakbulk, bulk, liquid, all types).']
  container_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of container types to which this BAF applies (e.g., standard, reefer, dangerous goods, oversized, all types).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this BAF record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the BAF rate is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD).']
  customer_tier_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of customer tiers or segments to which this BAF applies (e.g., tier_1, tier_2, tier_3, all_customers).']
  dwt_band_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum Deadweight Tonnage (DWT) for which this BAF rate applies. Nullable if no maximum.']
  dwt_band_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum Deadweight Tonnage (DWT) for which this BAF rate applies. Nullable if no minimum.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this BAF rate becomes applicable and enforceable for billing purposes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this BAF rate remains valid. Nullable for open-ended rates.']
  exemption_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of conditions under which customers or transactions may be exempt from this BAF surcharge.']
  fuel_index_reference varchar [note: 'The fuel price index or benchmark used as the basis for calculating the BAF rate (e.g., Platts Singapore, MOPS, IFO380, VLSFO, MGO, Brent, WTI, or custom index). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: platts|mops|ifo380|vlsfo|mgo|brent|wti|custom — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fuel_index_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual fuel price index value (in USD per metric ton or other unit) at the time of BAF calculation or publication.']
  grt_band_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum Gross Registered Tonnage (GRT) for which this BAF rate applies. Nullable if no maximum.']
  grt_band_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum Gross Registered Tonnage (GRT) for which this BAF rate applies. Nullable if no minimum.']
  loa_band_max_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel length overall (LOA) in meters for which this BAF rate applies. Nullable if no maximum.']
  loa_band_min_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel length overall (LOA) in meters for which this BAF rate applies. Nullable if no minimum.']
  maximum_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum charge amount cap for the BAF surcharge. Nullable if no cap applies.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum charge amount that will be applied regardless of calculated BAF value. Nullable if no minimum.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this BAF record was last modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review of this BAF rate.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this BAF rate record.']
  publication_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the authority or organization that published or mandated this BAF rate (e.g., port authority, shipping line consortium, regulatory body).']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date on which this BAF rate was officially published or announced to stakeholders.']
  rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the BAF rate (per TEU, per FEU, per container, per ton, per CBM, percentage, per vessel call). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_teu|per_feu|per_container|per_ton|per_cbm|percentage|per_vessel_call — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the regulatory filing associated with this BAF rate, if applicable.']
  regulatory_filing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BAF rate requires filing with a regulatory authority (True) or not (False).']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this BAF rate is reviewed and potentially updated (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, event-driven, continuous).']
  trade_lane_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of trade lanes to which this BAF applies (e.g., Asia-Europe, Transpacific, Intra-Asia, All Lanes).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this BAF rate record, incremented with each revision to support historical tracking.']
  vessel_size_category varchar [note: 'Vessel size category to which this BAF applies (feeder, Panamax, Post-Panamax, New Panamax, Suezmax, Ultra Large, all sizes). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: feeder|panamax|post_panamax|new_panamax|suezmax|ultra_large|all_sizes — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vessel_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of vessel types to which this BAF applies (e.g., container, bulk, tanker, RoRo, general cargo, all types).']

  Note: 'BAF (Bunker Adjustment Factor) rate record defining the periodic fuel surcharge applied to port and shipping tariffs to compensate for bunker fuel price volatility. Captures BAF rate, applicable fuel index reference (Platts, MOPS), effective period, trade lane scope, vessel type applicability, calculation method (flat per TEU or percentage of base rate), and publication authority. Maintained as a time-series enabling accurate historical charge reconstruction.'
}

Table tariff.filing {
  filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tariff filing record. Primary key for the tariff filing entity.']
  compliance_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Tariff filings subject to regulatory audits for compliance with published rates and anti-competitive practices. Regulatory oversight process by port authorities and competition regulators.']
  filing_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the initial tariff filing record.']
  filing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the port authority user who submitted the tariff filing to the regulator.']
  filing_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the tariff filing record.']
  filing_withdrawn_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the port authority user who initiated the withdrawal of the tariff filing.']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port tariff schedule being filed with the regulatory authority.']
  primary_superseded_by_filing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subsequent tariff filing that supersedes this filing, creating a version history chain.']
  superseded_filing_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on filing (superseded_filing_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority formally approved the tariff filing, granting permission for the tariff to take effect.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Final decision status from the regulatory authority on the tariff filing, indicating approval, conditional approval, rejection, or withdrawal.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the regulatory authority approved the tariff filing, including time zone information.']
  approved_by_authority_officer varchar [note: 'Name and title of the regulatory authority officer who signed off on the tariff filing approval.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the complete audit trail of all actions, reviews, and decisions related to this tariff filing.']
  conditions_imposed varchar [note: 'Specific conditions, restrictions, or requirements imposed by the regulatory authority as part of the tariff approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tariff filing record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts in the tariff filing.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the official tariff filing document submitted to the regulatory authority.']
  effective_date_granted date [note: 'Date granted by the regulatory authority when the approved tariff is authorized to take effect and be applied to port services.']
  estimated_revenue_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected annual revenue impact of the proposed tariff changes, used in regulatory impact assessment.']
  expiry_date_granted date [note: 'Date when the approved tariff expires and must be renewed or refiled, as specified by the regulatory authority.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the tariff filing in the regulatory approval lifecycle, from draft preparation through final approval or rejection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|public_consultation|approved|rejected|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tariff filing indicating whether it is a new tariff, amendment to existing tariff, full revision, withdrawal, or emergency filing.']
  gazette_publication_date date [note: 'Date when the approved tariff was published in the official government gazette or public registry, making it legally effective.']
  gazette_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number or citation of the gazette publication where the approved tariff was published.']
  impact_assessment_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory impact assessment was completed and submitted as part of the tariff filing.']
  legal_review_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether internal legal review was completed before submitting the tariff filing to the regulatory authority.']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'Date when the internal legal review of the tariff filing was completed and approved for submission.']
  legal_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the legal counsel or compliance officer who reviewed the tariff filing for regulatory compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tariff filing record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or observations related to the tariff filing process, regulatory interactions, or special circumstances.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required between approval and effective date, allowing stakeholders to prepare for tariff changes.']
  public_comment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of public comments or submissions received during the consultation period from stakeholders.']
  public_consultation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the public consultation period closes and no further stakeholder comments will be accepted.']
  public_consultation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tariff filing requires a public consultation period for stakeholder feedback before approval.']
  public_consultation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the public consultation period begins, allowing stakeholders to submit comments on the proposed tariff.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned by the port regulator or maritime authority upon submission of the tariff filing.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the port regulator or maritime authority responsible for reviewing and approving the tariff filing.']
  regulatory_authority_contact varchar [note: 'Contact information for the regulatory authority case officer or department handling the tariff filing.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided by the regulatory authority for rejecting the tariff filing, including specific deficiencies or non-compliance issues.']
  stakeholder_feedback_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of key themes, concerns, and recommendations raised by stakeholders during the public consultation period.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the tariff filing was formally submitted to the port regulator or maritime authority for review and approval.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the tariff filing was submitted to the regulatory authority, including time zone information.']
  supporting_documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documents submitted with the filing, such as cost justifications, impact assessments, or stakeholder consultation records.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date when the tariff filing was withdrawn by the port authority before regulatory approval was granted.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the tariff filing was withdrawn, such as business decision change, stakeholder opposition, or regulatory feedback.']

  Note: 'Regulatory filing and gazette publication record for port tariff schedules. Captures the submission to the port regulator or maritime authority, filing reference number, gazette publication date, public comment period, approval status, conditions imposed, and effective date granted. Tracks the full regulatory lifecycle from draft submission through public consultation to final approval. Required for compliance with port pricing regulations and enables audit of tariff legitimacy.'
}

Table tariff.negotiation {
  negotiation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tariff or rate negotiation record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Tariff negotiations culminate in binding agreements. The negotiation process tracks commercial discussions that result in formal agreements governing negotiated rates. Critical for commercial negotiat']
  counter_negotiation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on negotiation (counter_negotiation_id)']
  masterdata_shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: Commercial negotiations occur with shipping line customers for volume-based discounts, SLA-linked pricing, and service commitments. Links negotiation records to customer master for contract lifecycle ']
  negotiation_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who provided final approval for this negotiation.']
  negotiation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port staff member who initiated this negotiation.']
  negotiation_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this negotiation record.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (shipping line, cargo owner, terminal operator) involved in this negotiation.']
  resulting_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate card created as a result of this successful negotiation.']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of authority required to approve this negotiation based on materiality and deviation from standard terms.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the negotiation was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this negotiation requires formal approval from senior management or commercial committee.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the negotiated agreement automatically renews at expiry unless terminated.']
  cargo_type varchar [note: 'Type of cargo covered by this negotiation (e.g., containerized, bulk, breakbulk, RoRo, liquid bulk).']
  commercial_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed business rationale and justification for the proposed rates and terms, including competitive analysis and strategic considerations.']
  commitment_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months for which the volume or revenue commitment applies.']
  competitor_benchmark_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Competitive benchmark rate from rival ports used as reference during negotiation.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the confidentiality level for this negotiation and its terms.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Specific container type applicable to this negotiation if service is container-related.']
  counter_offer_rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The counter-offer rate amount proposed by the customer in response to the port\'s proposal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this negotiation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this negotiation.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Segmentation tier of the customer for pricing and service differentiation purposes.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount from standard tariff rates being negotiated.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the negotiated rates and terms become effective.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which the negotiated rates and terms remain valid.']
  escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Description of any rate escalation mechanism agreed upon (e.g., CPI-linked, fuel-indexed, annual percentage increase).']
  final_agreed_rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The final rate amount agreed upon by both parties after negotiation completion.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'Date when the negotiation was formally initiated.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this negotiation record was last updated.']
  negotiation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the negotiation process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_progress|under_review|approved|rejected|withdrawn|completed|superseded — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  negotiation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the negotiation based on its purpose and scope. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_contract|renewal|amendment|exception|volume_discount|sla_linked|ad_hoc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing key discussion points, customer concerns, and negotiation dynamics throughout the process.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required for termination or material changes to the agreement.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days allowed for payment after invoice date as negotiated.']
  penalty_clause_description varchar [note: 'Description of penalties applicable if committed volumes or service levels are not met.']
  premium_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage premium above standard tariff rates being negotiated for enhanced service levels.']
  proposed_rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The rate amount proposed by the port in the current negotiation round.']
  rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the negotiated rate (e.g., per TEU, per GRT, per move, per hour, per tonne).']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number assigned to this negotiation for tracking and communication purposes.']
  revenue_commitment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum revenue commitment amount offered by the customer over the contract period.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of commercial risk associated with the negotiated terms (e.g., revenue impact, customer credit risk, volume uncertainty).']
  round varchar [note: 'Sequential counter indicating the current round of negotiation (1 for initial proposal, incremented for each counter-offer cycle).']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of port service being negotiated (e.g., THC, wharfage, pilotage, demurrage, storage, detention).']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service level tier associated with the negotiated rates, defining performance commitments.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Terms and conditions under which either party may terminate the negotiated agreement.']
  trade_lane varchar [note: 'Specific trade lane or route to which the negotiated rates apply.']
  volume_commitment_teu decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum volume commitment in TEU offered by the customer as part of the negotiation terms.']

  Note: 'Record of a tariff or rate negotiation between the port and a customer (shipping line, cargo owner, terminal operator). Captures negotiation reference, parties involved, proposed rates, counter-offers, negotiation rounds, commercial justification, volume commitments offered, final agreed terms, and link to resulting rate card or exception. Provides audit trail for how commercial pricing decisions were reached and supports internal governance review.'
}

  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Invoices in maritime ports must reference master service agreements for rate validation, payment terms inheritance, revenue allocation, and contract compliance auditing. Essential for contract-based b']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Each invoice is generated within a specific billing cycle. The billing_cycle table contains cycle_code, start_date, end_date, fiscal_year, fiscal_period. Adding billing_cycle_id allows JOIN to retriev']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Invoices track which terminal or operational cost center generated revenue. Essential for profitability analysis by berth, terminal, or service line. Management reporting requires cost center attribut']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Invoice creation audit trail requires tracking which billing clerk prepared each invoice for dispute resolution, compliance audits, and performance management. Maritime ports require strict financial ']
  flag_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.flag_state. Business justification: Vessel flag state determines applicable port charge exemptions, bilateral agreements, and regulatory fee structures. Critical for international maritime billing compliance and tariff application.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Invoice revenue posts to GL accounts for financial reporting. Monthly close requires invoice-to-GL reconciliation. Port billing systems map service charges to chart of accounts for revenue recognition']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner) being invoiced. Links to customer master data.']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Invoices must reference the published port tariff schedule under which charges were calculated for regulatory compliance, audit trail, and dispute resolution. Maritime port authorities require invoice']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Maritime ports operate multiple profit centers (container terminal, bulk cargo, passenger services). Invoice revenue must be attributed for segment reporting and divisional P&L analysis required by po']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: When invoicing under negotiated commercial agreements, invoice must reference the applicable rate card for contract compliance, audit trail, and to distinguish negotiated rates from published tariff r']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.run. Business justification: Invoices are generated by billing runs. The invoice table has navis_billing_reference, and run table has navis_export_batch_reference. Adding run_id FK enables direct linkage to the billing run that g']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Invoices to sanctioned entities must be flagged and blocked. Billing systems reference screening results to prevent invoice generation for sanctioned parties, supporting OFAC/EU sanctions compliance a']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Security incidents (theft, cargo damage, unauthorized access, security breaches during vessel operations) generate billable charges for investigation costs, additional security measures, equipment dam']
  shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: Invoices are often addressed to shipping lines as the commercial party responsible for vessel operations charges. Essential for shipping line account management and consolidated billing processes.']
  vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel call for which services were rendered. Links invoice to specific vessel visit and berth allocation.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net adjustment amount for credits, chargebacks, or billing corrections. Can be positive or negative.']
  baf_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Bunker Adjustment Factor surcharge applied to compensate for fuel price fluctuations. Common in maritime billing.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading reference number for cargo-related charges. Links invoice to specific cargo shipment documentation.']
  caf_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Currency Adjustment Factor surcharge applied to compensate for exchange rate fluctuations. Common in international maritime billing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the billing system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  credit_note_number varchar [note: 'Credit note number issued against this invoice for refunds, adjustments, or dispute resolutions. Links to credit memo document.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the invoice amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Defines the monetary unit for all amounts.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the invoice was delivered to the customer. Supports multi-channel billing communication.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the invoice based on volume agreements, early payment terms, or promotional offers.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was formally disputed by the customer. Triggers dispute resolution workflow.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by customer for disputing the invoice. Captured when invoice status transitions to disputed.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected per the agreed payment terms. Used for accounts receivable aging and overdue calculation.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was officially issued to the customer. Principal business event timestamp for revenue recognition and accounts receivable aging.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique invoice number issued to port community participants. Generated from NAVIS N4 TOS billing module and synchronized with SAP S/4HANA FI document number.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the billing workflow. Tracks progression from draft through payment or dispute resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|paid|partially_paid|disputed|cancelled|overdue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this invoice record. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates and billing adjustments.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which payment is expected or received. Instrument used for settlement of the invoice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bank_transfer|wire_transfer|credit_card|debit_card|check|cash|edi_payment|direct_debit — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date payment was received and cleared in the port authority bank account. Used for cash flow reporting and revenue recognition.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Bank transaction reference or remittance advice number provided with payment. Used for payment reconciliation.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms defining the number of days from invoice date until payment is due. Determines due date calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_7|net_15|net_30|net_45|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|prepaid — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pod_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE five-character code identifying the port of discharge for the cargo. Used for trade lane analysis and tariff determination.']
  pol_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE five-character code identifying the port of loading for the cargo. Used for trade lane analysis and tariff determination.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or special instructions related to this invoice. Used for billing notes, customer communications, or internal annotations.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue from this invoice is recognized in financial statements per IFRS 15 criteria. May differ from invoice date.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI accounting document number for this invoice. Links billing record to financial accounting system of record.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Primary category of port service being billed. Includes Terminal Handling Charge (THC), wharfage, pilotage, demurrage, detention, and other maritime services. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: thc|wharfage|pilotage|demurrage|detention|towage|mooring|berth_hire|reefer|storage — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of all line items before taxes, adjustments, and discounts. Base amount for revenue calculation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice, including VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes per jurisdiction.']
  tax_exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the tax exemption certificate on file justifying tax exemption status. Required for audit compliance.']
  tax_exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is exempt from taxation due to customer status, service type, or regulatory exemption.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code determining which tax rates and rules apply to this invoice. ISO country code or regional tax authority identifier.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final total amount due including subtotal, taxes, discounts, and adjustments. Net amount payable by customer.']

  Note: 'Core billing document issued to port community participants (shipping lines, freight forwarders, cargo owners) for all port services rendered. Generated from NAVIS N4 TOS and integrated with SAP S/4HANA FI. Captures invoice number, invoice date, due date, billing period, total amount, currency, tax amount, invoice status (draft/issued/paid/disputed/cancelled), payment terms, vessel call reference, BOL reference, service type (THC, wharfage, pilotage, demurrage, detention), port of loading/discharge, and SAP document number. SSOT for all revenue billing documents.'
}

Business justification: Line-level profit center attribution for multi-service invoices spanning different business units. Transfer pricing between port divisions requires granular profit center tracking at the service line ']
  rate_card_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card_line. Business justification: Line items invoiced under negotiated rates must link to the specific rate card line that defines the agreed unit rate, volume tiers, and service level. Required for contract compliance verification an']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.rate_schedule. Business justification: Each invoice line item must trace to contracted rate schedule for tariff audit, regulatory compliance (port authority rate filing), and billing dispute resolution. Core linkage for automated rate appl']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific port service rendered. Maps to the port tariff schedule and service catalog.']
  terminal_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port equipment used to provide the service (crane, RTG, AGV). Enables equipment utilization and cost allocation analysis.']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Container handling and ground slot charges must reference the terminal zone where services occurred for zone-level revenue analysis and operational cost allocation. Critical for multi-terminal port op']
  vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel call if this line item relates to vessel-specific services (pilotage, towage, berth charges). Links the charge to the vessel visit.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.warehouse. Business justification: Cargo storage charges must reference which warehouse facility provided the service for accurate billing and warehouse-level revenue tracking. Essential for bonded vs non-bonded storage differentiation']
  adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a billing adjustment or correction. True for adjustments (credit notes, rebills), False for original charges.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number associated with this line item. Links the charge to the specific cargo shipment documentation.']
  charge_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the charge type. THC=Terminal Handling Charge, WHR=Wharfage, DMG=Demurrage, DET=Detention, BAF=Bunker Adjustment Factor, CAF=Currency Adjustment Factor. Enables revenue analysis by charge category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: THC|WHR|DMG|DET|BAF|CAF|PILOTAGE|TOWAGE|STORAGE|HANDLING|BERTH|SECURITY — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO 6346 container identification number if this line item relates to a specific container. Format: 4 letters (owner code) + 7 digits (serial number + check digit).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line (unit rate, line amount, tax amount, net amount).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of discount applied to this line item. Reduces the line amount before tax calculation.']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any discount applied to this line item. References promotional discounts, volume discounts, or contractual rate reductions.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is under dispute by the customer. True if disputed, False otherwise. Affects accounts receivable aging and collection processes.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for dispute if dispute_flag is True. Captures customer objections and billing discrepancies.']
  line_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount for this line item before tax. Calculated as quantity multiplied by unit rate, plus any applicable adjustments.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the invoice. Determines the ordering and display sequence of line items on the invoice document.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data governance.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount for this line item including tax. Sum of line amount and tax amount. Contributes to the invoice total.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this line item. Captures special instructions, billing clarifications, or operational context.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the service rendered. May represent TEUs, FEUs, CBM, hours, moves, or other units depending on the service type.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date when revenue for this line item is recognized in the financial statements. May differ from invoice date based on revenue recognition policies.']
  service_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service was completed. Used to calculate duration-based charges and service level agreement compliance.']
  service_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service commenced. Used for time-based charges such as storage, demurrage, and detention calculations.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount calculated for this line item. Derived from line amount multiplied by tax rate.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code determining the tax treatment for this line item. References the tax jurisdiction and rate applicable to the service.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tax rate percentage applied to this line item. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10% tax).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity. TEU=Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit, FEU=Forty-foot Equivalent Unit, CBM=Cubic Meter. Aligns with maritime logistics industry standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TEU|FEU|CBM|HOUR|MOVE|TON|UNIT|DAY — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Rate per unit of measure. The price charged for one unit of the service (e.g., rate per TEU, rate per hour).']

  Note: 'Individual line items on a port services invoice, each representing a discrete chargeable service or tariff component. Captures line number, service code, service description, tariff item reference, quantity (TEUs, FEUs, CBM, hours, moves), unit of measure, unit rate, line amount, tax code, tax amount, net amount, container number, BOL reference, vessel call ID, charge category (THC, WHR, DMG, DET, BAF, CAF, pilotage, towage, storage), and NAVIS N4 charge code. Enables granular revenue analysis at the service level.'
}

Table billing.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment record. Primary key for the payment entity.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Payments are allocated to billing cycles for period accounting and cash flow reporting. This FK enables cycle-based payment analysis. Keeps fiscal_year and fiscal_period on payment as execution-specif']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Payment cost center attribution enables cash collection performance tracking by operational unit. Port finance teams measure collection efficiency by terminal or service division for working capital m']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Cash receipts post to bank GL accounts for cash accounting. Daily bank reconciliation process requires linking payments to specific bank account GL codes for treasury management and cash flow reportin']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice against which this payment is applied. Links payment to billing document.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant account making the payment. Identifies the payer.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Cash receipts attributed to profit centers for cash-basis divisional reporting. Port authorities track cash realization by business segment for liquidity planning and capital allocation decisions.']
  reconciled_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Payment reconciliation is a critical financial control requiring employee accountability. Port finance teams need to track who reconciled each payment for audit trails and segregation of duties compli']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount that has been allocated to specific invoices. May be less than amount_paid if payment is partially applied or held on account.']
  amount_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount paid in the transaction currency. Represents the gross payment received before any adjustments or allocations.']
  bank_reference varchar [note: 'Bank transaction reference or confirmation number provided by the financial institution. Used for payment tracing and dispute resolution.']
  base_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Payment amount converted to the port\'s base reporting currency using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting and EBITDA calculation.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the payment was submitted (online portal, bank transfer, Port Community System, SAP, manual entry, or Electronic Data Interchange).']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was cleared and funds were confirmed available in the port\'s bank account. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, SGD).']
  discount_taken decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early payment discount amount claimed by the customer and deducted from the invoice total. Reflects payment terms incentives.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for payment dispute case if reconciliation status is disputed. Links to dispute resolution workflow.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert payment currency to port base currency. Used for multi-currency payment reconciliation and financial reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the payment was received. Used for monthly financial close and reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the payment was received. Used for annual financial reporting and revenue recognition.']
  is_advance_payment boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is an advance payment received before services are rendered. True if advance, False otherwise.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  navis_billing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System billing transaction that generated the invoice. Links payment back to operational billing source.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the payment. May include remittance advice, special instructions, or reconciliation remarks.']
  payer_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number from which the payment originated. Used for payment verification and reconciliation. Business-confidential financial information.']
  payer_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution from which the payment was sent. Used for payment tracing and reconciliation.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was received or processed by the port authority. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method or instrument used to make the payment. Indicates how funds were transferred (Electronic Funds Transfer, SWIFT wire, cheque, direct debit, letter of credit, or cash).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment in the accounts receivable lifecycle. Tracks payment from receipt through clearing and application to invoices.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of payment purpose. Distinguishes between advance payments, payments on account, invoice settlements, refunds, and credit note applications.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was first received or recorded in the system. Principal business event timestamp. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was reconciled. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of payment reconciliation against bank statements and invoice records. Tracks whether payment has been matched and verified.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External payment reference number provided by the payer or payment system. Used for reconciliation and customer inquiries.']
  remittance_advice_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number from the remittance advice document sent by the payer. Links payment to payer\'s accounts payable records.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was reversed or bounced. Null if payment has not been reversed. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for payment reversal if payment status is reversed. Documents reason for chargeback, NSF (non-sufficient funds), or cancellation.']
  sap_clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI clearing document number generated when payment is applied against open invoice items. Links to SAP accounts receivable clearing process.']
  sap_payment_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI payment document number created during payment posting. Primary reference for financial audit trail in SAP S/4HANA.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount not yet allocated to invoices. Held on account for future invoice settlement or refund.']

  Note: 'Records of payments received from port customers against outstanding invoices.
