Captures payment reference number, payment date, payment method (EFT, SWIFT, cheque, direct debit, letter of credit), amount paid, currency, exchange rate, bank reference, clearing date, SAP clearing document number, payer account, payment status (pending/cleared/bounced/reversed), and allocation to invoices. SSOT for all inbound revenue cash receipts and accounts receivable clearing. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA FI payment clearing.'
}

Table billing.payment_allocation {
  payment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment allocation record. Primary key for the payment allocation entity.']
  adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.adjustment. Business justification: Payment allocations can be applied against credit adjustments (credit memos) to reduce receivables. This FK enables tracking of payments allocated to credit adjustments, completing the payment allocat']
  debit_note_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.debit_note. Business justification: Payment allocations can be applied against debit notes (additional charges). This FK enables tracking of payments allocated to debit notes, completing the payment allocation model (currently links to ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that performed the allocation. Used for audit trail and accountability in manual cash application processes.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Payment allocations may hit different GL accounts for discounts, write-offs, or foreign exchange variances. Variance accounting requires GL-level tracking of allocation adjustments for financial state']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice receiving the payment allocation. Links to the invoice record generated from NAVIS N4 billing module.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific invoice line item receiving the allocation. Nullable when allocation applies to entire invoice rather than specific line.']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment transaction being allocated. Links to the payment record in the payment processing system.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Financial period (YYYY-MM format) to which this allocation is posted. Critical for period-end close, revenue recognition, and financial reporting.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount allocated from the payment to the invoice or invoice line. Represents the portion of payment applied to reduce outstanding balance.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The date when the payment was allocated to the invoice. Critical for accounts receivable aging and financial period assignment.']
  allocation_reference varchar [note: 'Business reference number for the payment allocation transaction. Used for tracking and reconciliation purposes across systems.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'System or process that created the allocation. Manual: user-entered; Automated: system-matched; EDI: electronic data interchange; Lockbox: bank lockbox service; API: external system integration.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the allocation. Pending: awaiting confirmation; Confirmed: successfully applied; Reversed: allocation undone; Cancelled: allocation voided; Disputed: under review for billing dispute.']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the allocation was processed in the system. Used for audit trail and reconciliation timing.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation transaction. Full: entire invoice paid; Partial: portion of invoice paid; Advance: payment before invoice; Overpayment: payment exceeds invoice; On-account: unallocated payment held for future invoices; Credit-note: allocation from credit memo.']
  bank_reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number from bank statement used to match and reconcile this allocation. Critical for automated cash application and bank reconciliation processes.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Business area code for cross-company financial reporting. Enables consolidated financial statements across multiple legal entities.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity for which the allocation is recorded. Critical for multi-entity financial consolidation.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center code for internal cost allocation and profitability analysis. Links revenue collection to organizational units.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the allocation record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocated amount. Supports multi-currency payment allocation for international trade transactions.']
  discount_taken decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early payment discount amount deducted during allocation. Reflects payment terms incentives such as 2/10 net 30. Reduces allocated amount but clears full invoice value.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation is under dispute. True when customer has raised a billing dispute affecting this allocation. Impacts accounts receivable aging classification.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing dispute case if allocation is disputed. Links to dispute resolution workflow. Nullable when no dispute exists.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency exchange rate applied when payment currency differs from invoice currency. Used for multi-currency allocation and foreign exchange gain/loss calculation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the allocation belongs. Supports financial reporting and year-end close processes.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated amount converted to the company code local currency using the exchange rate. Used for financial reporting in local GAAP.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified the allocation record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the allocation record was last updated. Tracks changes for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the allocation. Used for documenting special circumstances, manual adjustments, or reconciliation details.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unpaid balance on the invoice after this allocation is applied. Zero indicates invoice is fully paid. Critical for accounts receivable aging reports.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Profit center code for segment reporting and EBITDA-level revenue analysis. Supports business unit performance measurement.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation was reversed or cancelled. Nullable for active allocations. Used for audit trail and financial period correction.']
  reversal_document varchar [note: 'SAP FI document number of the reversal transaction. Links to the offsetting entry that undoes the original allocation. Nullable for non-reversed allocations.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for reversing or cancelling the allocation. Nullable when allocation is not reversed. Examples: payment error, invoice dispute, incorrect amount, duplicate payment.']
  sap_clearing_document varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI clearing document number that links the payment and invoice in the financial system. Critical for financial close and reconciliation.']
  sap_clearing_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the SAP clearing document. Enables precise traceability to specific line-level clearing entries in SAP FI.']
  withholding_tax_deducted decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount withheld at source during payment allocation as per jurisdictional tax regulations. Common in international maritime trade transactions.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking individual payments to specific invoices or invoice lines, enabling many-to-many cash application where one payment covers multiple invoices or one invoice receives multiple partial payments. Captures allocation reference, allocation date, allocated amount, allocation type (full/partial/advance/overpayment/on-account), outstanding balance after allocation, discount taken, withholding tax deducted, SAP clearing item reference, allocation status (pending/confirmed/reversed), and reversal reason if applicable. Critical for accurate accounts receivable aging, bank reconciliation, cash application automation, and financial close in SAP S/4HANA FI.'
}

Table billing.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for adjustment']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Credit adjustments are posted to specific billing cycles for period accounting. The billing_cycle table contains fiscal_year and fiscal_period. This FK enables period-based adjustment reporting. Keeps']
  closure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.infrastructure_closure. Business justification: Credits and adjustments for infrastructure outages (berth closure, channel restriction, gate downtime) must reference the specific closure event for revenue impact analysis and SLA-based compensation ']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.dispute. Business justification: Credit adjustments are often issued to resolve customer disputes. The adjustment table has dispute_reference_number (STRING), and dispute table has reference_number. Adding dispute_id FK enables direc']
  dredging_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.dredging_campaign. Business justification: Credits issued for service disruptions during dredging (berth closures, draft restrictions) must reference the dredging campaign for cost recovery tracking and customer dispute resolution. Links reven']
  exception_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.tariff_exception. Business justification: Credit notes issued for approved tariff exceptions (waivers, special rates, free time extensions) must reference the exception record for audit trail, revenue variance analysis, and to ensure adjustme']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Credit notes post to revenue contra accounts or adjustment accounts. Revenue reversal accounting requires proper GL mapping for financial statement presentation and audit trail of billing corrections.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice being credited or adjusted. Links this credit note to the source billing document generated from NAVIS N4 billing module.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Credit adjustments often apply to specific invoice line items (not just invoice headers). This FK enables line-level credit tracking. Keeps invoice_id on adjustment as adjustments can apply to entire ']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant account receiving the credit adjustment. Identifies the customer or service recipient in the billing relationship.']
  port_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel visit if the credit note relates to vessel-based services. Enables analysis of billing adjustments by vessel operation and service delivery performance.']
  reversal_credit_note_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reversing credit note if this credit note was subsequently cancelled. Creates bidirectional link for complete reversal audit trail.']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.run. Business justification: Credit adjustments can be batch-generated during billing runs (e.g., automated credit processing). This FK enables tracking which billing run generated each adjustment, supporting batch processing and']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: Credit notes for vessel charge disputes require vessel master linkage for audit trail and dispute analysis by vessel characteristics. Supports vessel-specific billing adjustment reporting.']
  applied_date date [note: 'Date when the credit note was applied to the customer\'s accounts receivable balance. Marks the effective date of revenue reversal in financial systems.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authorized person or role who approved the credit note issuance. Provides audit trail for financial control and segregation of duties compliance.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the credit note was formally approved by authorized personnel. Critical for audit trail and financial control compliance.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading reference number if the credit note relates to cargo handling or Terminal Handling Charge (THC) adjustments. Links billing adjustment to specific cargo shipment.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO 6346 standard container identification number if the credit relates to container handling charges. Follows format AAAA1234567 with owner code and serial number.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA CO cost center responsible for the service that generated the original charge. Enables operational performance analysis by business unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credit note record was first created in the billing system. Audit trail timestamp for record creation.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the credit being issued before tax adjustments. Represents the base revenue reversal amount in the port\'s operating currency.']
  credit_note_date date [note: 'Official issue date of the credit note document. Determines the accounting period for revenue reversal and financial reporting under IFRS 15 standards.']
  credit_note_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the credit note document. Follows port billing numbering convention CN-XXXXXXXX for audit trail and customer reference.']
  credit_note_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credit note in the billing adjustment workflow. Tracks progression from draft creation through approval, application to accounts receivable, or cancellation.']
  credit_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized classification code indicating the business reason for issuing the credit note. Used for revenue adjustment analysis, dispute tracking, and service quality monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing_error|service_failure|tariff_adjustment|commercial_concession|dispute_resolution|overcharge|damaged_cargo|delayed_service|contract_amendment|goodwill — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  credit_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the specific circumstances justifying the credit adjustment. Provides business context for audit, dispute resolution, and customer communication.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit note transaction. Supports multi-currency billing operations at international port facilities.']
  customer_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether formal notification of the credit note has been sent to the customer via Port Community System or EDI. Tracks communication compliance.']
  customer_reference varchar [note: 'Customer-provided reference number or purchase order number associated with the original transaction. Facilitates customer reconciliation and accounts payable matching.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for the credit note. Enables monthly revenue adjustment tracking and variance analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the credit note was issued for financial reporting and EBITDA analysis. Supports period-based revenue adjustment reporting.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential internal comments and notes regarding the credit note decision, approval rationale, or special handling instructions. Not shared with customer.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified the credit note record. Maintains complete audit trail of changes to billing adjustments.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credit note record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to the billing adjustment for audit and change management.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when customer notification was transmitted. Provides audit trail for customer communication and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']
  original_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original billed amount from the source invoice before credit adjustment. Provides reference for calculating credit percentage and analyzing billing accuracy.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA CO profit center for revenue adjustment allocation. Supports segment reporting and EBITDA analysis by terminal or business division.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this credit note itself has been reversed or cancelled. Supports correction of erroneous credit notes and maintains complete audit trail.']
  sap_credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI credit memo document number generated upon posting to the financial accounting system. Provides integration reference between TOS billing and ERP financial records.']
  sap_posting_date date [note: 'Date when the credit memo was posted to SAP S/4HANA FI general ledger. Determines the accounting period for financial statement impact and EBITDA reporting.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the port service category for which the credit is being issued. Enables revenue adjustment analysis by service line and operational performance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vessel_services|cargo_handling|storage|equipment_rental|pilotage|towage|mooring|wharfage|terminal_handling|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Port tariff classification code for the service being credited. References the published port tariff schedule and enables tariff adjustment analysis.']
  tax_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the credit adjustment, reversing previously charged VAT, GST, or other applicable port service taxes. Ensures compliance with tax authority reporting requirements.']
  total_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit value including base credit amount and tax credit amount. Represents the full accounts receivable reduction applied to the customer account.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created the credit note record. Supports audit trail and accountability for billing adjustments.']

  Note: 'Formal credit documents issued to customers to reverse or reduce previously billed charges, arising from billing disputes, service failures, tariff adjustments, or commercial concessions. Captures credit note number, originating invoice reference, credit reason code, credit amount, tax credit amount, approval authority, approval date, credit status (draft/approved/applied/cancelled), and SAP credit memo document number. SSOT for all revenue reversals and billing adjustments in the port billing cycle.'
}

Table billing.debit_note {
  debit_note_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the debit note record. Primary key.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Debit notes are issued within billing cycles for additional charges. This FK enables cycle-based debit note tracking and period reporting. No redundant columns - debit_note dates are issuance-specific']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.dispute. Business justification: Debit notes can be disputed by customers. The debit_note table has dispute_flag and dispute_reason. Adding dispute_id FK enables direct linkage to the formal dispute record. Removes dispute_reason str']
  edi_message_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the EDI message used to transmit this debit note to the customer via Port Community System. Null if not transmitted via EDI.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Additional charges (debit notes) post to revenue GL accounts. Supplementary billing accounting requires GL attribution for revenue recognition and reconciliation with original invoice postings.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice that this debit note supplements or adjusts. Links to the base billing document.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Debit notes often reference specific invoice line items for additional charges. This FK enables line-level debit tracking. Keeps invoice_id on debit_note as debit notes can reference entire invoices o']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.tariff_item. Business justification: Debit notes for additional charges (demurrage, detention, storage escalation) must reference the tariff item that defines the rate and calculation basis. Required for customer dispute resolution and t']
  ohs_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ohs_incident. Business justification: Additional charges raised post-incident for damage, cleanup, or third-party claims discovered after initial invoicing. Standard maritime claims process requiring traceability from debit note to incide']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant account being debited. Identifies the customer or service recipient.']
  port_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel visit associated with this debit note, if applicable. Links debit to specific vessel operations.']
  primary_debit_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who approved the debit note for issuance. Null if not yet approved or if approval is not required.']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.run. Business justification: Debit notes can be batch-generated during billing runs (e.g., automated additional charge processing). This FK enables tracking which billing run generated each debit note, supporting batch processing']
  tertiary_debit_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who last modified the debit note record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: Debit notes for additional vessel charges (demurrage, extra services) require vessel master reference for charge validation and vessel account reconciliation. Critical for supplementary billing accura']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date when the customer formally acknowledged or responded to the debit note. Null if not yet acknowledged.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this debit note requires management approval before issuance. True if approval workflow is required, False if auto-approved.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the debit note was approved. Null if not yet approved or if approval is not required.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading reference number associated with the cargo or shipment related to this debit note. Links debit to specific cargo documentation.']
  charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross additional charge amount being debited to the customer before tax. Represents the base value of the supplementary billing.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO 6346 standard container identification number if the debit note relates to a specific container. Format: 4 letters (owner code) + 7 digits (serial number + check digit).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center or profit center code associated with the service that generated the debit note. Used for internal management accounting and profitability analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this debit note record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the debit note amounts. Indicates the monetary unit for all financial values.']
  customer_acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Tracks whether the customer has acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the debit note. Values: not_sent (not yet transmitted), sent (transmitted but not acknowledged), acknowledged (customer accepted), rejected (customer disputed), pending_review (under customer review).']
  debit_note_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the debit note, typically formatted as DN- followed by numeric sequence. Used for customer communication and reference.']
  debit_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for issuing the debit note. Common values: DMG (Demurrage adjustment), DET (Detention extension), THC_ADJ (Terminal Handling Charge adjustment), BAF_ADJ (Bunker Adjustment Factor correction), SURCHARGE (Additional surcharge), PORT_DUES (Supplementary port dues), LATE_FEE (Late payment fee), PENALTY (Contractual penalty). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DMG|DET|THC_ADJ|BAF_ADJ|SURCHARGE|PORT_DUES|LATE_FEE|PENALTY — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  debit_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the debit note was issued, providing context beyond the reason code. Includes specific circumstances, dates, and reference information.']
  debit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the debit note in the billing workflow. Tracks progression from draft through approval, issuance, customer acknowledgement, and potential dispute or reversal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|issued|acknowledged|disputed|cancelled|reversed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has disputed this debit note. True if disputed, False otherwise. Triggers dispute resolution workflow.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment of the debit note amount is expected from the customer. Calculated based on payment terms.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date when the debit note was formally issued to the customer. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this debit note record was last modified. Audit trail for record lifecycle and change management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, comments, or special instructions related to the debit note. Not visible to customer.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment terms applicable to this debit note (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD). Determines due date calculation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date when the debit note was posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for financial period assignment and revenue recognition.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date when the debit note was reversed or cancelled. Null if not reversed.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this debit note has been reversed or cancelled. True if reversed, False if active. Reversed debit notes do not contribute to accounts receivable.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the debit note was reversed. Null if not reversed. Documents the business justification for cancellation.']
  sap_debit_memo_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA FI debit memo document number generated when the debit note is posted to the financial system. Links billing document to accounting records.']
  service_period_end_date date [note: 'The ending date of the service period or event that triggered the additional charge. Used for calculating duration-based fees.']
  service_period_start_date date [note: 'The beginning date of the service period or event that triggered the additional charge. Used for demurrage, detention, and time-based charges.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount applicable to the additional charge. Calculated based on applicable tax rates and jurisdictional requirements.']
  total_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of the debit note including all charges and taxes. Represents the net amount to be collected from the customer.']

  Note: 'Formal debit documents issued to customers for additional charges not captured in the original invoice, including demurrage adjustments, detention extensions, surcharge corrections, or supplementary port dues. Captures debit note number, originating invoice reference, debit reason code, additional charge amount, tax amount, approval status, SAP debit memo document number, and customer acknowledgement status. Complements credit_note as the upward billing adjustment mechanism.'
}

Table billing.dispute {
  dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing dispute record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Billing disputes frequently reference contract terms, rate schedules, SLA commitments, and service scope definitions. Required for dispute resolution workflow, contractual compliance verification, and']
  assigned_handler_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee assigned to investigate and resolve the dispute. Links to workforce employee record.']
  compliance_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Disputes arise from compliance violations (overweight cargo penalties, MARPOL fines, ISPS security breaches). Dispute records reference violations to support charge justification, penalty assessment v']
  crm_service_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated service request record in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM. Links the billing dispute to the broader customer service case.']
  customs_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_hold. Business justification: Billing disputes frequently arise from customs holds causing demurrage/detention charges. Dispute resolution requires linking to the hold record to verify legitimacy of charges, hold duration, and res']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice that is being disputed. Links to the invoice record in the billing system.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Customer disputes often target specific invoice line items (not just invoice headers). This FK enables line-level dispute tracking and resolution. Keeps invoice_id on dispute as disputes can apply to ']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that raised the dispute. Links to the port community participant account.']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Customers frequently dispute security-related charges, claiming incidents were port\'s responsibility, charges excessive, or services unnecessary. Dispute investigation requires direct access to origin']
  assigned_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was assigned to an internal handler for investigation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual monetary amount credited to the customer as a result of the dispute resolution. May be equal to or less than the disputed amount.']
  credit_note_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the credit note issued if the dispute was accepted (fully or partially). Links to the credit note record in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  customer_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the customer contact who lodged the dispute. Primary channel for dispute correspondence.']
  customer_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the customer representative who lodged the dispute. Used for follow-up communications.']
  customer_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the customer contact for verbal follow-up on the dispute.']
  customer_satisfaction_rating varchar [note: 'Post-resolution satisfaction rating provided by the customer on a scale of 1-5. Used for service quality monitoring and Key Performance Indicator (KPI) reporting.']
  dispute_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the disputed charges by port service type. Enables dispute trend analysis by service line.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispute. Tracks progression from lodgement through investigation to final resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lodged|under_investigation|pending_customer_response|pending_internal_review|resolved_accepted|resolved_rejected|resolved_partial_credit|withdrawn|escalated — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value being disputed by the customer. Represents the portion of the invoice amount that the customer contests.']
  disputed_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the disputed amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was escalated to a higher management level. Null if no escalation occurred.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the management level to which the dispute has been escalated, if applicable. Used for complex or high-value disputes requiring senior review.']
  investigation_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and findings recorded by the dispute handler during the investigation process. May include references to supporting documentation, system checks, and communication history.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified the dispute record. Supports audit and accountability requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the dispute record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']
  lodged_date date [note: 'Date when the customer formally lodged the dispute against the invoice. Represents the business event timestamp for dispute initiation.']
  lodged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the dispute was lodged in the system, including time zone. Used for SLA tracking and audit purposes.']
  preventive_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any preventive or corrective actions implemented to prevent recurrence of similar disputes. Supports continuous improvement initiatives.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for the dispute. Includes port-specific charge types such as Terminal Handling Charge (THC), Demurrage (DMG), Detention (DET), and Wharfage (WHR) disputes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: incorrect_rate|wrong_quantity|service_not_rendered|duplicate_charge|tariff_misapplication|thc_dispute|demurrage_dispute|detention_dispute|wharfage_dispute|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation provided by the customer describing the nature and basis of the dispute.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique reference number for the dispute, used in customer communications and tracking. Format: DSP-YYYYNNNN.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was formally resolved and closed. Used for SLA compliance reporting and dispute lifecycle analytics.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the resolution decision, including rationale, policy references, and any corrective actions taken.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the dispute resolution was recorded in the system, including time zone.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Final outcome classification of the dispute resolution. Indicates whether the dispute was accepted (full credit), rejected (no credit), partially accepted, withdrawn, or escalated.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the billing error or dispute. Used for continuous improvement and process optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_entry_error|system_error|tariff_interpretation|customer_misunderstanding|process_failure|documentation_missing|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_actual_resolution_days varchar [note: 'Actual number of business days taken to resolve the dispute. Calculated from lodged date to resolution date for SLA compliance reporting.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the dispute resolution exceeded the SLA target resolution time. True indicates an SLA breach.']
  sla_target_resolution_days varchar [note: 'Number of business days within which the dispute should be resolved according to the applicable SLA. Used for performance tracking.']
  supporting_documents_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifiers or file paths to supporting documentation provided by the customer (e.g., Bill of Lading (BOL), Delivery Order (D/O), email correspondence).']

  Note: 'Formal records of customer-raised disputes against invoiced charges, tracking the full dispute lifecycle from lodgement through investigation to resolution. Captures dispute reference number, disputed invoice reference, dispute date, dispute reason (incorrect rate, wrong quantity, service not rendered, duplicate charge, tariff misapplication), disputed amount, customer contact, assigned dispute handler, investigation notes, resolution type (accepted/rejected/partial credit), resolution date, and credit note reference if applicable. Integrates with Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM service requests.'
}

Table billing.receivable_account {
  receivable_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the receivable account. Primary key for the customer billing and accounts receivable master record.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Each customer receivable account has a default billing cycle. The receivable_account table has billing_cycle (STRING), and billing_cycle table has cycle_code. Adding billing_cycle_id FK enables JOIN t']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this customer in the port\'s EDI network. Used for automated invoice transmission via EDIFACT or X12 protocols.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for managing this customer relationship, credit decisions, and collections activities.']
  haulier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.haulier. Business justification: Hauliers are billed customers for drayage and gate services. Ports maintain receivable accounts for hauliers to track outstanding balances, credit limits, payment terms, and aging. Essential for credi']
  parent_account_receivable_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent receivable account if this is a subsidiary or branch account. Enables hierarchical billing and consolidated statements.']
  rail_operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.rail_operator. Business justification: Rail operators are billed for terminal services (loading, unloading, storage, track usage). Ports maintain receivable accounts to manage rail operator billing, credit terms, payment tracking, and dunn']
  account_classification varchar [note: 'Business classification of the account holder based on their role in the port community ecosystem. Determines applicable tariff structures and service entitlements.']
  account_closed_date date [note: 'Date when this receivable account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.']
  account_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique alphanumeric code identifying the receivable account. Used for operational reference and reporting.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trading name of the customer organization or individual holding this receivable account.']
  account_opened_date date [note: 'Date when this receivable account was first established and activated in the billing system.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receivable account. Active accounts can transact; suspended/blocked accounts require approval; closed accounts are archived.']
  aging_bucket_0_30_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding balance for invoices aged 0 to 30 days past due date. Used for accounts receivable aging analysis and collections prioritization.']
  aging_bucket_31_60_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding balance for invoices aged 31 to 60 days past due date. Indicates moderate collection risk.']
  aging_bucket_61_90_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding balance for invoices aged 61 to 90 days past due date. Indicates elevated collection risk requiring escalation.']
  aging_bucket_over_90_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding balance for invoices aged over 90 days past due date. Indicates high collection risk; may require legal action or write-off consideration.']
  auto_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled in automatic payment via direct debit or standing order. True means invoices are auto-settled on due date.']
  average_days_to_pay decimal(5,1) [note: 'Rolling average number of days from invoice date to payment receipt over the last 12 months. Key metric for credit risk assessment.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the postal address to which invoices and statements are sent. Typically contains street number and name.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing postal address. Used for suite/floor/building details or additional address information.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality component of the billing address.']
  billing_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the billing address country.']
  billing_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for sending invoices, statements, and billing-related communications.']
  billing_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for billing inquiries and dispute resolution. Format varies by country.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code component of the billing address. Format varies by country.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region component of the billing address. Format varies by country.']
  consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether invoices for this account should be consolidated with parent account for statement purposes. True enables group-level billing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this receivable account record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is currently on credit hold. True means no new services can be rendered until outstanding balance is reduced.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding balance allowed for this account before credit hold is triggered. Expressed in the account\'s base currency.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal or external credit rating assessment for this account. Used to determine credit limits and payment terms. D indicates default risk. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D|unrated — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_count_ytd varchar [note: 'Number of billing disputes raised by this account in the current calendar year. High counts indicate service quality or billing accuracy issues.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current collections escalation level (0=none, 1=reminder, 2=warning, 3=final notice, 4=legal action). Increments with each overdue period.']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for delivering invoices to the customer. EDI is common for large shipping lines; email for smaller customers.']
  last_invoice_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent invoice issued to this account. Used to identify dormant accounts.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent payment received from this account in the account\'s base currency.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received from this account. Used to calculate average days to pay and payment behavior trends.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this receivable account record was last updated. Updated automatically on any field change.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total amount owed by the customer across all unpaid invoices. Updated in real-time as invoices are issued and payments are received.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms defining the number of days from invoice date until payment is due. Net 7/14/30/60 are common port industry terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_7|net_14|net_30|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|cod — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  preferred_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for invoicing this account. All invoices will be issued in this currency unless overridden at transaction level.']
  sap_customer_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA customer master number (KNA1/KNB1) linking this receivable account to the ERP financial accounting module. Ten-digit numeric identifier.']
  statement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which account statements summarizing all invoices and payments are generated and sent to the customer.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary tax jurisdiction for this account. Determines applicable tax rates and compliance requirements.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'Tax registration identifier for VAT/GST purposes. Required for invoicing and tax compliance reporting. Format varies by jurisdiction.']
  write_off_amount_ytd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount written off as bad debt for this account in the current calendar year. Impacts credit rating and future credit decisions.']

  Note: 'The single authoritative customer billing and accounts receivable master for each port community participant — THE unified customer record for all billing, credit management, and collections operations in this domain. Combines billing configuration (invoice delivery method, EDI partner ID, preferred currency, payment terms net 7/14/30/60, billing cycle vessel-call/weekly/monthly, account manager, billing address) with financial position tracking (credit limit, outstanding balance, aging buckets 0-30/31-60/61-90/90+ days, dunning level, credit hold flag, average days to pay). Captures account code, SAP customer account number (KNA1/KNB1), account name, VAT/tax registration, account classification (shipping line/freight forwarder/cargo owner/terminal operator/government), account status (active/suspended/blocked/closed), last payment date, credit rating, and EDI partner configuration. SSOT for customer identity, billing preferences, credit exposure, AR aging, and settlement terms. All invoices, payments, disputes, adjustments, dunning notices, and statements reference this single master.'
}

Table billing.dunning_notice {
  dunning_notice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dunning notice record. Primary key.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Dunning notices are generated per billing cycle for overdue accounts. This FK enables cycle-based dunning analysis and escalation tracking. No redundant columns - dunning_date is the notice issuance d']
  dunning_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch dunning run that generated this notice, allowing grouping of notices issued in the same collections cycle.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal collections staff member or relationship manager responsible for managing this dunning case.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that has outstanding receivables and is subject to this dunning notice.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the customer contact person for dunning correspondence.']
  contact_person_name varchar [note: 'Name of the customer contact person to whom the dunning notice was addressed, typically the accounts payable manager or financial controller.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the customer contact person for follow-up on the dunning notice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dunning notice record was first created in the system.']
  credit_block_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account has been placed on credit hold due to overdue receivables, preventing further service provisioning until payment is received.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The approved credit limit for the customer account at the time of dunning notice issuance, used to assess credit risk exposure.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the overdue amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_response_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer provided a response to the dunning notice, if applicable.']
  customer_response_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether and how the customer has responded to the dunning notice.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of days since the oldest invoice payment due date, calculated as the aging period for the most delinquent receivable.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Customer-provided reason for disputing the overdue invoices, if a dispute has been raised in response to the dunning notice.']
  dunning_date date [note: 'The date on which the dunning notice was issued to the customer. This is the principal business event timestamp for the notice.']
  dunning_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Administrative fee charged to the customer for issuing the dunning notice, as per port tariff schedule and credit policy.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Escalation level of the dunning notice (1=first reminder, 2=second reminder, 3=final notice, 4=legal action). Higher levels indicate increased severity and urgency.']
  dunning_method varchar [note: 'The communication channel and delivery method used to send the dunning notice to the customer.']
  dunning_notice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the dunning notice, used in customer correspondence and legal proceedings.']
  dunning_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dunning notice indicating its processing state and customer response.']
  dunning_text varchar [note: 'The body text or message content of the dunning notice sent to the customer, including payment instructions and consequences of non-payment.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date on which the dunning notice was escalated to the next level or to external collections, if applicable.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dunning notice has been escalated to legal or external collections agencies due to non-response or non-payment.']
  external_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external collections agency to which the account has been referred, if escalation has occurred.']
  interest_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Late payment interest accrued on the overdue receivables, calculated per the credit agreement terms.']
  legal_action_threatened_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dunning notice includes a threat of legal action or litigation if payment is not received by the deadline.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments from collections staff regarding customer interactions, payment promises, or special circumstances related to this dunning case.']
  number_of_overdue_invoices varchar [note: 'Count of distinct invoices that are overdue and included in this dunning notice.']
  oldest_overdue_invoice_date date [note: 'The invoice date of the oldest unpaid invoice included in this dunning notice, indicating how long receivables have been outstanding.']
  payment_plan_offered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a payment plan or installment arrangement has been offered to the customer as part of the dunning resolution process.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The original payment terms applicable to the overdue invoices (e.g., Net 30, Net 60), providing context for the delinquency.']
  response_deadline_date date [note: 'The date by which the customer must respond or settle the outstanding amount to avoid further escalation.']
  sap_dunning_document_reference varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA FI dunning document number that corresponds to this dunning notice, providing traceability to the source ERP system.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The negotiated settlement amount if the customer and port have agreed to a reduced payment to resolve the overdue receivables.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which the overdue receivables were settled or paid in full, closing the dunning case.']
  total_overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of all outstanding receivables included in this dunning notice, representing the sum of all overdue invoices.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dunning notice record was last modified, tracking the most recent change to status or customer response.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'The date on which the overdue receivables were written off, if applicable.']
  write_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the overdue receivables have been written off as uncollectible bad debt.']

  Note: 'Formal overdue payment reminder notices issued to customers with outstanding receivables, escalating through configurable dunning levels. Captures dunning notice number, customer account reference, dunning level (1-4), dunning date, total overdue amount, oldest overdue invoice date, dunning method (email/letter/legal), response deadline, customer response status, escalation flag, and SAP dunning document reference. Supports systematic collections management and credit risk mitigation for port revenue.'
}

Table billing.revenue_event {
  revenue_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the revenue recognition event. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial contract or service level agreement (SLA) under which the revenue is recognized. Applicable for long-term agreements with shipping lines, terminal operators, or freight forwarders.']
  berth_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the berth or wharf where the revenue-generating service was delivered. Used for berth-level revenue analysis and capacity planning.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Revenue recognition events are tied to billing cycles for IFRS 15 period accounting. The billing_cycle table contains fiscal_year, fiscal_period, revenue_recognition_date. This FK enables period-based']
  charge_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.charge_event. Business justification: Revenue events recognize revenue from underlying charge events in accordance with IFRS 15. Each revenue recognition event should reference the specific charge event(s) it recognizes. This link is crit']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Revenue events tied to specific operational cost centers for performance measurement. Earned revenue by terminal enables matching revenue recognition with operational cost incurrence for margin analys']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Business justification: Revenue recognition for customs-related services requires linking to declarations for compliance reporting and revenue stream analysis by cargo type. Supports regulatory reporting on customs service r']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finance user or manager who approved the revenue recognition event. Used for audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue recognition events directly drive GL postings under IFRS 15. Accrual-basis revenue accounting requires linking performance obligations to specific revenue GL accounts for compliance and financ']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice that triggered this revenue recognition event. Links to the billing invoice generated in NAVIS N4 or SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.tariff_item. Business justification: Revenue recognition events must link to the tariff item that defines the service and rate for IFRS 15 compliance, performance obligation tracking, and revenue stream categorization. Essential for fina']
  marine_service_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying service order or work order that generated the revenue. May link to vessel services, terminal handling, or other port operations.']
  original_revenue_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original revenue event that is being reversed or adjusted. Populated only when reversal_flag is True. Enables audit trail for revenue corrections.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant account (customer, shipping line, freight forwarder, etc.) for whom revenue is being recognized.']
  performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific performance obligation within a multi-element contract, as defined by IFRS 15. Used to track revenue recognition for distinct service components within a bundled agreement. Format: PO- followed by 8 digits.']
  port_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel visit or call that generated the revenue. Links revenue events to specific vessel operations (berthing, cargo operations, pilotage, towage).']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Revenue recognition by profit center for segment reporting under IFRS 8. Divisional revenue recognition enables separate financial performance tracking for container, bulk, and passenger operations.']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.service_code. Business justification: Revenue recognition events must link to service catalog for correct GL account posting and revenue stream classification. Essential for financial accounting and revenue reporting compliance.']
  sustainability_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.sustainability_initiative. Business justification: Revenue recognition for green tariff premiums, carbon offset programs, shore power incentives, and sustainability-linked service fees. Emerging port revenue model (WPSP-aligned) requiring traceability']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the terminal or facility where the revenue-generating activity occurred. Supports multi-terminal port operations and terminal-level profitability reporting.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (YYYY-MM format) to which this revenue recognition event is allocated. Used for period-end financial close and EBITDA reporting.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustment applied to the base revenue amount, such as discounts, surcharges (BAF, CAF), rebates, or contractual adjustments. Positive values represent surcharges; negative values represent discounts or credits.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of the reason for any revenue adjustment (e.g., volume discount applied, BAF surcharge for fuel cost, contractual rebate, billing correction, dispute resolution credit).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the revenue recognition event. Pending approval indicates awaiting finance manager review; Approved indicates cleared for GL posting; Rejected indicates the event was declined and must be corrected.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the revenue recognition event was approved for GL posting. ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this revenue event record was first created in the system. ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the recognized and deferred revenue amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency port operations.']
  deferred_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue deferred to future accounting periods. Applicable for services delivered over time (e.g., storage, lease agreements, long-term contracts).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (YYYY) to which this revenue recognition event belongs. Supports multi-year financial analysis and annual reporting.']
  gl_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA FI document number assigned to the general ledger posting for this revenue recognition event. Used for financial audit and reconciliation. 10-digit numeric format.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the revenue recognition event was posted to the SAP S/4HANA FI general ledger. May differ from recognition_date due to period-end close timing or batch processing.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this revenue event record was last modified. ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, explanations, or audit notes related to the revenue recognition event. Used for documenting special circumstances, manual adjustments, or compliance annotations.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'The quantity of service units delivered that generated the revenue (e.g., number of TEUs handled, number of pilotage hours, number of storage days, tonnage moved). Unit of measure is context-dependent on service type.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue is recognized in the accounting system. This is the business event date for revenue realization, distinct from invoice date or service delivery date.']
  recognition_event_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the revenue recognition event, formatted as REV- followed by 10 digits. Used for audit trails and financial reporting.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'The method used to recognize revenue for this event. Point-in-time applies to discrete services (THC, pilotage, towage); Over-time applies to continuous services (storage, lease); Milestone applies to project-based services; Proportional applies to usage-based services; Straight-line applies to subscription or fixed-term contracts.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the revenue recognition event. Pending indicates awaiting approval; Recognized indicates revenue has been posted to GL; Deferred indicates revenue is held for future periods; Reversed indicates a correction or cancellation; Adjusted indicates a modification post-recognition; Cancelled indicates the event was voided.']
  recognized_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue recognized in this event, posted to the general ledger. Represents the portion of the invoice or service order value that is realized in the current accounting period.']
  revenue_stream_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the revenue stream for strategic reporting and EBITDA analysis. Vessel services include pilotage, towage, berth fees; Cargo handling includes THC, stevedoring; Storage/demurrage includes container yard charges; Marine services include launch, survey; Facility lease includes terminal, warehouse rental; Ancillary services include utilities, security.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this revenue event is a reversal or correction of a previously recognized revenue event. True indicates a reversal; False indicates a normal recognition event.']
  service_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the underlying port service was delivered or completed (e.g., vessel departure, container discharge completion, pilotage service completion). Used to determine point-in-time vs over-time recognition.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this revenue event originated. NAVIS_N4 = NAVIS N4 Billing Module; SAP_S4HANA = SAP S/4HANA FI; MANUAL_ENTRY = Manual finance entry; PCS = Port Community System; TOPS = TOPS Expert TOS.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the transaction in the source system (e.g., NAVIS N4 billing transaction ID, SAP FI document number). Used for data lineage and reconciliation back to the source system.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the service quantity. TEU = Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit; FEU = Forty-foot Equivalent Unit; TON = Metric ton; CBM = Cubic meter; HOUR = Service hour; DAY = Calendar day; MOVE = Container move; UNIT = Generic unit count; KG = Kilogram; MT = Metric tonne. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TEU|FEU|TON|CBM|HOUR|DAY|MOVE|UNIT|KG|MT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'The rate per unit of measure applied to calculate the revenue amount. Represents the tariff or contracted price per TEU, per hour, per day, etc.']

  Note: 'Records the recognition of port service revenue against accounting periods in accordance with IFRS 15 / port revenue recognition policies. Captures recognition event date, invoice or service order reference, recognized revenue amount, deferred revenue amount, recognition method (point-in-time for THC/pilotage vs over-time for storage/lease), accounting period, SAP revenue account code, cost centre, profit centre, and recognition status. Enables EBITDA-level revenue reporting and period-end financial close in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.'
}

Table billing.run {
  run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing run batch execution record. Primary key.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Each billing run executes for a specific billing cycle. The billing_cycle table defines the authoritative billing period. Adding billing_cycle_id allows JOIN to retrieve cycle configuration and remove']
  rerun_of_billing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original billing run ID if this run is a rerun or correction of a previous failed or cancelled run. Null for original runs.']
  run_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the finance manager or authorized approver who approved the billing run for release. Null if approval is not required or not yet granted.']
  run_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that initiated or executed the billing run. For scheduled runs, this may be a system service account; for ad-hoc runs, this is the manual operator.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing run requires manual approval before invoices are released to customers. True for high-value or adjustment runs; false for routine periodic runs.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing run was approved for invoice release. Null if approval is not required or not yet granted.']
  charge_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system from which billing charges were extracted: NAVIS-N4 (primary TOS), TOPS-EXPERT (alternate TOS), MANUAL (manual entry), or PCS (Port Community System for customs/trade charges).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this billing run record was first created in the system. Represents the initial scheduling or setup of the billing run.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total invoice value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All invoices in a single run use the same base currency.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from run start to run end. Used for performance monitoring and capacity planning of billing batch jobs.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing run execution completed or terminated. Null if run is still in-progress or suspended.']
  error_count varchar [note: 'Number of errors or exceptions encountered during billing run execution. Zero indicates a clean run; non-zero requires review of error logs.']
  error_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary description of errors encountered during the billing run. Null if no errors occurred. Detailed error logs are maintained separately.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing run revenue was posted to the General Ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. Null if GL posting has not yet occurred.']
  invoice_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when invoices from this billing run were released and made available to customers via EDI, email, or customer portal. Null if not yet released.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this billing run record was last updated. Tracks any changes to status, approval, or other attributes throughout the run lifecycle.']
  navis_export_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the NAVIS N4 TOS charge export batch that sourced the billing data for this run. Provides traceability back to the Terminal Operating System.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by billing operators regarding special circumstances, manual adjustments, or issues encountered during the billing run execution.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for the billing run, formatted as BR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for traceability and audit purposes.']
  revenue_recognition_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period (YYYY-MM format) to which revenue from this billing run is recognized for financial reporting and EBITDA calculation purposes.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing run: scheduled (queued for execution), in-progress (actively processing), completed (successfully finished), failed (error encountered), cancelled (manually aborted), or suspended (paused pending resolution).']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing run execution type: periodic (scheduled monthly/weekly), vessel-call (per vessel departure), ad-hoc (manual trigger), final (end-of-period close), adjustment (correction run), or reversal (cancellation run).']
  sap_posting_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the SAP S/4HANA FI posting batch where generated invoices were posted for financial accounting. Null if posting has not yet occurred or failed.']
  scheduled_run_date date [note: 'Date on which this billing run was originally scheduled to execute. May differ from actual run start date if the run was delayed or rescheduled.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing run execution began processing charges from NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System (TOS). Represents the actual start of the batch job.']
  total_charge_lines_processed varchar [note: 'Total number of individual charge line items processed and aggregated into invoices during this billing run.']
  total_customers_billed varchar [note: 'Distinct count of customer accounts that received at least one invoice in this billing run.']
  total_invoice_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all invoices generated in this billing run, representing gross revenue before adjustments. Used for EBITDA-level revenue reporting.']
  total_invoices_generated varchar [note: 'Total number of invoices successfully generated and created during this billing run execution.']

  Note: 'Batch billing execution records capturing each scheduled or ad-hoc billing cycle run that generates invoices from NAVIS N4 TOS charge data. Captures billing run reference, run type (periodic/vessel-call/ad-hoc/final), billing period start and end dates, run date and time, total invoices generated, total invoice value, currency, run status (scheduled/in-progress/completed/failed/cancelled), operator ID, NAVIS N4 export batch reference, and SAP posting batch reference. Provides full audit trail of invoice generation cycles.'
}

Table billing.charge_event {
  charge_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the charge event record. Primary key for the charge event entity.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Charge approval is a critical authorization control point. Port operations require tracking which supervisor/manager approved each charge event for audit compliance and dispute resolution.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Charge events are accumulated and billed within specific billing cycles. This FK enables period-based charge aggregation and billing cycle reporting. No redundant columns to remove - charge_event.even']
  charge_call_id varchar [note: 'FK to vessel.call.call_id — Revenue reconciliation requires joining every chargeable event to the vessel call that triggered it. Without this FK, billing disputes cannot be correlated to vessel visits — a daily operational requi']
  charge_vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel call during which this charge event occurred. Links to the vessel operations domain.']
  commodity_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.commodity_code. Business justification: Charge events for cargo handling require commodity code linkage for hazmat handling fees, special storage charges, and regulatory compliance. Essential for cargo service billing rules.']
  compliance_hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.hs_code. Business justification: Charge calculation for cargo services depends on commodity classification (dangerous goods handling, CITES-regulated cargo, dual-use items). Charge events reference HS codes for rate determination and']
  contractor_safety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.contractor_safety.   customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Essential for real-time billing of customs services and fo']
  customs_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_hold. Business justification: Charge events for hold-related fees (extended storage during hold, inspection costs, examination fees) directly reference the customs hold. Required for real-time billing of hold-related services and ']
  drayage_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.drayage_order. Business justification: Drayage services generate billable charge events.
