  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice that includes this charge event, if the event has been billed. Null for unbilled events.']
  leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.intermodal_leg. Business justification: Each intermodal leg (rail/truck segment) generates handling and transport charges. Ports track which charges apply to which leg for multi-modal billing, cost allocation across transport modes, and rev']
  masterdata_container_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.container_type. Business justification: Charge events for container services require container type classification for accurate tariff lookup and TEU conversion. Essential for container terminal billing automation.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant account (customer, shipping line, freight forwarder, etc.) to whom this charge event will be billed.']
  performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.performance_obligation. Business justification: Each charge event satisfies a specific IFRS 15 performance obligation. The performance_obligation table defines revenue_recognition_method, satisfaction_criteria, and revenue account codes. This FK en']
  permit_to_work_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.permit_to_work. Business justification: Hot work permits, confined space entry, and other permitted activities often carry service charges (permit issuance fees, safety supervision charges, gas testing fees). Real port revenue stream requir']
  port_gate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port_gate. Business justification: Gate transaction charges (truck processing fees, RFID/OCR service charges) must reference which gate processed the transaction for gate-level revenue analysis and throughput-based billing validation. ']
  rail_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.rail_visit. Business justification: Rail terminal handling generates billable events (loading, unloading, storage). Linking charge events to rail visits enables service verification, supports billing accuracy audits, and facilitates dis']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.rate_schedule. Business justification: Charge events (container handling, berth occupancy, pilotage) are priced per contracted rate schedules. Required for real-time tariff application, billing accuracy, and automated charge calculation in']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_run. Business justification: Charge events are processed and aggregated by billing runs. Each charge event should reference which billing run processed it for traceability and reconciliation. This link enables tracking which char']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Charge events for sanctioned vessels/parties must be screened before billing. Real-time screening at charge capture prevents service provision to sanctioned entities and creates audit trail for compli']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Security incidents trigger immediate charge events: emergency security deployment, additional screening requirements, incident response team costs, enhanced surveillance. Charge events must reference ']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific port service rendered that generated this charge event (e.g., container move, gate transaction, crane hire, reefer connection, hazardous handling).']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.shipment. Business justification: Charge events capture billable activities; linking to shipment enables direct cargo-to-charge traceability for freight charges, surcharges, and ancillary fees. Supports charge validation against shipm']
  terminal_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the port equipment (crane, RTG, forklift, etc.) used to perform the service that generated this charge event.']
  trade_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_document. Business justification: Charge events for document processing fees (certificate issuance, document verification, legalization services) reference specific trade documents. Essential for real-time billing of documentary servi']
  truck_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.truck_visit. Business justification: Gate transactions and truck handling generate charges (gate fees, weighbridge, inspection). Linking charge_event to truck_visit enables automated billing from gate systems, supports haulier invoicing,']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique seven-digit IMO number assigned to the vessel associated with this charge event, used for statutory charge calculations.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this charge event was approved for billing by an authorized user or automated workflow.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current billing status of the charge event indicating whether it has been invoiced, is pending billing, has been disputed, waived, cancelled, or reversed.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number associated with the cargo related to this charge event, if applicable.']
  cargo_volume_cbm decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of the cargo in cubic meters associated with this charge event, used for volume-based wharfage calculations.']
  cargo_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Weight of the cargo in kilograms associated with this charge event, used for wharfage and weight-based charge calculations.']
  charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated charge amount for this event before any adjustments, discounts, or taxes. Represents the base charge value.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO 6346 standard container identification number associated with this charge event, if applicable. Null for non-container charges.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this charge event record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the charge amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  daily_rate decimal(15,4) [note: 'Daily rate applied for demurrage or detention charges per excess day.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Discount amount applied to this charge event based on customer agreements or promotional tariffs.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge event is currently under dispute by the customer.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description or code indicating the reason for the billing dispute, if the dispute flag is true.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the chargeable service event occurred in the operational system. This is the business event time, not the record creation time.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the charge event indicating whether it is a commercial service charge, statutory/regulatory charge, demurrage, detention, wharfage, or port dues.']
  excess_days varchar [note: 'Number of days beyond the free time period for which demurrage or detention charges are calculated.']
  exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge event is exempt from billing due to contractual agreements, regulatory exemptions, or special circumstances.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or code indicating the reason for charge exemption, if the exemption flag is true.']
  free_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of free days allowed before demurrage or detention charges begin to accrue, as per tariff or customer agreement.']
  free_time_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the free time period ends and chargeable demurrage or detention time begins.']
  free_time_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the free time period begins for demurrage or detention calculation.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge event is related to hazardous cargo handling, which may attract additional charges per IMDG Code requirements.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG classification code for hazardous cargo associated with this charge event, if applicable.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this charge event record was last modified. Audit trail field.']
  net_charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net charge amount after applying discounts and adding taxes. This is the final billable amount for this charge event.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of the service unit rendered (e.g., number of moves, number of days, number of connections).']
  reefer_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge event is related to a refrigerated (reefer) container requiring power connection and monitoring services.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the charge event, used for tracking and reconciliation across systems.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system that originated this charge event record (e.g., NAVIS N4, VTMS, PCS, TOPS, SAP S/4HANA, or MANUAL entry).']
  source_system_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or reference key from the source operational system that generated this charge event, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  statutory_authority_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the statutory authority, regulation, or legal framework under which statutory charges (wharfage, port dues, pilotage levies, navigation fees) are assessed.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Applicable tariff code from the port tariff schedule used to calculate the charge amount for this event.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax amount applied to this charge event based on applicable tax regulations and jurisdictions.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the service quantity (e.g., move, day, hour, TEU, FEU, ton, CBM, connection, lift, each). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: move|day|hour|teu|feu|ton|cbm|connection|lift|each — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_rate decimal(15,4) [note: 'Rate per unit of measure applied from the tariff schedule to calculate the charge amount.']
  vessel_grt decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross Registered Tonnage of the vessel, used as a basis for calculating port dues and other vessel-based statutory charges.']
  vessel_nrt decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net Registered Tonnage of the vessel, used for certain statutory charge calculations and regulatory reporting.']

  Note: 'Granular chargeable service events captured from NAVIS N4 TOS and other operational systems, representing all raw billable activities before invoice consolidation. Covers commercial charges (container moves, gate transactions, crane hire, reefer connections, hazardous handling, storage days) and statutory/regulatory charges (wharfage assessments based on cargo weight/volume, port dues based on vessel GRT/NRT, pilotage levies, navigation fees). Also captures demurrage and detention charge calculations including free time start/end, excess days, and daily rates. Captures charge event reference, event type, event timestamp, container number, vessel call ID, vessel IMO/GRT/NRT, BOL number, cargo weight/volume, equipment ID, quantity, unit of measure, applicable tariff code, calculated charge amount, currency, billing status (unbilled/billed/disputed/waived), exemption flag, free time days allowed, excess days, statutory authority reference, and source system reference. SSOT for all pre-invoice chargeable activity including commercial, statutory, and demurrage/detention charges.'
}

Table billing.proforma_invoice {
  proforma_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the proforma invoice record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Proforma invoices for vessel calls reference service agreements to pre-calculate port charges based on contracted rates, volume commitments, and SLA terms before vessel arrival. Essential for advance ']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Proforma invoices are preliminary estimates issued within billing cycles. This FK enables cycle-based proforma tracking and conversion analysis. No redundant columns - proforma dates (issue_date, vali']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Proforma invoice preparation requires tracking the billing clerk for customer queries, accuracy accountability, and workload management in port billing operations.']
  final_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the final invoice generated upon conversion of this proforma invoice. Null if not yet converted.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipping line, agent, or customer account to whom this proforma invoice is issued.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Proforma invoices for negotiated-rate customers must reference the applicable rate card to ensure estimated charges reflect contracted rates, not published tariff. Critical for vessel operators to val']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.run. Business justification: Proforma invoices can be batch-generated by billing runs. This FK enables tracking which billing run generated each proforma invoice, supporting batch processing and audit trails.']
  shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: Proforma invoices are typically issued to shipping lines for advance billing of expected vessel call charges. Essential for shipping line credit management and pre-arrival billing.']
  slot_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.slot_booking. Business justification: Slot bookings for rail/truck services trigger proforma invoices before service delivery. Standard practice in intermodal terminal operations for advance billing, deposit collection, and service confir']
  vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel call for which this proforma invoice was issued. Links to the vessel operations domain.']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique seven-digit IMO ship identification number assigned to the vessel. Denormalized for quick reference.']
  acknowledged_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the customer representative who acknowledged the proforma invoice.']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer acknowledged the proforma invoice. Null if not yet acknowledged.']
  acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the customer has acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the proforma invoice. Used for pre-clearance and financial planning.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional adjustments applied to the proforma invoice, such as Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF), Currency Adjustment Factor (CAF), or other surcharges.']
  berth_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the berth allocated to the vessel for this call. Used for berth-specific tariff calculation.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the billing contact person for proforma invoice delivery and correspondence.']
  billing_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary billing contact person at the customer organization to whom the proforma invoice is addressed.']
  billing_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the billing contact person for proforma invoice inquiries and clarifications.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date on which the proforma invoice was cancelled. Null if not cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for cancellation of the proforma invoice, such as vessel call cancellation, customer request, or billing error. Null if not cancelled.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'Date on which the proforma invoice was converted to a final invoice. Null if not yet converted.']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the proforma invoice has been converted to a final invoice, expired without conversion, or cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proforma invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the proforma invoice amounts are denominated.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the proforma invoice was delivered to the customer. Options include email, Electronic Data Interchange (EDI), customer portal, or printed copy.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proforma invoice was delivered to the customer via the specified delivery method.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the proforma invoice, including volume discounts, promotional discounts, or contractual rebates.']
  eta timestamp [note: 'Estimated time of arrival of the vessel at the port. Used to calculate time-based charges such as berth occupancy and pilotage.']
  etd timestamp [note: 'Estimated time of departure of the vessel from the port. Used to calculate duration-based charges.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the proforma invoice was generated and issued to the customer.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified the proforma invoice record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proforma invoice record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the proforma invoice, including special instructions, clarifications, or customer communications.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms applicable to the proforma invoice, such as net 30 days, advance payment required, or credit terms per Service Level Agreement (SLA).']
  proforma_invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the proforma invoice. Tracks progression from draft through issuance, customer acknowledgement, conversion to final invoice, expiration, or cancellation.']
  proforma_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique proforma invoice number used for customer reference and tracking. Typically follows port-specific numbering convention.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Primary service category for which charges are estimated. Includes vessel dues, berth charges, pilotage, towage, Terminal Handling Charge (THC), and wharfage.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated charges before taxes, adjustments, and discounts. Sum of all line item charges.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated tax amount applicable to the proforma invoice, including VAT, GST, or other applicable port service taxes.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final estimated total amount payable on the proforma invoice after all taxes, discounts, and adjustments. This is the amount the customer is expected to pay.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of the proforma invoice validity period. Charges are estimated as of this date.']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the proforma invoice. After this date, the proforma is no longer valid and must be reissued or converted to final invoice.']

  Note: 'Preliminary invoice estimates issued to shipping lines and agents prior to vessel departure or cargo release, providing advance notice of expected charges for vessel calls, THC, and port dues. Captures proforma number, vessel call reference, estimated charges by service category, validity period, customer acknowledgement status, conversion status (converted/expired/cancelled), and final invoice reference upon conversion. Supports pre-clearance financial planning for shipping agents and port revenue forecasting.'
}

Table billing.statement_of_account {
  statement_of_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the statement of account record. Primary key.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Statements of account are generated per billing cycle. The billing_cycle table contains the authoritative period dates and fiscal period information. Adding billing_cycle_id normalizes period tracking']
  edi_message_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the EDI message used to transmit the statement electronically to the customer, if EDI delivery method was used.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The system user identifier of the person or automated process that created the statement record.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The system user identifier of the person or automated process that last modified the statement record.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receivable account for which this statement is issued. Links to the billing account master.']
  prior_statement_of_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on statement_of_account (prior_statement_of_account_id)']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing run batch process during which this statement was generated as part of the month-end or period-end billing close.']
  acknowledged_by_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the customer contact person who acknowledged the statement on behalf of the customer organization.']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer formally acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the statement.']
  acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the statement, raised a dispute, or has not responded.']
  aging_31_60_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are overdue by 31-60 days from their due date.']
  aging_61_90_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are overdue by 61-90 days from their due date.']
  aging_current decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are current or overdue by 0-30 days from their due date.']
  aging_over_90_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are overdue by more than 90 days from their due date. Indicates high collection risk.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the primary billing contact person at the customer organization.']
  billing_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary billing contact person at the customer organization to whom the statement is addressed.']
  billing_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number of the primary billing contact person at the customer organization.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date on which the statement was cancelled, if applicable.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for cancelling the statement, if the statement status is cancelled. Used for audit and process improvement.']
  closing_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding accounts receivable balance at the end of the statement period. Calculated as opening balance plus invoices and debit adjustments minus payments and credit adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the statement record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  credit_adjustment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of credit adjustment transactions applied to the customer account during the statement period.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary amounts on this statement are denominated.']
  debit_adjustment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of debit adjustment transactions applied to the customer account during the statement period.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The channel through which the statement was delivered to the customer. Aligns with customer communication preferences.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the statement was successfully delivered to the customer through the specified delivery method.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has raised a dispute or discrepancy regarding the statement contents or balances.']
  dispute_reference_number varchar [note: 'The unique reference number of the billing dispute case raised by the customer in relation to this statement, if applicable.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which the statement period falls.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the statement period falls, used for financial reporting and period alignment.']
  invoice_count varchar [note: 'The total number of invoices issued to the customer during the statement period.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the statement record was last modified in the system. Audit trail for record updates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional remarks, special instructions, or contextual information related to the statement.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding accounts receivable balance at the beginning of the statement period. Represents carried-forward balance from prior period.']
  payment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of payment transactions received from the customer during the statement period.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA FI document number associated with the statement generation or posting transaction in the financial system.']
  statement_date date [note: 'The date on which the statement was generated and issued to the customer. Represents the as-of date for all balances and transactions included.']
  statement_frequency varchar [note: 'The periodic cadence at which this statement is generated for the customer account. Aligns with billing cycle configuration.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique statement reference number issued to the customer. Format: SOA-YYYYMMDD-NNNNNN.']
  statement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the statement. Tracks progression from draft through issuance, customer acknowledgement, and closure.']
  total_credit_adjustments decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate value of all credit notes, credit memos, and favorable adjustments applied to the customer account during the statement period.']
  total_debit_adjustments decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate value of all debit notes, debit memos, and unfavorable adjustments applied to the customer account during the statement period.']
  total_invoices_raised decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate value of all invoices issued to the customer during the statement period. Increases the receivable balance.']
  total_payments_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate value of all payments received from the customer during the statement period. Reduces the receivable balance.']

  Note: 'Periodic accounts receivable statement documents issued to port customers summarizing all billing activity (invoices raised, payments received, credit/debit memos applied, outstanding balance) for a defined statement period. Captures statement number, customer account reference, statement date, statement period (monthly/quarterly), opening balance, total invoices raised, total payments received, total adjustments, closing balance, aging summary (current/30/60/90/90+ days), statement delivery method, and customer acknowledgement. Supports regulatory requirements for periodic AR disclosure, customer reconciliation, and dispute prevention. Generated as part of month-end billing close process.'
}

Table billing.performance_obligation {
  performance_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for performance_obligation']
  parent_performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on performance_obligation (parent_performance_obligation_id)']
  superseded_by_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the performance obligation that supersedes this one when retired or deprecated. Null if current version or not superseded.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether special approval is required before this performance obligation can be included in customer contracts. True if approval required, False if standard.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard billing frequency for this performance obligation: immediate (upon service), daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annually, milestone-based, or upon completion.']
  constraint_estimation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to estimate variable consideration constraint: expected value (probability-weighted), most likely amount (single best estimate), or not applicable for fixed obligations.']
  contract_asset_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code for contract asset when performance obligation is satisfied before payment is received. Represents conditional right to consideration.']
  contract_modification_impact varchar [note: 'How contract modifications affect this performance obligation: separate contract (new distinct goods/services), prospective (forward-looking adjustment), cumulative catch-up (retrospective adjustment), or not applicable.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center code in SAP S/4HANA where direct costs for delivering this performance obligation are tracked. Used for cost allocation and variance analysis.']
  cost_of_service_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code for direct costs associated with delivering this performance obligation. Used for margin analysis and EBITDA reporting.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this performance obligation master record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance obligation master record was first created in the system.']
  deferred_revenue_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code for deferred revenue liability when payment is received before performance obligation is satisfied. Used for ASC 606 compliance.']
  distinct_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this performance obligation is distinct and separately identifiable from other obligations in the contract per ASC 606 criteria. True if distinct, False if bundled.']
  ebitda_classification varchar [note: 'Classification for EBITDA-level revenue reporting: operating revenue (core port services), non-operating revenue (one-time or non-core), or ancillary revenue (supplementary services).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this performance obligation definition is no longer effective. Null for open-ended obligations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this performance obligation definition becomes effective and can be used in contracts and billing.']
  invoice_timing varchar [note: 'Timing of invoice generation relative to service delivery: advance (before service), concurrent (during service), or arrears (after service completion).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this performance obligation master record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance obligation master record was last modified.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the performance obligation master record: active (in use), inactive (temporarily disabled), deprecated (being phased out), pending approval (awaiting activation), or retired (no longer used).']
  navis_n4_service_code varchar [note: 'Service code in NAVIS N4 TOS that maps to this performance obligation. Used for invoice generation from terminal operations.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the performance obligation, used for external reference and reporting.']
  obligation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service or deliverable that constitutes the performance obligation under the contract.']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the performance obligation (e.g., Container Handling Service, Vessel Berthing, Storage Fee).']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the performance obligation by nature: service (e.g., cargo handling), goods (e.g., equipment sale), license, access (e.g., berth access), or combined bundle.']
  performance_duration_days varchar [note: 'Expected duration in days to satisfy this performance obligation. Null for point-in-time obligations. Used for over-time revenue recognition calculations.']
  price_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standalone selling price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center code in SAP S/4HANA for internal management reporting and profitability analysis. Typically represents terminal, business unit, or service line.']
  progress_measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method for measuring progress toward satisfaction of over-time obligations: input method (costs incurred), output method (units delivered), time elapsed (straight-line), or not applicable for point-in-time.']
  regulatory_reporting_category varchar [note: 'Category used for regulatory revenue reporting to port authorities, maritime regulators, or government agencies.']
  revenue_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code in SAP S/4HANA FI where revenue for this performance obligation is posted. Maps to chart of accounts for financial reporting.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method by which revenue is recognized for this performance obligation: point in time (upon completion), over time (duration-based), milestone-based (upon achievement), or usage-based (per transaction).']
  satisfaction_criteria varchar [note: 'Business criteria that define when the performance obligation is considered satisfied (e.g., container loaded onto vessel, vessel departed berth, storage period completed).']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Maritime-specific service category for the performance obligation: container handling, vessel services (berthing, pilotage), storage (container yard), cargo operations, terminal access, or ancillary services.']
  standalone_selling_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The price at which the entity would sell the promised good or service separately to a customer. Used for transaction price allocation in multi-element contracts.']
  standard_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Standard or default quantity associated with this performance obligation when used in contracts (e.g., 1.0 for single container move, 24.0 for daily storage).']
  tariff_item_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the published port tariff item or rate card entry that defines pricing for this performance obligation. Used for regulatory compliance and customer transparency.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Specific tax category or jurisdiction code applicable to this performance obligation for VAT/GST/sales tax calculation.']
  tax_treatment_code varchar [note: 'Tax treatment classification for this performance obligation: taxable (subject to VAT/GST), exempt (no tax), zero-rated (0% tax but input credit allowed), or out of scope.']
  transfer_control_indicator varchar [note: 'Primary indicator used to determine when control transfers to the customer: customer acceptance, physical possession, legal title transfer, payment obligation created, or significant risks and rewards transferred.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for quantifying the performance obligation: TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit), FEU (Forty-foot Equivalent Unit), ton, cubic meter, hour, day, vessel call, transaction, or each.']
  variable_consideration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this performance obligation includes variable consideration such as volume discounts, performance bonuses, penalties, or usage-based pricing. True if variable, False if fixed.']
  variable_consideration_type varchar [note: 'Type of variable consideration applicable: volume discount, performance bonus, penalty (e.g., demurrage), usage-based, time-based, or none if fixed pricing.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this performance obligation definition. Incremented when material changes are made to pricing, terms, or recognition criteria.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for performance_obligation. Referenced by performance_obligation_id.'
}

Table billing.billing_cycle {
  billing_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for billing_cycle']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this billing cycle record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who closed and finalized this billing cycle. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this billing cycle record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  parent_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent billing cycle if this is an adjustment or sub-cycle. Used to maintain hierarchical relationships between regular and adjustment cycles.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity or business unit responsible for issuing invoices in this billing cycle. Used for multi-entity port operations.']
  prior_billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on billing_cycle (prior_billing_cycle_id)']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marine terminal or port facility for which this billing cycle applies. Links to terminal master data.']
  auto_invoice_generation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether invoices for this billing cycle are generated automatically by the billing system or require manual review and approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this billing cycle record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary transactions in this billing cycle. Typically USD for international maritime trade.']
  cycle_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this billing cycle was closed and finalized, preventing further transaction posting.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the billing cycle, used for external reference and reporting. Typically follows port authority naming conventions.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the billing cycle, such as \'Monthly Container Services - January 2024\' or \'Quarterly Vessel Operations Q1-2024\'.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing cycle. Active cycles are open for transaction posting; closed cycles are finalized for revenue recognition.']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing cycle frequency. Determines the recurring pattern for invoice generation and revenue recognition.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the billing cycle, including any special conditions, adjustments, or notes relevant to this cycle.']
  dispute_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date during which customers can raise billing disputes for invoices in this cycle. Typically 30-90 days.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last date of the billing cycle period. All transactions with service dates on or before this date are eligible for inclusion in this cycle.']
  external_cycle_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or identifier used by upstream systems (NAVIS N4, SAP) to identify this billing cycle. Used for system integration and reconciliation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period number within the fiscal year (1-12 for monthly, 1-4 for quarterly). Used for period-based financial reporting.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter number (1-4) to which this billing cycle belongs, used for quarterly financial reporting and analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this billing cycle belongs, used for annual financial reporting and budget tracking.']
  gl_period varchar [note: 'The general ledger accounting period in YYYYMM format to which this billing cycle is assigned for financial reporting.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which financial transactions for this billing cycle are posted to the SAP S/4HANA general ledger for accounting purposes.']
  integration_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system from which billing cycle data originates, typically NAVIS N4 TOS or SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  invoice_generation_date date [note: 'The date on which invoices for this billing cycle are generated and issued to customers. Typically occurs after the cycle end date.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing cycle record is currently active and available for use. Inactive cycles are retained for historical reference but not used in current operations.']
  is_adjustment_cycle boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a special adjustment or correction cycle used to process chargebacks, credit memos, or billing corrections outside the regular billing schedule.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this billing cycle record was last updated or modified.']
  late_fee_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether late payment fees are applicable for invoices in this billing cycle if payment is received after the due date.']
  late_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate applied to overdue invoice amounts as a late payment penalty. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 1.5 for 1.5% per month).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes for internal use, capturing operational comments, exceptions, or special handling instructions for this billing cycle.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment for invoices in this billing cycle must be received to avoid late fees or penalties. Calculated based on payment terms.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days from invoice date to payment due date for this billing cycle. Common values are 30, 45, or 60 days.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which revenue for this billing cycle is recognized in the general ledger for financial reporting and EBITDA calculation.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'The primary category of port services covered by this billing cycle. Used to segment revenue streams and align with Terminal Operating System (TOS) NAVIS N4 service classifications.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first date of the billing cycle period. All transactions with service dates on or after this date are eligible for inclusion in this cycle.']
  total_billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount billed across all invoices in this billing cycle, before adjustments or payments. Used for revenue forecasting and EBITDA reporting.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total discount amount applied across all invoices in this billing cycle, including volume discounts, early payment discounts, and promotional adjustments.']
  total_invoice_count varchar [note: 'The total number of invoices generated for this billing cycle. Used for cycle reconciliation and billing volume tracking.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total net amount due across all invoices in this billing cycle after applying discounts and taxes. Represents the actual revenue to be collected.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount (VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes) included in all invoices for this billing cycle.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for billing_cycle. Referenced by billing_cycle_id.'
}

Table booking.call_booking {
  call_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for call_booking']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Vessel calls operate under terminal operating agreements (TOAs) or berth license agreements that define service terms, rates, SLAs, and berth access rights. Essential for rate lookup, billing validati']
  compliance_marpol_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.marpol_record. Business justification: Vessel calls generate MARPOL waste disposal records at port (ballast water, oily waste, garbage). Port reception facilities log waste operations. Environmental compliance and IMO MARPOL requirement.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Vessel call operations (pilotage, towage, berth services) incur direct costs allocated to marine operations cost centres. Essential for operational cost tracking and variance analysis in port P&L repo']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Vessel call bookings are the primary trigger for port service invoicing - pilotage, towage, wharfage, and navigation charges. Port billing systems must link invoices to the originating vessel call boo']
  last_port_un_locode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.un_locode. Business justification: last_port_of_call field exists. FK to un_locode master enables port validation, voyage tracking, customs reporting, health declaration processing, and quarantine requirement determination for vessel c']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the local shipping agent representing the shipping line for this vessel call. Responsible for pre-arrival coordination and documentation.']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Vessel call bookings must reference the applicable port tariff schedule to determine vessel dues, berth charges, and service rates. Core business process: tariff application at booking confirmation an']
  primary_port_call_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vessel.call. Business justification: Vessel call booking represents the reservation/request for a port call, must link to the actual vessel.call record for berth planning, VTS coordination, and operational execution. Port operations team']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Vessel call revenue (port dues, berth charges, service fees) is tracked by profit centre (container terminal, bulk terminal, marine services) for business unit P&L reporting and performance management']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Commercial vessel calls for regular shipping line customers operate under negotiated rate cards with preferential pricing. Real business process: contracted rate application for vessel services and ca']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Vessel call bookings undergo sanctions screening of vessel, owner, operator, and flag state. Port denies entry to sanctioned vessels. ISPS and national security requirement.']
  shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping line (carrier) operating the vessel for this call. Links to the customer/shipping line master record.']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: imo_number exists on call_booking. FK to vessel_master enables vessel specification lookup for berth assignment, pilotage requirement determination, towage calculation, safety compliance verification,']
  ata timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vessel arrived at the port anchorage or pilot boarding area. Populated after arrival event occurs.']
  atb timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vessel berthed at the terminal. Populated after berthing event occurs.']
  atd timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vessel departed from the berth. Populated after departure event occurs.']
  booking_cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vessel call booking was cancelled by either the shipping line or port authority.']
  booking_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vessel call booking was officially confirmed by port operations and berth allocation was finalized.']
  booking_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique booking reference number assigned to the vessel call booking. Used for communication with shipping lines and agents.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vessel call booking. Tracks progression from initial nomination through confirmation to completion or cancellation.']
  booking_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vessel call booking request was initially submitted by the shipping line or agent to the port.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the vessel call booking was cancelled (e.g., schedule change, weather, operational constraints).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vessel call booking record was first created in the system.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of customs clearance process for the vessel and its cargo. Determines whether cargo operations can commence.']
  dangerous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vessel is carrying IMDG-classified dangerous goods requiring special handling and safety protocols.']
  eta timestamp [note: 'Estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to arrive at the port anchorage or pilot boarding area.']
  etb timestamp [note: 'Estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to berth at the assigned terminal.']
  etd timestamp [note: 'Estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to depart from the berth and leave the port.']
  expected_container_moves varchar [note: 'Estimated total number of container handling moves (load + discharge) for this vessel call. Used for resource planning and berth time estimation.']
  expected_teu varchar [note: 'Estimated total TEU volume (standardized container unit) for this vessel call. Used for yard planning and capacity management.']
  isps_security_level varchar [note: 'ISPS security level applicable to the vessel call. Level 1 (normal), Level 2 (heightened), Level 3 (exceptional).']
  next_port_of_call varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the next port the vessel will visit after departing this port. Used for cargo planning and customs clearance.']
  pilotage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether marine pilotage service is required for the vessel to navigate into and out of the port.']
  reefer_container_count varchar [note: 'Number of refrigerated containers requiring power supply and temperature monitoring during the vessel call.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or coordination details related to the vessel call booking.']
  service_requirements varchar [note: 'Summary of requested port services for the vessel call (e.g., pilotage, towage, mooring, bunkering, waste disposal, fresh water supply).']
  service_rotation varchar [note: 'Shipping line service route or rotation code that this vessel call is part of (e.g., Asia-Europe Express, Pacific Loop).']
  towage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tug boat assistance is required for berthing and unberthing operations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vessel call booking record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Core master record for a vessel call booking request submitted by a shipping line or agent. Represents the commercial intake of a vessel visit to the port, capturing vessel identity (IMO number, vessel name), expected arrival/departure windows (ETA/ETD/ETB), requested berth, service requirements summary, voyage details (voyage number, service rotation, last/next port), booking status lifecycle (nominated, pending, confirmed, cancelled, completed), and pre-arrival coordination data. Central SSOT for all vessel call bookings — all other booking domain entities reference this record. Aligned with DCSA booking model concepts. Sourced from Port Community System (PCS) and NAVIS N4.'
}

Table booking.booking_service_order {
  booking_service_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the booking service order. Primary key for this entity.']
  berth_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.berth. Business justification: Service orders (pilotage, towage, mooring, bunkering) are executed at specific berths. Operations teams dispatch resources and coordinate safety based on berth location. Replaces denormalized berth_co']

  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.tariff_item. Business justification: Each service order (pilotage, towage, mooring, cargo handling) must link to the specific tariff item defining its pricing structure, units, and rates. Core business process: service charge calculation']

  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: service_location field exists. FK to port_location master enables resource dispatch routing, location-based billing rate application, operational zone validation, and equipment positioning for service']
  rate_card_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card_line. Real business process: contracted service pricing and SLA complianc']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Service orders for vessels/cargo require sanctions screening before approval. Port authority validates no sanctioned entities before providing services. OFAC/UN sanctions compliance mandate.']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.service_code. Business justification: service_type field exists.   service_scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.service_scope.   sla_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sla_definition. Business justification: Service orders have SLA targets (response time, completion time) that should reference master SLA definitions in contracts for consistency. Required for SLA compliance measurement, performance trackin']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Ground services (container handling, cargo operations, equipment deployment) occur in specific terminal zones. Yard planners and equipment dispatchers require this link to allocate mobile equipment an']
  vessel_call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent vessel call booking that this service order is linked to. Establishes the relationship between the service request and the vessel visit.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.warehouse. Business justification: Warehouse services (storage, stuffing/stripping, inspection) are booked against specific warehouse facilities. Warehouse managers track bookings to manage capacity, schedule dock assignments, and allo']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for the service after completion. Used for final billing and financial reconciliation.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the service execution was completed. Used for billing, performance measurement, and SLA compliance.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the service execution began. Used for operational tracking and Service Level Agreement (SLA) measurement.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service order requires management or operational approval before confirmation. True if approval is required, False otherwise.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service order was approved. Used for audit trail and workflow tracking.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the service order. Populated when approval_required_flag is True.']
  assigned_crew_count varchar [note: 'Number of crew members or personnel assigned to execute the service. Used for resource planning and cost allocation.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for cancelling the service order. Populated only when service_status is cancelled or no_show.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service order was cancelled. Used for audit trail and cancellation fee calculation.']
  confirmed_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the port confirmed the service would be completed. Represents the committed service appointment end.']
  confirmed_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the port confirmed the service would begin. Represents the committed service appointment start.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this service order.']
  edi_message_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the EDI message (e.g., IFTMIN instruction message) that initiated or confirmed this service order. Used for system integration traceability.']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service execution met all environmental compliance requirements (emissions, waste handling, spill prevention). True if compliant, False if non-compliant.']
  estimated_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost for the service at the time of booking. Used for budgeting and customer quotation purposes.']
  hazardous_cargo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service involves handling or supporting hazardous cargo as defined by International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Code. True if hazardous, False otherwise.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG classification of hazardous cargo involved in this service. Populated only when hazardous_cargo_flag is True.']
  isps_security_level varchar [note: 'ISPS security level in effect at the time of service execution. Influences operational procedures and access controls.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service order record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the service order. Influences resource allocation and scheduling decisions.']
  requested_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer or vessel agent requested the service to be completed. Defines the requested service window end.']
  requested_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer or vessel agent requested the service to begin. Represents the initial service window request.']
  requesting_party_contact varchar [note: 'Contact information (phone or email) for the party who requested the service. Used for operational coordination and confirmation.']
  requesting_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or individual who requested the service. Typically the vessel agent, shipping line, or cargo owner.']
  service_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique business identifier for the service order. Used for customer communication and operational tracking.']
  service_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantitative measure of the service being provided. Unit depends on service type (e.g., cubic meters for bunkering, hours for pilotage, number of containers for stevedoring).']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service order. Tracks the progression from initial request through to completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|confirmed|scheduled|in_progress|completed|cancelled|no_show — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the service quantity. Defines how the service is quantified for billing and operational tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours|cubic_meters|metric_tons|teu|feu|moves|trips|units — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_actual_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual time in minutes taken to complete the service from the confirmed start time. Used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  sla_actual_response_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual time in minutes taken to respond to and confirm the service request. Used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service order met all SLA commitments (response time and completion time). True if compliant, False if breached.']
  sla_target_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'The contractual target time in minutes for the service to be completed from the confirmed start time. Part of the SLA commitment.']
  sla_target_response_minutes varchar [note: 'The contractual target time in minutes for the port to respond to and confirm the service request. Part of the SLA commitment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that originated this service order record. Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any special instructions, requirements, or notes related to the service execution. Used for operational coordination.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for a specific port service request (pilotage, towage, mooring, stevedoring, crane allocation, bunkering, fresh water, waste reception) linked to a vessel call booking. Captures service type, requested and confirmed time windows, assigned resources/crew, service status lifecycle (requested, confirmed, scheduled, in-progress, completed, cancelled, no-show), priority, SLA parameters, and actual execution timestamps. Consolidates both the service request and the confirmed appointment into a single lifecycle entity — there is no separate appointment record. Aligned with SMDG port service message standards. Sourced from NAVIS N4 and VTMS.'
}

Business justification: Berth reservations validate ISPS security level compatibility between vessel and berth facility. Declaration of Security (DoS) required when security levels differ. ISPS Code mandatory protocol.']

Real business process: towage charge estimation and tug resource planning.']
  vessel_call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: Berth reservations are created as part of vessel call bookings - they represent the physical berth slot allocation for a commercial booking. berth_reservation currently links to vessel_call_id (cross-']
  vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel call booking that this berth reservation is associated with. Links the berth slot to the broader vessel visit.']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Berths are located within designated security zones. Access control enforcement, patrol routing, CCTV coverage, and MARSEC-level measures depend on knowing which security zone the berth occupies. Esse']
  atb timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vessel completed berthing and mooring operations. Recorded for performance measurement and billing purposes.']
  atd timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vessel departed the berth and the berth slot was released. Recorded for performance measurement and billing purposes.']
  beam_meters decimal(7,2) [note: 'Maximum width of the vessel at its widest point in meters. Used to validate berth width constraints and safe maneuvering clearances.']
  berth_side varchar [note: 'Indicates which side of the vessel will be positioned alongside the berth face. Critical for crane positioning and cargo handling planning.']
  berth_utilization_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned duration in hours that the berth will be occupied by this vessel. Calculated from ETB to ETD for capacity planning and billing purposes.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the berth reservation was cancelled. Captures business context for cancelled bookings for analysis and dispute resolution.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the berth reservation was cancelled. Used for berth slot release and capacity reallocation.']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the berth reservation status was changed to confirmed. Marks the point when the berth slot became a firm commitment.']
  dangerous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vessel is carrying IMDG-classified dangerous goods requiring special berth allocation and safety protocols. True if dangerous cargo is present.']
  draft_meters decimal(6,2) [note: 'Vertical distance between the waterline and the bottom of the vessel hull in meters. Critical for under-keel clearance calculations.']
  dwt_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight capacity of the vessel including cargo, fuel, water, stores, and crew in metric tonnes. Used for berth load capacity validation.']
  etb timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when the vessel is expected to berth and commence mooring operations. Used for berth slot planning and resource allocation.']
  etd timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when the vessel is expected to complete cargo operations and depart the berth. Used for berth slot release planning.']
  expected_teu_volume varchar [note: 'Anticipated number of TEU containers to be handled during this berth visit. Used for resource planning and berth productivity forecasting.']
  high_tide_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the next high tide event relevant to this berth reservation. Used for tidal window planning when vessel draft requires maximum water depth.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG classification code for dangerous goods carried by the vessel. Determines berth safety zone requirements and segregation rules.']
  isps_security_level varchar [note: 'ISPS security level applicable to this berth reservation. Determines security protocols and access controls during the vessel visit.']
  loa_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum length of the vessel from bow to stern in meters. Used to validate berth length capacity and ensure adequate berth space allocation.']
  low_tide_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the next low tide event relevant to this berth reservation. Used to validate minimum under-keel clearance during the berthing period.']
  mooring_gang_size varchar [note: 'Number of mooring personnel required for line handling operations during berthing and unberthing. Based on vessel size and mooring configuration.']
  number_of_tugs varchar [note: 'Number of tugboats required to assist with berthing and unberthing operations. Determined by vessel size, weather conditions, and port regulations.']
  pilotage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether marine pilotage services are mandatory for this vessel berthing operation based on vessel size, cargo type, or port regulations. True if pilot is required.']
  planning_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the system or module that created or last updated this berth reservation. Tracks data lineage for berth planning decisions.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this berth reservation. Influences berth allocation decisions during conflicts or capacity constraints.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'General free-text notes and comments related to this berth reservation. Used for operational coordination and information sharing among port stakeholders.']
  reservation_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this berth reservation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and booking lead time analysis.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique business identifier for the berth reservation. Used for external communication and tracking. Format: BR followed by 10 digits.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the berth reservation. Tracks progression from initial booking through vessel departure or cancellation.']
  reservation_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this berth reservation record was last modified. Tracks changes to berth allocation, timing, or status throughout the booking lifecycle.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of cargo service or vessel operation associated with this berth reservation. Determines required berth facilities and handling equipment.']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling, equipment, or operational requirements for this berth reservation. Examples include reefer power, fresh water supply, or security protocols.']
  tidal_window_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vessel requires specific tidal conditions for safe berthing or departure due to draft constraints. True if tidal window planning is mandatory.']
  towage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tugboat assistance is required for safe berthing or unberthing operations. True if towage services are needed.']
  under_keel_clearance_meters decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum required clearance between the vessel keel and the seabed in meters. Ensures safe navigation and berthing operations considering tidal variations.']
  weather_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this berth reservation is subject to weather-related operational restrictions. True if weather conditions may impact berthing operations.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a berth slot reservation linked to a vessel call booking. Stores the assigned berth identifier, planned and actual berthing/unberthing timestamps (ETB/ATB/ETD/ATD), berth face allocation (port/starboard side-to), LOA and DWT constraints, beam restrictions, tidal window requirements, under-keel clearance parameters, reservation status (tentative, confirmed, occupied, departed, cancelled), and planning source (TOPS Expert or NAVIS N4 berth planner). SSOT for berth slot assignments within the booking lifecycle. Aligned with SMDG berth allocation message standards.'
}
