  wharfage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.wharfage_schedule. Business justification: Wharfage (cargo dues) must be calculated per cargo booking based on commodity type, weight, and applicable wharfage schedule. Real business process: wharfage charge calculation and regulatory complian']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date when the cargo arrived at the port of discharge. Used for demurrage calculation and performance tracking.']
  booking_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the booking was confirmed by the terminal operator or shipping line.']
  booking_date date [note: 'Date when the cargo booking was initially created or requested by the customer.']
  booking_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique booking reference number issued to customer. Used for tracking and communication across stakeholders.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cargo booking. Tracks progression from initial request through completion or cancellation.']
  cargo_type varchar [note: 'Classification of cargo handling method. FCL (Full Container Load), LCL (Less than Container Load), RoRo (Roll-on Roll-off), LoLo (Lift-on Lift-off), bulk, or breakbulk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cargo booking record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  delivery_order_number varchar [note: 'Delivery order number authorizing release of cargo to consignee or designated party. Required for cargo pickup.']
  estimated_arrival_date date [note: 'Estimated date when the cargo will arrive at the port of discharge. Used for planning and customer notification.']
  feu_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of Forty-foot Equivalent Units for this cargo booking. Alternative measure of container capacity.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross weight of the cargo including packaging and container tare weight, measured in kilograms.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Special handling instructions or operational notes for terminal operators. May include stacking restrictions, priority handling, or safety precautions.']
  is_dangerous_goods boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cargo contains dangerous goods requiring special handling and compliance with IMDG Code.']
  is_oversized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cargo exceeds standard container dimensions requiring special handling equipment or stowage planning.']
  is_overweight boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cargo exceeds standard weight limits requiring heavy-duty handling equipment or special permits.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cargo requires temperature-controlled (reefer) container with active refrigeration or heating.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cargo booking record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and data quality.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment responsibility terms for freight and terminal handling charges. Prepaid (shipper pays), collect (consignee pays), or third-party.']
  pod_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE 5-character code for the port where cargo is discharged from the vessel.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of port service requested for this cargo booking. Determines operational workflow and billing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that originated this cargo booking data. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  stowage_position varchar [note: 'Six-digit stowage position code from BAPLIE message. Format: BBRRTT (Bay-Row-Tier) indicating container location on vessel.']
  temperature_setpoint_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target temperature setpoint for reefer cargo in degrees Celsius. Critical for perishable goods and pharmaceuticals.']
  teu_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of Twenty-foot Equivalent Units for this cargo booking. Standard measure of container capacity.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying dangerous goods substance. Required for IMDG compliance and safety documentation.']
  volume_cbm decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of the cargo measured in cubic meters. Critical for LCL and breakbulk cargo planning.']

  Note: 'Master record for cargo booked against a vessel call, consolidating commercial booking, slot allocation, and pre-arrival manifest declaration into a single cargo-level SSOT. Captures BOL reference, cargo type (FCL/LCL/RoRo/LoLo/bulk), commodity/HS Code, TEU/FEU count, gross weight, POL/POD, shipper/consignee references, IMDG DG class, temperature-controlled flag, stowage position (bay-row-tier from BAPLIE), manifest reference, customs entry status, and booking confirmation status. Aligned with DCSA transport document and UN/EDIFACT IFTMBF booking message standards. Sourced from PCS and NAVIS N4.'
}

Table booking.slot_reservation {
  slot_reservation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the container slot reservation record. Primary key.']
  cargo_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent cargo booking for which this slot is reserved. Links the physical slot allocation to the commercial booking contract.']
  container_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.container. Business justification: Slot reservations allocate specific containers in vessel stowage - direct operational link for slot management, container tracking, and load planning. Slot allocation systems require container_id for ']
  imdg_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.imdg_class. Business justification: hazmat_class field exists. FK to imdg_class master enables stowage compatibility checking, segregation table enforcement, bay planning validation, and special handling instruction retrieval for hazard']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Slot reservations for regular shipping line customers reference negotiated slot rates and volume commitments in rate cards. Real business process: slot pricing under service contracts and volume commi']
  vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vessel call on which this slot is reserved. Identifies the voyage and port call for the container stowage.']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for slot allocation when multiple bookings compete for limited space. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Typically based on booking time, customer tier, contract terms, or cargo urgency. Used by auto-allocation algorithms.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the slot allocation decision. Shipping Line Nomination: slot requested by carrier. Terminal Allocation: terminal assigned slot based on operational optimization. Auto Allocation: system-generated based on rules. Manual Override: planner manually assigned slot. Tracks allocation authority and negotiation outcome.']
  bay_position varchar [note: 'Longitudinal bay number on the vessel where the container is stowed. Part of the BAPLIE stowage coordinate system (bay-row-tier). Odd numbers typically indicate 20ft bays, even numbers indicate 40ft bays.']
  cancelled_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the slot reservation was cancelled. Null if not cancelled. Cancellation releases the slot back to available inventory and may trigger rebooking or penalty assessment.']
  confirmed_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipping line confirmed the slot reservation. Null if not yet confirmed. Confirmation locks the slot and triggers downstream gate and yard planning processes.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this slot reservation record was first created in the terminal operating system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that created this slot reservation record. Used for audit trail, accountability, and operational quality tracking.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the unconfirmed reservation will automatically expire and be released. Typically set based on terminal policy (e.g., 24-48 hours from reservation). Null for confirmed reservations.']
  final_destination varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the ultimate destination for the cargo, which may differ from port of discharge if transshipment or inland transport is involved. Used for end-to-end supply chain visibility.']
  oversize_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the cargo exceeds standard container dimensions. Standard: fits within ISO container envelope. Out of Gauge: exceeds one or more dimensions. Over Height: exceeds standard height. Over Width: exceeds standard width. Over Length: exceeds standard length. OOG cargo requires special stowage planning and may incur surcharges.']
  port_of_discharge varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the port where the container will be discharged from the vessel. Five-character code. Determines discharge sequence planning and onward transport coordination.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the port where the container will be loaded onto the vessel. Five-character code (2-letter country + 3-letter location). Used for routing, customs, and manifest generation.']
  reefer_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this slot requires a reefer plug connection for refrigerated container power supply. True for temperature-controlled cargo requiring continuous power during voyage.']
  reefer_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required temperature setting in degrees Celsius for refrigerated containers. Null for non-reefer slots. Typical range: -30°C to +30°C. Critical for perishable cargo integrity.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the slot reservation, used in communications with shipping lines and customers. May follow terminal-specific numbering convention.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the slot reservation. Reserved: initial allocation pending confirmation. Confirmed: shipping line has confirmed the slot. Cancelled: reservation voided before use. Substituted: replaced by another slot. Released: slot returned to available pool. Expired: reservation lapsed without confirmation. No Show: container did not arrive for loading. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reserved|confirmed|cancelled|substituted|released|expired|no_show — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reserved_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the slot was initially reserved. Marks the start of the reservation lifecycle. Used for reservation expiry calculations and slot utilization analytics.']
  restow_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this container requires restowing (shifting) during the voyage or at an intermediate port to access containers beneath it. True for containers that will be temporarily moved and re-stowed. Impacts operational cost and vessel schedule.']
  row_position varchar [note: 'Transverse row number on the vessel where the container is stowed. Part of the BAPLIE stowage coordinate system. Odd numbers port side, even numbers starboard side, with 00 at centerline.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique seal number applied to the container door to ensure cargo integrity and detect tampering. Recorded at stuffing and verified at unstuffing. Required for customs and security compliance (ISPS, C-TPAT).']
  shipping_line_code varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) of the shipping line that nominated or owns this slot. Four-letter code assigned by NMFTA. Used for carrier identification, billing, and slot allocation tracking.']
  slot_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Commercial rate charged for this slot reservation. May be per TEU, per container, or per booking depending on tariff structure. Null if slot is allocated under volume contract without per-slot pricing. Used for revenue recognition and billing.']
  slot_rate_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the slot rate amount. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP. Null if slot_rate_amount is null.']
  slot_type varchar [note: 'Physical container type and size for which the slot is allocated. Determines TEU/FEU calculation, stowage constraints, and handling requirements. Standard: dry cargo. High Cube: 9ft 6in height. Reefer: refrigerated. Open Top: removable roof. Flat Rack: collapsible sides. Tank: liquid cargo. Platform: flat base only. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard_20|standard_40|high_cube_40|high_cube_45|reefer_20|reefer_40|open_top_20|open_top_40|flat_rack_20|flat_rack_40|tank_20|platform_20|platform_40 — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for special stowage, handling, or operational instructions for this slot. Examples: \'Keep away from heat sources\', \'Load last for priority discharge\', \'Requires customs inspection\'. Used by vessel planners and stevedores.']
  stowage_location varchar [note: 'General stowage area classification on the vessel. Below Deck: in cargo holds. On Deck: above deck stacked. Hatch Cover: on top of cargo hold covers. Affects cargo safety, insurance, and handling sequence.']
  teu_count decimal(5,2) [note: 'TEU equivalent of this slot reservation. 20ft containers = 1.0 TEU, 40ft containers = 2.0 TEU, 45ft containers = 2.25 TEU. Used for vessel capacity planning and terminal throughput metrics.']
  tier_position varchar [note: 'Vertical tier number on the vessel where the container is stowed. Part of the BAPLIE stowage coordinate system. 02, 04, 06 etc. for below deck; 82, 84, 86 etc. for on deck.']
  transshipment_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this container will be transshipped to another vessel at the port of discharge rather than being delivered to final consignee. True for transshipment cargo requiring onward vessel connection.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for dangerous goods (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline). Null for non-hazardous cargo. Used for regulatory compliance, emergency response, and stowage segregation per IMDG Code.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this slot reservation record was last modified. Updated on any field change. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified this slot reservation record. Used for change audit trail and operational accountability.']
  verified_gross_mass_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Verified Gross Mass of the container in kilograms, as required by SOLAS VGM regulation. Mandatory for all packed containers since July 2016. Used for vessel stability calculations and safe stowage planning.']
  vgm_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the container gross mass per SOLAS VGM regulation. Method 1: weighing the packed container. Method 2: weighing all contents and adding tare weight. Both methods are acceptable; method must be documented.']
  vgm_verified_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the VGM was verified. Must be before vessel loading per SOLAS requirements. Used for compliance audit trail and operational gate planning.']
  vgm_verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the party responsible for VGM verification (shipper, freight forwarder, or authorized agent). Required for SOLAS VGM compliance and liability tracking.']
  weight_class varchar [note: 'Weight category of the container for stowage planning. Light: <10 tonnes. Medium: 10-20 tonnes. Heavy: 20-30 tonnes. Super Heavy: >30 tonnes. Determines bottom-tier placement and stack height limits per vessel stability rules.']

  Note: 'Container slot reservation on a vessel for a specific cargo booking, representing the physical bay-row-tier stowage position (BAPLIE reference) allocated to cargo. Captures slot type (20ft/40ft/HC/RF/OOG), slot status (reserved, confirmed, cancelled, substituted), weight class, reefer plug requirement, and allocation source (shipping line nomination or terminal allocation). Manages the commercial slot allocation negotiation between shipping lines and the terminal operator. Links to cargo_booking as the commercial parent. Sourced from NAVIS N4 vessel planning module.'
}

  submitted_by_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the individual who submitted the pre-arrival notification for urgent communication.']
  total_cargo_weight_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight of all cargo on board in metric tonnes as declared in the pre-arrival notification.']
  total_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total container capacity in TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Units) declared in the cargo manifest for this vessel call.']
  towage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether towage (tug) services are required for berthing or unberthing operations.']
  vts_coordination_status varchar [note: 'Status of coordination with Vessel Traffic Service for vessel arrival, pilotage scheduling, and anchorage management.']

  Note: 'Captures the formal pre-arrival notification (PAN/FAL forms) submitted by the vessel master, agent, or shipping line prior to port entry, as required by IMO FAL Convention and national maritime authority regulations. Feeds customs pre-clearance, port health, and VTS coordination workflows. Sourced from PCS and Maritime Single Window.'
}

  exception_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.tariff_exception. Business justification: Booking amendments that trigger tariff exceptions (rate waivers, free time extensions, charge adjustments) must link to the exception record for audit trail and approval tracking. Real business proces']

  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, or agent) who submitted the amendment request. Links to customer or stakeholder master data.']
  vessel_call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: booking_amendment has a polymorphic original_booking_id that can reference multiple booking entity types (vessel call, cargo, berth, etc.). Adding vessel_call_booking_id as a new nullable FK handles a']

  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the amendment request in the approval workflow. Tracks lifecycle from submission through approval or rejection. Superseded indicates a newer amendment has replaced this one.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of amendment being requested. Indicates which aspect of the original booking is being modified: ETA (Estimated Time of Arrival) change, ETB (Estimated Time of Berthing) change, ETD (Estimated Time of Departure) change, berth allocation change, cargo manifest revision, service order modification, quantity adjustment, equipment type change, full cancellation of booking, or partial cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: eta_change|etb_change|etd_change|berth_change|cargo_revision|service_change|quantity_change|equipment_change|full_cancellation|partial_cancellation — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the amendment request was approved by the port authority or terminal operator. Null if still pending or rejected. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  booking_entity_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the type of booking entity being amended: vessel call booking, cargo booking, service order, berth reservation, or slot booking. Enables polymorphic amendment tracking across different booking types.']
  cancellation_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the cancellation or amendment fee charged to the customer. Null if no fee applies. Used for billing and revenue recognition.']

  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has raised a dispute regarding this amendment, its fees, or its impact. True if dispute exists, False otherwise.']
  dispute_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal dispute case raised in relation to this amendment. Null if no dispute exists. Links to dispute management system.']
  edi_message_type varchar [note: 'Type of EDI message sent to communicate the amendment to external systems and stakeholders. COPARN (Container Pre-Announcement), BAPLIE (Bayplan/Stowage Plan), IFTMIN (Instruction Message), CODECO (Container Discharge/Loading Order).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the approved amendment becomes effective and the revised booking values take effect. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  field_name_amended varchar [note: 'Name of the specific booking field being amended (e.g., estimated_time_arrival, berth_code, cargo_quantity, service_type). Enables precise tracking of what aspect of the booking changed.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automated notification has been sent to affected stakeholders (shipping line, cargo owner, customs, etc.) about this amendment. True if notification sent, False otherwise.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when stakeholder notification was sent via EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) or PCS messaging. Null if notification not yet sent. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  original_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original value of the field being amended, stored as string to accommodate various data types (dates, berth codes, quantities, service codes). Provides audit trail of what was changed.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for processing the amendment request. Urgent amendments require immediate attention and expedited approval workflow.']
  processing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes added by port operations staff during amendment review and processing. Captures operational context, coordination details, and special handling instructions.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the amendment request. Used for root cause analysis, demurrage dispute resolution, and operational performance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather_delay|vessel_breakdown|cargo_delay|operational_constraint|customer_request|port_congestion|customs_hold|equipment_unavailability|commercial_change|force_majeure — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text detailed explanation of the reason for the amendment request. Provides context beyond the standardized reason code for dispute resolution and audit purposes.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the approver when an amendment request is rejected. Null if approved or still pending. Used for customer communication and dispute resolution.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the amendment request was submitted by the requestor. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  requestor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual contact person who submitted the amendment request on behalf of the requestor party. Used for communication and dispute resolution.']
  requestor_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the requestor contact person for amendment-related communication and notifications.']
  requestor_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the requestor for urgent amendment coordination and clarification.']
  revised_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The new requested value for the field being amended, stored as string to accommodate various data types. Represents the desired state after amendment approval.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this amendment was caused by or resulted in a breach of service level agreement commitments. True if SLA breach occurred, False otherwise.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system where the amendment request originated: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System, Port Community System (PCS), TOPS Expert TOS, Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS), or manual entry.']
  source_system_reference varchar [note: 'Original amendment identifier or transaction reference from the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this amendment record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Audit trail for data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Tracks all amendments, change requests, and cancellation requests made to any booking entity (vessel call bookings, cargo bookings, or service orders) after initial submission. Captures amendment type (ETA change, berth change, cargo revision, service change, full cancellation), original and revised values, reason code, requestor identity, approval workflow status (pending, approved, rejected), cancellation fee applicability, effective date, and processing timestamp. Provides complete audit trail of all booking lifecycle modifications. Supports demurrage/cancellation charge calculations and dispute resolution. Sourced from PCS and NAVIS N4.'
}

  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the gate appointment. Tracks progression from initial request through confirmation, arrival, processing, and completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|confirmed|checked_in|in_progress|completed|cancelled|no_show — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  appointment_time_slot_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the scheduled appointment window. Defines the latest time the truck should arrive to honor the appointment.']
  appointment_time_slot_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the scheduled appointment window. Defines the earliest time the truck should arrive at the gate.']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the gate appointment based on the transaction purpose. Import delivery (container leaving terminal), export receival (container entering terminal for loading), empty return (returning empty container), empty pickup (collecting empty container), inspection, or repair movements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: import_delivery|export_receival|empty_return|empty_pickup|inspection|repair_gate_in|repair_gate_out — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  booking_reference_number varchar [note: 'External booking reference number provided by the shipping line or freight forwarder. Links the gate appointment to the cargo booking.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the appointment was cancelled. Captured when appointment_status is set to cancelled for operational analysis and customer service.']
  cargo_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Verified gross mass of the container and cargo in kilograms. Required for SOLAS VGM compliance and safe handling operations.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the truck completed check-in procedures at the gate and was authorized to enter the terminal. Marks the start of gate transaction processing.']
  container_condition varchar [note: 'Physical and cargo status of the container at the time of gate appointment. Full indicates laden container, empty indicates no cargo, damaged indicates equipment requiring inspection or repair.']
  container_size_type varchar [note: 'ISO 6346 container size and type code. Indicates physical dimensions and equipment type (e.g., 20GP = 20-foot general purpose, 40HC = 40-foot high cube, 20RF = 20-foot refrigerated). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 20GP|40GP|40HC|45HC|20RF|40RF|20OT|40OT|20FR|40FR — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  coparn_message_reference varchar [note: 'EDI COPARN message reference number for container pre-advice. Links the gate appointment to the electronic pre-notification received from the shipping line or freight forwarder.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created the appointment booking. Supports audit trail and accountability for appointment management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the gate appointment booking record was first created in the system. Audit trail for appointment request lifecycle.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued driver license number for identity verification and security screening at the port facility.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the truck driver assigned to the appointment. Required for security clearance and access control compliance.']
  driver_phone_number varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number of the driver for real-time communication regarding appointment changes, delays, or gate instructions.']
  dwell_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes the truck spent inside the terminal from check-in to gate-out. Key performance indicator for gate efficiency and truck turnaround time.']
  gate_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the truck exited the terminal after completing the container transaction. Marks the completion of the gate appointment lifecycle.']
  iftmin_message_reference varchar [note: 'EDI IFTMIN message reference number for transport instruction. Links the gate appointment to electronic shipping instructions received via EDI.']
  is_hazardous_cargo boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the container holds dangerous goods requiring special handling and compliance with IMDG Code regulations.']
  is_oversize boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the container or cargo exceeds standard dimensions, requiring special handling equipment or routing through the terminal.']
  is_refrigerated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the container is a reefer unit requiring temperature-controlled handling and power connection at the terminal.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified the appointment record. Supports change tracking and operational accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the appointment record. Tracks changes to appointment details, status transitions, or corrections.']
  no_show_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the truck failed to arrive during the scheduled appointment window. Used for carrier performance tracking and slot utilization analysis.']
  reefer_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target temperature setting in degrees Celsius for refrigerated containers. Critical for maintaining cold chain integrity for perishable cargo.']
  shipping_line_code varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) identifying the ocean carrier or shipping line responsible for the container. Used for billing and operational coordination.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes regarding special handling requirements, equipment needs, or safety precautions for this gate appointment.']
  truck_license_plate varchar [note: 'Vehicle registration license plate number of the truck making the gate appointment. Used for vehicle identification and access control at the terminal gate.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline). Required for dangerous goods shipments per IMDG Code.']

  Note: 'Manages truck gate appointment bookings for container pickup and delivery at the terminal. Captures truck registration, driver identity, container number, booking reference, appointment window (date/time slot), gate lane assignment, appointment type (import delivery, export receival, empty return, empty pickup), appointment status, and COPARN/IFTMIN EDI reference. Supports controlled gate access and yard pre-planning. Sourced from NAVIS N4 gate module.'
}

Table booking.voyage_nomination {
  voyage_nomination_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the voyage nomination record. Primary key for the voyage nomination entity.']
  container_preadvice_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the COPARN EDI message that originated this nomination. Enables traceability back to source system and message audit trail.']
  edi_message_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the IFTMIN EDI message if nomination was submitted via instruction message format. Alternative to COPARN for some carriers.']
  last_port_un_locode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.un_locode. Business justification: last_port_of_call field exists. FK to un_locode master enables port name resolution, country identification, routing validation, customs origin reporting, and PSC jurisdiction determination for voyage']
  nominated_berth_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preferred berth requested by the shipping line for this vessel call. Subject to port authority approval based on availability and operational constraints.']
  processed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Voyage nominations are reviewed and processed by port planning staff who assess berth availability, terminal capacity, and service feasibility before acceptance/rejection. Tracks accountability for no']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Voyage nominations from shipping lines operating under service contracts reference applicable rate cards for voyage-level pricing agreements. Real business process: contracted voyage pricing and volum']
  shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipping line or carrier submitting the voyage nomination.']
  superseded_by_nomination_voyage_nomination_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer nomination that replaced this one. Null if this is the current active nomination. Used to track nomination revision history.']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preferred terminal facility for cargo handling operations. May be specified instead of or in addition to specific berth.']
  vessel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the nominated vessel for this port call.']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: imo_number exists on voyage_nomination. FK to vessel_master enables vessel capability validation against nominated cargo volumes, berth compatibility checking, crane reach verification, and compliance']
  voyage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vessel.voyage. Business justification: Voyage nomination by shipping lines must link to the actual vessel.voyage record for schedule validation, rotation sequence verification, and cargo manifest reconciliation. Terminal planners match nom']
  acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the port authority accepted the nomination and confirmed the berth allocation. Null if nomination is still pending or was rejected.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this voyage nomination record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  empty_count varchar [note: 'Number of empty containers nominated for repositioning during this call. Impacts yard utilization and vessel weight distribution.']
  export_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominated volume of export containers to be loaded at this port, expressed in TEU. Used for gate planning and vessel stowage preparation.']
  hazmat_count varchar [note: 'Number of containers carrying dangerous goods (IMDG cargo) nominated for this call. Triggers special handling, segregation, and compliance requirements.']
  import_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominated volume of import containers to be discharged at this port, expressed in TEU. Critical for yard planning and equipment allocation.']
  next_port_of_call varchar [note: 'Next scheduled port in the vessel\'s itinerary after this call. Provides context for transshipment cargo routing and vessel schedule reliability.']
  nominated_eta timestamp [note: 'Shipping line\'s initial estimate of vessel arrival time at port limits. First signal in the vessel scheduling process, typically provided 7-14 days before arrival.']
  nominated_etb timestamp [note: 'Requested berthing time window start. Represents the shipping line\'s preferred time to commence cargo operations.']
  nominated_etd timestamp [note: 'Planned departure time from berth as nominated by the shipping line. Used for berth occupancy planning and resource allocation.']
  nomination_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the voyage nomination, typically provided by the shipping line or generated by the Port Community System (PCS). Used for external communication and tracking.']
  nomination_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the nomination was first received by the port system. Used for SLA tracking and nomination lead-time analysis.']
  nomination_source varchar [note: 'Channel or system through which the nomination was received. Tracks data quality and integration pathway.']
  nomination_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the voyage nomination. Tracks progression from initial nomination through acceptance or rejection by port authority.']
  oversized_count varchar [note: 'Number of out-of-gauge or oversized containers (open-top, flat-rack, or containers with cargo exceeding standard dimensions) nominated for this call. Requires special yard slots and handling equipment.']
  pilotage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether marine pilotage services are required for this vessel call. Drives pilot scheduling and marine services coordination.']
  reefer_count varchar [note: 'Number of refrigerated containers (reefers) nominated for this call. Requires special yard planning for power plug availability and monitoring.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation for why the nomination was rejected (e.g., berth unavailable, vessel dimensions exceed berth capacity, insufficient advance notice). Null if nomination was accepted.']
  rejection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the nomination was rejected by the port authority. Null if nomination was accepted or is still pending.']
  restow_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominated volume of containers requiring restow operations (discharge and reload on same vessel for improved stowage). Impacts berth time and crane productivity.']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling requirements, operational constraints, or service requests submitted with the nomination (e.g., \'Requires mobile crane for heavy lift\', \'Priority berthing requested\').']
  total_moves varchar [note: 'Total number of container handling moves nominated for this vessel call (sum of all discharge and load operations). Key metric for crane hour estimation and berth productivity planning.']
  towage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tug boat assistance is required for berthing and unberthing operations. Drives towage resource planning.']
  transhipment_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominated volume of transhipment containers (discharge and reload) at this port, expressed in TEU. Represents containers moving between vessels without leaving port custody.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this voyage nomination record was last modified. Tracks data currency and change frequency for operational monitoring.']

  Note: 'Captures the shipping line\'s formal nomination of a vessel voyage for a port call, representing the earliest commercial signal in the booking lifecycle before a full vessel call booking is confirmed. Includes nominated vessel name, IMO number, voyage number, service rotation, nominated berth window, expected cargo volumes (TEU import/export/transhipment), and nomination acceptance status (nominated, accepted, rejected, superseded). Aligned with DCSA transport plan and COPARN nomination message flows. Sourced from PCS EDI messaging (COPARN/IFTMIN).'
}

Table booking.service_requirement {
  service_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service requirement record. Primary key for the service requirement entity.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Service requirements (pilotage, towage, special equipment) need operational approval by port duty managers before resource allocation. Tracks authorization for billing, safety compliance (ISPS/IMDG), ']
  booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent vessel call booking that this service requirement is part of. Links the service requirement to the overall booking context.']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.service_code. Business justification: service_type field exists. FK to service_code master enables service catalog validation, pricing lookup, billing integration, UOM standardization, and approval workflow routing for service requirement']
  vessel_call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: service_requirement has a polymorphic booking_id that can reference multiple booking entity types. Adding vessel_call_booking_id as a new nullable FK handles the most common case where service require']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service requirement was formally approved and authorized for service order creation. Marks the transition from demand signal to committed execution plan.']
  billing_reference varchar [note: 'Customer-provided reference number for billing and invoicing purposes. Links the service requirement to the customer\'s internal purchase order or cost center for financial reconciliation.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the service requirement was cancelled by the requester or port authority. May cite vessel schedule changes, cargo plan modifications, or force majeure events. Populated only when requirement_status is cancelled.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field documenting regulatory compliance considerations, customs requirements, or trade documentation needs associated with the service requirement. May reference SOLAS, MARPOL, or local port authority regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service requirement record was first created in the system. Represents the initial capture of the demand signal, which may precede formal submission.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost. Typically USD for international maritime transactions, but may vary based on port jurisdiction and customer agreement.']
  equipment_type_required varchar [note: 'Specific type of terminal equipment required to fulfill the service. Critical for equipment dispatch planning and capacity validation. STS = Ship-to-Shore crane, MHC = Mobile Harbour Crane, RTG = Rubber Tyred Gantry, AGV = Automated Guided Vehicle.']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Preliminary cost estimate for the requested service based on tariff schedules and service parameters. Provided for customer budgeting and approval purposes prior to service order confirmation.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected duration in hours for completing the requested service. Used for resource scheduling, berth planning, and service order timeline estimation.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier from the source system or customer system that originated this service requirement. Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation with external booking platforms or EDI messages.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG classification for dangerous goods handling requirements. Mandatory when service_type includes dangerous goods handling. Drives compliance protocols and specialized equipment allocation.']
  isps_security_level varchar [note: 'Required ISPS security level for the service execution. Level 1 = normal, Level 2 = heightened, Level 3 = exceptional. Determines access control, screening procedures, and security resource allocation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service requirement record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the requirement for audit trail and data freshness purposes.']
  oog_cargo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service requirement involves Out of Gauge cargo that exceeds standard container dimensions. Triggers specialized handling procedures and equipment requirements.']
  oog_dimensions varchar [note: 'Detailed dimensional specifications for OOG cargo in format Length x Width x Height (meters). Required when oog_cargo_flag is true for safe handling and stowage planning.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority indicator for the service requirement. Influences scheduling sequence and resource allocation decisions in terminal operations.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the service quantity field. Defines how the service volume is quantified for billing and resource allocation purposes.']
  reefer_humidity_required decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target humidity level as percentage for refrigerated container monitoring. Applicable for specific cargo types requiring controlled atmosphere.']
  reefer_temperature_required decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target temperature setting in Celsius for refrigerated container monitoring service. Critical for maintaining cold chain integrity for perishable cargo.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the service requirement was rejected. May cite capacity constraints, regulatory non-compliance, incomplete information, or operational conflicts. Populated only when requirement_status is rejected.']
  requested_service_date date [note: 'Target date when the service is required to be performed, typically aligned with vessel ETB (Estimated Time of Berthing) or cargo operation schedule.']
  requested_service_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the service is required to commence, providing hour-level scheduling granularity for operational planning.']
  requester_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the requester contact person for service requirement communication and confirmation.']
  requester_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual submitting or responsible for the service requirement on behalf of the requester organization.']
  requester_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number of the requester contact person for urgent service coordination and operational communication.']
  requester_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the shipping line, cargo owner, or freight forwarder organization submitting the service requirement. Acts as the commercial counterparty for the service request.']
  requirement_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the service requirement, typically formatted as SR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external communication and tracking.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service requirement. Tracks progression from initial declaration through approval or rejection.']
  service_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric quantity of the service required, measured in service-specific units (e.g., number of tugs for towage, number of crane hours for stevedoring, number of containers for reefer monitoring).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or channel through which the service requirement was submitted. Supports data lineage tracking and integration troubleshooting. EDI = Electronic Data Interchange, PCS = Port Community System.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing specific operational instructions, safety requirements, or handling procedures that must be followed during service execution. May include temperature settings for reefer cargo, securing requirements for OOG (Out of Gauge) cargo, or IMDG class-specific protocols.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service requirement was formally submitted by the shipping line or cargo owner for processing. Represents the official business event timestamp for requirement intake.']
  vessel_dwt decimal(12,2) [note: 'Deadweight Tonnage of the vessel, representing total carrying capacity. Used for berth allocation validation and service fee calculation. DWT = Deadweight Tonnage in metric tons.']
  vessel_loa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Length Overall of the vessel for which services are being requested. Critical for pilotage and towage resource planning. LOA = Length Overall, measured in meters.']

  Note: 'Declared service requirements submitted by a shipping line or cargo owner as part of a vessel call booking. Captures required service types (pilotage, towage, mooring, stevedoring, reefer monitoring, dangerous goods handling, OOG cargo handling), quantity, special handling instructions, equipment requirements (STS crane, MHC, RTG), compliance requirements (IMDG class, ISPS security level), and priority indicators. Acts as the demand signal that drives service_order creation and terminal resource planning. Distinct from service_order in that requirements represent declared needs before scheduling, while service_orders represent committed execution plans.'
}

Table booking.booking_anchorage_booking {
  booking_anchorage_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the anchorage booking record. Primary key for this entity.']
  agent_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipping agent or representative who submitted the anchorage booking request on behalf of the vessel owner or operator.']
  anchorage_area_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the designated anchorage area or zone requested for this booking. Links to anchorage area master data defining capacity and operational constraints.']
  booking_berth_reservation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated berth reservation if the vessel is anchoring while awaiting berth availability. Links anchorage to the planned berth allocation. Null if no berth reservation exists.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Anchorage services (VTS monitoring, pilot boat operations, buoy maintenance) incur costs allocated to marine services cost centre for anchorage operations cost tracking and anchorage fee pricing.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VTS operator or port authority user who approved and confirmed the anchorage booking. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Anchorage bookings incur anchorage dues calculated by vessel tonnage and duration at anchorage. Port authorities must link invoices to anchorage bookings for statutory charge calculation, compliance w']
  isps_facility_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.isps_facility_record. Business justification: Anchorage area assignments require ISPS security level validation and DoS coordination. Anchorage areas are designated port facilities under ISPS Code. Maritime security requirement.']
  port_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.port_asset. Business justification: Anchorage bookings use specific buoys and mooring equipment. Port authorities track which physical assets (buoys, anchors, chains) are assigned to each anchorage for maintenance scheduling, safety ins']
  port_dues_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_dues_schedule. Business justification: Anchorage bookings incur port dues based on vessel GRT, anchorage duration, and applicable port dues schedule. Real business process: anchorage charge calculation and billing to vessel agent.']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Security incidents at anchorage (stowaways, smuggling, unauthorized vessel approach, quarantine violations) must be linked to vessel booking for investigation, PFSO reporting, and liability determinat']
  vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vessel call or port visit associated with this anchorage booking. Links to the vessel call record.']
  vessel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel requesting anchorage. Links to the vessel master data.']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: vessel_id FK exists to vessel domain, but imo_number also present. FK to vessel_master enables independent vessel specification lookup for anchorage area compatibility, swinging circle calculation, an']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Anchorage areas are designated security zones with specific MARSEC measures, patrol requirements, and access restrictions. Links booking to zone for security patrol assignment, VTS coordination, and e']
  actual_anchor_drop_time timestamp [note: 'Actual recorded date and time when the vessel dropped anchor, as reported by VTS or pilot. Used for billing and performance tracking.']
  actual_anchorage_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual duration of anchorage in hours, calculated from actual anchor drop and weigh anchor times. Used for billing and performance analysis.']
  actual_weigh_anchor_time timestamp [note: 'Actual recorded date and time when the vessel weighed anchor and departed anchorage, as reported by VTS or pilot. Marks end of anchorage occupancy.']
  anchorage_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total anchorage fee charged for this booking, calculated based on vessel size, duration, and applicable tariff rates. Excludes additional service charges.']
  anchorage_charge_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the anchorage charge amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  anchorage_reason_code varchar [note: 'Primary reason for the vessel requesting anchorage. Determines operational handling and priority. STS = Ship-to-Ship transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: awaiting_berth|tidal_window|customs_clearance|bunkering|sts_transfer|crew_change|repairs|quarantine|weather|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  anchorage_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text detailed explanation of the anchorage reason, providing additional context beyond the reason code.']
  booking_cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the anchorage booking was cancelled, either by the vessel agent or port authority. Null if booking was not cancelled.']
  booking_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the anchorage booking was confirmed and allocated by VTS or port operations. Represents formal acceptance of the request.']
  booking_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique reference number for the anchorage booking, used in communications with vessel agents and VTS. Format: ANC-YYYYMMDD-XXXX.']
  booking_requested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the anchorage booking request was initially submitted to the port authority or VTS. Marks the start of the booking lifecycle.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the anchorage booking. Tracks progression from initial request through confirmation, occupation, and departure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|confirmed|allocated|occupied|departed|cancelled|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  buoy_number varchar [note: 'Specific buoy or mooring point assigned within the anchorage area, if applicable. Format varies by port (e.g., A-12, NW-5).']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the anchorage booking was cancelled. Null if booking was not cancelled.']
  confirmed_anchor_drop_time timestamp [note: 'VTS-confirmed date and time when the vessel is authorized to drop anchor. May differ from requested time due to capacity or operational constraints.']
  confirmed_weigh_anchor_time timestamp [note: 'VTS-confirmed date and time when the vessel is expected or authorized to weigh anchor and depart. Subject to operational adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this anchorage booking record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  hazmat_cargo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vessel is carrying hazardous or dangerous goods as defined by IMDG Code. True if hazmat cargo is present, requiring special anchorage area assignment and safety protocols.']
  imdg_class_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IMDG hazard class codes for dangerous goods on board (e.g., 1.1, 3, 5.1). Null if no hazmat cargo. Used for anchorage area segregation and emergency response planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this anchorage booking record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  pilot_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether pilotage service is required for the vessel to navigate to or from the anchorage area. True if pilot is mandatory per port regulations or vessel characteristics.']
  planned_anchorage_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated duration of anchorage in hours, calculated from requested anchor drop and weigh anchor times. Used for capacity planning.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for the anchorage request. Emergency and urgent requests receive expedited handling and preferential allocation.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vessel is under quarantine or health hold, requiring isolation anchorage. True if quarantine is in effect, restricting vessel movement and requiring health authority clearance.']
  requested_anchor_drop_time timestamp [note: 'Vessel agent\'s requested date and time for the vessel to drop anchor and commence anchorage. Used for capacity planning and VTS coordination.']
  requested_weigh_anchor_time timestamp [note: 'Vessel agent\'s requested date and time for the vessel to weigh anchor and depart anchorage. Used for duration estimation and capacity release planning.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Current ISPS security level applicable to this anchorage booking. Level 1 = normal, Level 2 = heightened, Level 3 = exceptional. Affects anchorage area access and monitoring requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system that originated this anchorage booking record. VTMS = Vessel Traffic Management System, PCS = Port Community System, NAVIS = Terminal Operating System, MANUAL = manually entered.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any special operational instructions, requirements, or notes related to this anchorage booking. Used for coordination between vessel agent, VTS, and port operations.']
  tug_assistance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tug (towage) assistance is required for the vessel to safely anchor or depart the anchorage area. True if tugs are needed due to vessel size, weather, or operational constraints.']
  vts_coordination_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the Vessel Traffic Service for coordination and tracking of this anchorage event. Links to VTS operational logs.']
  weather_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the anchorage booking is subject to weather-related restrictions or holds. True if adverse weather conditions (wind, swell, visibility) prevent safe anchoring or departure.']

  Note: 'Manages booking and reservation of anchorage positions for vessels awaiting berth availability, tidal windows, customs clearance, or performing at-anchor operations (bunkering, STS transfer). Captures vessel identity, requested anchorage area or designated buoy, planned anchor drop and weigh anchor times, reason for anchorage, anchorage status (requested, confirmed, occupied, departed), and VTS coordination reference. Distinct from berth_reservation as anchorage has separate planning rules and capacity constraints. Sourced from VTMS.'
}

Table booking.resource_allocation {
  resource_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the resource allocation record. Primary key for this entity.']
  booking_berth_reservation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.berth_reservation. Business justification: Resources (cranes, tugs, pilots, gangs) are allocated to specific berth reservations. resource_allocation currently has berth_code (STRING) which is a denormalized reference. Adding berth_reservation_']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Labor and equipment resources allocated to vessel services are charged to operational cost centres for actual cost capture, resource utilization analysis, and service costing.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this resource allocation, required for high-priority or special allocations.']
  equipment_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.equipment_type. Business justification: resource_type field exists. When resource_type=\'equipment\', FK to equipment_type master enables capability matching, SWL validation, certification requirement checking, maintenance schedule lookup, an']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Resources allocated to capital projects (berth upgrades, crane installation, infrastructure expansion) are charged to internal orders for project cost tracking and capex budget control.']
  personnel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_personnel. Business justification: Security personnel are allocated resources (vehicles, equipment, posts) for shift assignments and vessel security operations. Links allocation to specific officer for accountability, certification ver']
  port_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.port_asset. Business justification: Resource allocations assign specific cranes, RTGs, reach stackers, or tugs to vessel calls. Operations teams track which physical asset serves each booking for utilization reporting, billing accuracy,']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: service_location field exists. FK to port_location master enables crew dispatch optimization, equipment positioning, location-based costing, shift zone assignment, and operational area access control ']
  reassigned_resource_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on resource_allocation (reassigned_resource_allocation_id)']
  resource_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific port asset or crew resource being allocated (crane unit, tug vessel, pilot, gang).']
  vessel_call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the confirmed vessel call booking for which resources are being allocated.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Major infrastructure projects (terminal expansion, deepening, automation) use WBS elements for detailed phase-level cost tracking of allocated resources, essential for project financial control and ca']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for this resource allocation based on actual usage and applicable charges.']
  actual_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual duration in hours that the resource was deployed, used for billing and performance analysis.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resource actually completed service for the vessel call, used for billing and performance tracking.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resource actually began service for the vessel call, recorded from operational systems.']
  allocated_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of resource units allocated (e.g., 2 cranes, 3 tugs, 1 pilot, 4 gang members).']
  allocation_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique reference number for this resource allocation, used for operational tracking and communication.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'System or process that created this resource allocation (manual planning, automated scheduling, optimization engine, VTS coordination).']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the resource allocation from initial request through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|confirmed|assigned|in_progress|completed|cancelled|released — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  allocation_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the allocated resource quantity (individual units, gangs, crews, shifts, hours, container moves).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this resource allocation was formally approved by authorized personnel.']
  billing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this resource allocation to billing and invoicing systems for revenue capture.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this resource allocation was cancelled, used for operational analysis and dispute resolution.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this resource allocation was cancelled, marking the end of its active lifecycle.']
  confirmed_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resource is confirmed to complete service, may differ from requested time due to operational adjustments.']
  confirmed_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resource allocation was confirmed by the resource manager or scheduling system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this resource allocation record was first created in the system.']
  crew_size varchar [note: 'Number of personnel assigned to the resource for this allocation (e.g., gang size, tug crew complement).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  edi_message_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the EDI message (IFTMIN, COPARN) that triggered or communicated this resource allocation.']
  estimated_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated cost for this resource allocation based on tariff rates and planned duration.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned duration in hours for this resource allocation, used for scheduling and capacity planning.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocated resource is certified to handle dangerous goods per IMDG Code requirements.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG classification of dangerous goods that this resource is allocated to handle, if applicable.']
  isps_security_level varchar [note: 'ISPS security level applicable to this resource allocation, determining security protocols and clearances required.']
  performance_notes varchar [note: 'Operational notes capturing performance observations, issues, or exceptional circumstances during resource deployment.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this resource allocation, used for conflict resolution and scheduling optimization.']
  requested_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resource is expected to complete service for the vessel call.']
  requested_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resource is requested to begin service for the vessel call.']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Category of port resource being allocated. Includes equipment (cranes, tugs, RTG, MHC) and labor (pilots, gangs). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crane|tug|pilot|mooring_gang|lashing_gang|stevedore_gang|mobile_harbour_crane|reach_stacker|forklift|rtg — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  shift_assignment varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the resource is allocated (day shift, night shift, swing shift, or continuous operation).']
  sla_actual_response_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes taken for resource to respond and be ready for deployment, used for SLA compliance tracking.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this resource allocation met the defined service level agreement targets for response and completion.']
  sla_target_response_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for resource to be ready and respond to allocation request per service level agreement.']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any special operational requirements, certifications, or constraints for this resource allocation.']
  supervisor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supervisor or foreman responsible for overseeing this resource allocation during operations.']
  swl_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocated equipment resource is within its certified Safe Working Load limits for this operation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this resource allocation record was last modified, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  weather_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this resource allocation is subject to weather-related operational restrictions or safe working limits.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of port resources (cranes, tugs, pilots, gangs) allocated to a confirmed vessel call booking, linking service requirements to specific operational assets and time windows.'
}

Table booking.confirmation {
  confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the booking confirmation record. Primary key for this entity.']
  agent_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipping agent acting on behalf of the shipping line. The agent who receives and manages the confirmation on behalf of the vessel operator.']
  amended_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on confirmation (amended_confirmation_id)']
  confirmation_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port authority user who approved this booking confirmation before issuance. May be null if approval is not required or is automated.']
  confirmation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port authority user or system account that issued this booking confirmation.']
  primary_superseded_by_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer booking confirmation that supersedes this one. Null if this is the current active confirmation.']
  shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipping line organization that is the primary counterparty for this booking confirmation. The party to whom the confirmation is issued.']
  vessel_call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original vessel call booking request that this confirmation acknowledges. Links to the booking.call_booking product.']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipping line or agent formally acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the confirmation. May be null if acknowledgment is not yet received.']
  amendment_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether amendments to this confirmation are permitted under the current terms. True if amendments can be requested; false if the confirmation is locked.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this booking confirmation was approved for issuance. Null if approval is not required.']
  cancellation_policy varchar [note: 'Description of the cancellation policy and associated fees or penalties that apply if the shipping line cancels or amends this confirmed booking.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this confirmation was cancelled. Null if the confirmation has not been cancelled.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this booking confirmation was officially cancelled. Null if the confirmation is still active.']
  charges_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this confirmation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique confirmation reference number issued to the shipping line or agent. This is the business identifier used in all communications and documentation.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the booking confirmation. Tracks whether the confirmation is active, has been amended, superseded by a newer version, or cancelled.']
  confirmation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the confirmation document. Indicates whether this is the initial confirmation, an amendment to a prior confirmation, or a reconfirmation after schedule changes.']
  confirmed_berth_code varchar [note: 'The berth location code that has been confirmed and allocated for this vessel call. This is the binding berth assignment communicated to the shipping line.']
  confirmed_eta timestamp [note: 'The confirmed estimated time of arrival at the port as acknowledged in this confirmation. This is the ETA that the port commits to planning for.']
  confirmed_etb timestamp [note: 'The confirmed estimated time of berthing as committed in this confirmation. This is the berth window that the port guarantees to the vessel.']
  confirmed_etd timestamp [note: 'The confirmed estimated time of departure as committed in this confirmation. Represents the planned completion of all port operations.']
  confirmed_terminal_code varchar [note: 'The terminal facility code where the vessel will be handled. Confirms the terminal assignment for cargo operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this booking confirmation record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'The customs clearance status for this vessel call at the time of confirmation. Indicates whether pre-clearance has been obtained or if customs holds apply.']
  dangerous_goods_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port has approved the handling of dangerous goods declared in the booking. True if IMDG cargo has been reviewed and approved.']
  edi_message_reference varchar [note: 'The unique reference number or control number of the EDI message that transmitted this confirmation. Used for EDI reconciliation and tracking.']
  edi_message_type varchar [note: 'The EDI message type used to transmit this confirmation electronically (e.g., IFTMIN, COPARN, BAPLIE). Null if confirmation was not sent via EDI.']
  estimated_total_charges_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated total amount of all port charges and service fees for this vessel call as communicated in the confirmation. Provides cost transparency to the shipping line.']
  imdg_class_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IMDG hazard class codes for dangerous goods confirmed in this booking (e.g., 1.1, 3, 8). Null if no dangerous goods are involved.']
  imo_number varchar [note: 'The unique seven-digit IMO ship identification number assigned to the vessel. Captured at confirmation time for vessel identification and regulatory compliance.']
  isps_security_level varchar [note: 'The ISPS security level applicable to this vessel call at the time of confirmation. Level 1 is normal, Level 2 is heightened, Level 3 is exceptional.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the booking confirmation was officially issued to the shipping line or agent. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  mooring_services_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mooring gang services have been confirmed for berthing and unberthing operations.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the confirmation notification has been successfully sent to the shipping line or agent. True if notification delivery is confirmed.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the confirmation notification was sent to the shipping line or agent via email, EDI, or other communication channel.']
  number_of_tugs_confirmed varchar [note: 'The number of tug vessels confirmed for this vessel call. Null if towage is not required or not yet confirmed.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment terms and conditions applicable to this booking, such as payment due date, advance payment requirements, or credit terms.']
  pilotage_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether pilotage services have been confirmed as part of this booking. True if pilotage is confirmed and scheduled.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The priority classification assigned to this booking confirmation. Determines the precedence for resource allocation and scheduling conflicts.']
  service_orders_confirmed_count varchar [note: 'The total number of service orders (pilotage, towage, mooring, etc.) that are included and confirmed as part of this booking confirmation.']
  sla_completion_commitment_minutes varchar [note: 'The committed service completion time in minutes from berthing as per the SLA terms accepted in this confirmation.']
  sla_response_commitment_minutes varchar [note: 'The committed response time in minutes for initial service acknowledgment as per the SLA terms accepted in this confirmation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or code of the operational system that generated this booking confirmation record (e.g., NAVIS N4, TOPS, Port Community System).']
  source_system_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier or reference number for this confirmation in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  special_requirements_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling requirements, operational constraints, or unique conditions confirmed for this vessel call.']
  terms_accepted_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or role at the shipping line or agent who accepted the terms and conditions on behalf of their organization.']
  terms_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipping line or agent has formally accepted the terms and conditions associated with this confirmation. True if terms have been accepted.']
  terms_accepted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the terms and conditions were formally accepted by the shipping line or agent.']
  terms_and_conditions_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the port terms and conditions that apply to this booking confirmation. References the specific T&C document accepted by the shipping line.']
  towage_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether towage (tug) services have been confirmed as part of this booking. True if tug assistance is confirmed and scheduled.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this booking confirmation record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this confirmation becomes effective and binding. Typically the date of issuance unless specified otherwise.']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'The date until which this confirmation remains valid. May be null for confirmations that remain valid until the vessel call is completed or the confirmation is superseded.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel for which the booking is confirmed. Captured at confirmation time for reference and audit purposes.']
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'The voyage identifier for this specific vessel call. Captured at confirmation time to uniquely identify the voyage being confirmed.']

  Note: 'Formal confirmation record issued to the shipping line or agent acknowledging acceptance of a vessel call booking and all associated service orders, capturing confirmation reference, terms accepted, and SLA commitments.'
}

Table booking.vessel_call_security_assignment {
  vessel_call_security_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this security personnel assignment to a vessel call. Primary key for the association.']
  personnel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the security personnel member assigned to this vessel call']
