  imo_member_status varchar [note: 'Membership status of the country in the International Maritime Organization (IMO). Values: member (full member state), associate_member (associate member), non_member (not a member). Affects applicability of IMO conventions and compliance requirements.']
  indian_ocean_mou_member boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the country is a member of the Indian Ocean Memorandum of Understanding on Port State Control (Indian Ocean MOU). True if member; false otherwise. Determines PSC inspection regime applicability for vessels calling at ports in this country.']
  iso_alpha_2_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter country code as defined by ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 standard. Used in customs declarations, cargo manifests, and vessel registration documentation.']
  iso_alpha_3_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code as defined by ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 standard. Commonly used in Bill of Lading (BOL), Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) messages, and Port Community System (PCS) integrations.']
  iso_numeric_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit numeric country code as defined by ISO 3166-1 numeric standard. Used in international trade statistics and customs systems.']
  isps_code_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the country has implemented the International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code. True if compliant; false otherwise. Critical for port security assessments and vessel clearance procedures.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this country record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.']
  marpol_ratified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the country has ratified the International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships (MARPOL). True if ratified; false otherwise. Determines applicability of environmental protection and emissions standards.']
  mlc_ratified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the country has ratified the Maritime Labour Convention (MLC) 2006. True if ratified; false otherwise. Determines applicability of seafarer labor rights and working conditions standards.']
  official_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the country or territory as recognized by the United Nations and International Maritime Organization (IMO). Used in formal legal documentation, customs declarations, and compliance reporting.']
  paris_mou_member boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the country is a member of the Paris Memorandum of Understanding on Port State Control (Paris MOU). True if member; false otherwise. Determines PSC inspection regime applicability for vessels calling at ports in this country.']
  psc_targeting_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric targeting factor used by Port State Control (PSC) authorities to prioritize vessel inspections based on flag state performance. Higher values indicate higher risk and increased inspection likelihood. Range typically 0.00 to 10.00.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the country record. Values: active (currently in use), inactive (temporarily disabled), deprecated (no longer valid, retained for historical reference). Used to control operational visibility and data quality.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region classification (e.g., Africa, Americas, Asia, Europe, Oceania) as defined by the United Nations geoscheme. Used for regional trade analysis and cargo routing strategies.']
  sanctions_effective_date date [note: 'Date when current sanctions status became effective. Null if no sanctions are in place. Used for historical compliance tracking and audit trails.']
  sanctions_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when current sanctions are scheduled to expire or be reviewed. Null if sanctions are indefinite or no sanctions are in place. Used for forward-looking compliance planning.']
  sanctions_list_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the country is currently subject to international trade sanctions or embargoes (e.g., United Nations, United States OFAC, European Union). True if sanctioned; false otherwise. Critical for trade compliance screening and cargo booking restrictions.']
  solas_ratified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the country has ratified the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS). True if ratified; false otherwise. Determines applicability of SOLAS safety requirements for vessels flagged or calling at ports in this country.']
  sub_region varchar [note: 'Sub-regional classification within the broader region (e.g., Eastern Asia, Western Europe, Southern Africa) as defined by the United Nations geoscheme. Supports granular trade flow analysis and vessel routing optimization.']
  tokyo_mou_member boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the country is a member of the Tokyo Memorandum of Understanding on Port State Control (Tokyo MOU). True if member; false otherwise. Determines PSC inspection regime applicability for vessels calling at ports in this country.']
  trade_agreement_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of trade agreement codes applicable to this country (e.g., USMCA, EU, ASEAN, CPTPP, RCEP). Used to determine preferential tariff rates, customs duty exemptions, and Terminal Handling Charge (THC) adjustments.']
  un_locode_prefix varchar [note: 'Two-letter country prefix used in UN/LOCODE location codes. Matches ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code. Used to construct full UN/LOCODE identifiers for ports and inland terminals within this country.']
  wco_member boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the country is a member of the World Customs Organization (WCO). True if member; false otherwise. Affects customs clearance procedures, Harmonized System (HS) Code usage, and trade facilitation standards.']

  Note: 'Global reference for all countries and territories recognised in port trade, compliance, and vessel registration operations. Captures ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code, alpha-3 code, numeric code, country name, official name, region, sub-region, flag state indicator (boolean), IMO member status, Paris MOU/Tokyo MOU/Indian Ocean MOU membership, PSC targeting factor, grey/black/white list classification, flag state authority contact, maritime conventions ratified (SOLAS, MARPOL, MLC), WCO member flag, FATF status, sanctions list flag, and applicable trade agreement codes. Includes effectivity dating for sanctions status changes. Data steward: Compliance & Regulatory Affairs. SSOT for country identity used across customs clearance, cargo manifests, vessel registration (flag state), customer onboarding, and compliance domains.'
}

Table masterdata.edi_partner {
  edi_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the EDI trading partner. Primary key for the EDI partner master record.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned to this partner within the Port Community System for cross-system integration and stakeholder messaging.']
  acknowledgment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether functional acknowledgment messages (e.g., CONTRL, 997) are required for messages exchanged with this partner.']
  activation_date date [note: 'The date when the EDI connection was activated and live message exchange commenced.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'The authentication mechanism used to secure EDI message exchange with this partner.']
  business_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the business contact for commercial and operational coordination.']
  business_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the business relationship owner at the partner organization for commercial and operational matters.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'The technical protocol used for transmitting EDI messages to and from this partner.']
  connection_endpoint varchar [note: 'The technical endpoint URL or address for EDI message transmission. May be AS2 URL, SFTP host, or API gateway endpoint.']
  connection_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the EDI connection with this partner. Determines whether messages can be exchanged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EDI partner master record was first created in the system.']
  data_steward varchar [note: 'The internal team or role responsible for maintaining this EDI partner master record. Typically IT or Port Community Systems team.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'The date when the EDI connection was deactivated or suspended. Null if currently active.']
  edi_standard varchar [note: 'The primary EDI messaging standard used by this partner for electronic data interchange.']
  encryption_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether message encryption is enabled for EDI transmissions with this partner.']
  last_inbound_message_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent EDI message received from this partner.']
  last_outbound_message_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent EDI message sent to this partner.']
  last_successful_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful EDI message transmission (inbound or outbound) with this partner. Used for connection health monitoring.']
  message_format_version varchar [note: 'The specific version of the EDI standard or message format used by this partner (e.g., EDIFACT D95B, X12 4010).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EDI partner master record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional configuration notes, special requirements, or operational considerations for this EDI partner.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'The date when this EDI partner was first onboarded and configured in the system.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the EDI trading partner. Used in message routing and partner identification across Port Community System (PCS) and Terminal Operating System (TOS).']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the EDI trading partner organization. Used for contract management and billing purposes.']
  partner_short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name or alias for the EDI partner used in operational displays and reports.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the EDI partner based on their role in the maritime logistics supply chain.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary technical contact for EDI support and issue resolution.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary technical contact person at the partner organization responsible for EDI operations.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary technical contact for urgent EDI connectivity issues.']
  receiver_identification varchar [note: 'The unique receiver identification code used in EDI message interchange headers for this partner.']
  receiver_qualifier varchar [note: 'The qualifier code identifying the receiver ID type in EDI message headers.']
  sender_identification varchar [note: 'The unique sender identification code used in EDI message interchange headers for this partner.']
  sender_qualifier varchar [note: 'The qualifier code identifying the sender ID type in EDI message headers (e.g., DUNS, SCAC, GLN).']
  sla_availability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractual uptime availability percentage guaranteed for EDI connectivity with this partner (e.g., 99.50 for 99.5% uptime).']
  sla_response_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The contractual maximum response time in minutes for EDI message acknowledgment or processing by this partner.']
  supported_message_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EDI message types supported by this partner (e.g., COPARN, BAPLIE, IFTMIN, COARRI, CUSCAR, CUSRES, CODECO, IFTMBC). Defines the scope of electronic transactions enabled.']
  test_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this partner connection is configured for test/sandbox mode rather than production message exchange.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'The primary time zone of the partner organization (IANA time zone identifier) used for scheduling and timestamp interpretation.']

  Note: 'Master record for all EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) trading partners connected to the port\'s PCS (Port Community System) and TOS. Captures EDI partner code, partner name, EDI standard (EDIFACT, X12, XML, NAVIS EDI, API/JSON), supported message types (COPARN, BAPLIE, IFTMIN, COARRI, CUSCAR, CUSRES, CODECO, IFTMBC), communication protocol (AS2, SFTP, VAN, API gateway), port community participant reference, onboarding date, last successful transmission date, connection status (active/suspended/decommissioned), and SLA response time. Data steward: IT / Port Community Systems. SSOT for EDI partner identity used across cargo, customs, vessel operations, and intermodal domains.'
}

Table masterdata.imdg_class {
  imdg_class_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IMDG hazard classification record. Primary key.']
  parent_imdg_class_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on imdg_class (parent_imdg_class_id)']
  class_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the IMDG hazard class (e.g., \'Explosives\', \'Flammable Gases\', \'Flammable Liquids\', \'Toxic Substances\', \'Corrosive Substances\', \'Radioactive Material\'). Human-readable label for the class.']
  class_number varchar [note: 'Primary IMDG hazard class number (1 through 9), with optional decimal subdivision (e.g., 2.1, 2.2, 2.3 for gases; 6.1, 6.2 for toxic substances). Defines the principal hazard category per IMO IMDG Code.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IMDG classification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_steward varchar [note: 'Name or role of the data steward responsible for maintaining and validating this IMDG classification master data. Typically the Safety & Compliance team or Dangerous Goods Compliance Officer.']
  division varchar [note: 'Subdivision or division within the IMDG class (e.g., 2.1 for flammable gases, 3 for flammable liquids, 6.1 for toxic substances). Provides finer hazard categorization within the primary class.']
  ems_fire_code varchar [note: 'EmS fire schedule code (e.g., F-A, F-B, F-C) specifying emergency response procedures for fire incidents involving this IMDG class. Referenced from the Emergency Response Procedures for Ships Carrying Dangerous Goods (EmS Guide).']
  ems_spillage_code varchar [note: 'EmS spillage schedule code (e.g., S-A, S-B, S-C) specifying emergency response procedures for spillage or leakage incidents involving this IMDG class. Referenced from the Emergency Response Procedures for Ships Carrying Dangerous Goods (EmS Guide).']
  excepted_quantity_code varchar [note: 'Excepted quantity code (E0, E1, E2, E3, E4, E5) defining the maximum quantity per inner packaging and per package for excepted quantity shipments. Excepted quantities are subject to minimal regulatory requirements.']
  hazard_label_type varchar [note: 'Type or code of the hazard label required for containers and packages carrying goods of this IMDG class (e.g., \'Explosive 1.1\', \'Flammable Gas\', \'Toxic\'). Used for visual identification and compliance with labeling regulations.']
  imdg_code_amendment_cycle varchar [note: 'Amendment cycle or version of the IMDG Code under which this classification is defined (e.g., \'Amendment 40-20\', \'Amendment 41-22\'). The IMDG Code is updated biennially; this field tracks which amendment cycle applies to this record.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'Date when this IMDG classification record was last verified against the current IMO IMDG Code amendment. Used to ensure data currency and compliance with the latest regulatory requirements.']
  limited_quantity_threshold varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity per package or inner packaging that qualifies for limited quantity (LQ) exemptions under the IMDG Code. Expressed as a volume or mass (e.g., \'1L\', \'5kg\'). Limited quantities are subject to reduced regulatory requirements.']
  marine_pollutant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether substances in this IMDG class are classified as marine pollutants under MARPOL Annex III. True if the class typically contains marine pollutants requiring special marking and documentation.']
  marpol_annex_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicable MARPOL annex for pollution prevention related to this IMDG class (e.g., \'Annex I - Oil\', \'Annex II - Noxious Liquid Substances\', \'Annex III - Harmful Substances in Packaged Form\'). Used for environmental compliance and spill response planning.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IMDG classification record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or additional guidance related to this IMDG class. May include port-specific handling instructions, local regulatory variations, or clarifications for operational staff.']
  packing_group varchar [note: 'Packing group classification (I, II, or III) indicating the degree of danger: I = high danger, II = medium danger, III = low danger. Used to determine packaging specifications and handling requirements.']
  segregation_group_code varchar [note: 'Segregation group code assigned to this IMDG class for stowage and segregation purposes (e.g., acids, alkalis, ammonium compounds). Used to determine compatibility and separation requirements in vessel stowage planning and yard block allocation.']
  segregation_table_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the segregation table in the IMDG Code specifying separation requirements (e.g., \'away from\', \'separated from\', \'separated by a complete compartment or hold from\'). Defines minimum physical separation distances for safe stowage.']
  solas_regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicable SOLAS Chapter VII regulation governing the carriage of this dangerous goods class by sea (e.g., \'SOLAS Ch.VII Part A\', \'SOLAS Ch.VII Part B\'). SOLAS Chapter VII addresses the carriage of dangerous goods in packaged form or in solid form in bulk.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system or authoritative source from which this IMDG classification record was sourced (e.g., \'NAVIS N4\', \'Port Community System\', \'Manual Entry from IMO IMDG Code\'). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  special_provisions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of special provision codes applicable to this IMDG class (e.g., SP23, SP172, SP274). Special provisions modify or supplement the general requirements for specific substances or articles within the class.']
  stowage_category varchar [note: 'Stowage category code (A, B, C, D, or E) defining where this IMDG class may be stowed on a vessel. Category A = on deck or under deck; B = on deck or under deck with specific conditions; C = on deck only; D = on deck only with specific conditions; E = prohibited from carriage. Used in BAPLIE stowage planning.']
  un_number_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending UN number in the range typically associated with this IMDG class. Defines the upper bound of UN numbers commonly classified under this hazard class.']
  un_number_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting UN number in the range typically associated with this IMDG class. UN numbers are four-digit identifiers assigned to hazardous substances and articles by the United Nations Committee of Experts on the Transport of Dangerous Goods.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Effective start date for this IMDG classification record. Used to manage amendment transitions and ensure the correct classification rules are applied based on the cargo acceptance or vessel departure date.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Effective end date for this IMDG classification record. Nullable for current/active classifications. Used to manage historical records when IMDG Code amendments supersede or modify classifications.']

  Note: 'Reference master for IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) hazard classifications as defined by the IMO IMDG Code. Captures IMDG class number (1–9), division (e.g., 2.1 flammable gas, 3 flammable liquid, 6.1 toxic), UN number range, hazard label type, packing group (I/II/III), segregation group code, segregation table references (away from, separated from), EmS (Emergency Schedule) fire/spillage codes, special provisions, applicable SOLAS Ch.VII regulation, and IMDG Code amendment cycle reference with effectivity dates (valid_from/valid_to) for amendment transitions. Data steward: Safety & Compliance. SSOT for dangerous goods classification used in BAPLIE stowage planning, gate-in DG acceptance checks, yard block segregation rules, and CUSCAR customs compliance.'
}

Table masterdata.flag_state {
  flag_state_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for flag_state']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Flag states are countries that register vessels under their maritime authority. Normalizing to FK allows joining to country for full country data (ISO codes, region, sub-region, trade agreements, curr']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the flag state in the port\'s master data system. Active indicates the flag state is recognized and vessels under this flag are accepted; inactive indicates the flag state is no longer recognized; suspended indicates temporary restrictions.']
  authority_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting the flag state maritime authority for official correspondence and inquiries.']
  authority_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for contacting the flag state maritime authority.']
  authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the official maritime authority or administration responsible for vessel registration and flag state control in this country.']
  authority_website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the flag state maritime authority for accessing regulations, forms, and public information.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flag state record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this flag state record is effective and valid for use in operational systems.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this flag state record is effective. Null indicates the record is currently active with no planned end date.']
  flag_of_convenience boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flag state is commonly recognized as a flag of convenience, where vessel registration requirements are less stringent and often used for tax or regulatory advantages.']
  flag_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code representing the flag state under which vessels are registered. This is the primary business identifier for the flag state.']
  flag_state_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the flag state country as recognized under international maritime law.']
  imo_member_since_date date [note: 'Date when the flag state became a member of the International Maritime Organization (IMO). Null if not a member.']
  imo_member_status varchar [note: 'Membership status of the flag state in the International Maritime Organization (IMO). Indicates whether the flag state is a full member, associate member, or non-member.']
  indian_ocean_mou_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flag state is a member of the Indian Ocean MOU on Port State Control.']
  isps_code_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flag state is compliant with the International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code, which prescribes responsibilities for governments, shipping companies, and ship and port personnel to detect and deter security threats.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flag state record was last modified or updated.']
  marpol_ratification_date date [note: 'Date when the flag state ratified the MARPOL Convention. Null if not ratified.']
  marpol_ratified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flag state has ratified the International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships (MARPOL).']
  mlc_ratification_date date [note: 'Date when the flag state ratified the Maritime Labour Convention. Null if not ratified.']
  mlc_ratified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flag state has ratified the Maritime Labour Convention (MLC) 2006, which sets out seafarers\' rights to decent working conditions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional information, special considerations, or operational notes related to the flag state.']
  paris_mou_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flag state is a member of the Paris MOU on Port State Control, covering European and North Atlantic regions.']
  psc_list_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the flag state on Port State Control (PSC) performance lists. White list indicates high performance, grey list medium performance, black list poor performance, and not classified indicates no formal classification.']
  psc_targeting_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical targeting factor used by Port State Control (PSC) authorities to determine inspection priority for vessels flying this flag. Higher values indicate higher inspection priority due to poor performance history.']
  recognized_organization_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flag state authorizes Recognized Organizations (classification societies) to carry out statutory surveys and certification on its behalf.']
  registry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the flag state registry. National registries require vessel ownership by nationals; open registries (flags of convenience) allow foreign ownership; international registries are hybrid models.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Internal risk assessment rating assigned to the flag state based on PSC performance, compliance history, and safety record. Used for prioritizing inspections and resource allocation.']
  solas_ratification_date date [note: 'Date when the flag state ratified the SOLAS Convention. Null if not ratified.']
  solas_ratified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flag state has ratified the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS).']
  stcw_ratification_date date [note: 'Date when the flag state ratified the STCW Convention. Null if not ratified.']
  stcw_ratified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flag state has ratified the International Convention on Standards of Training, Certification and Watchkeeping for Seafarers (STCW).']
  tokyo_mou_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the flag state is a member of the Tokyo MOU on Port State Control, covering Asia-Pacific region.']
  total_registered_dwt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Deadweight Tonnage (DWT) of all vessels registered under this flag state. Represents the total cargo-carrying capacity of the fleet.']
  total_registered_grt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Gross Registered Tonnage (GRT) of all vessels registered under this flag state. Used for assessing the overall size and capacity of the flag state\'s fleet.']
  total_registered_vessels varchar [note: 'Total number of vessels currently registered under this flag state. This is a snapshot metric used for fleet size analysis and port planning.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all flag states (countries of vessel registration) recognised under international maritime law. Captures flag state code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2), flag state name, IMO member status, Paris MOU/Tokyo MOU/Indian Ocean MOU membership, PSC (Port State Control) targeting factor, grey/black/white list classification, flag state authority contact, and applicable maritime conventions ratified (SOLAS, MARPOL, MLC). Used in vessel master, PSC inspection, and compliance domains.'
}

Table masterdata.shipping_line {
  shipping_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shipping_line']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: shipping_line has registered_country_code (STRING) which should reference country master. Adding country_id FK normalizes the country reference and removes the denormalized code. Country table contain']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier in the port\'s EDI messaging system. Used for automated exchange of BAPLIE (stowage plans), COPARN (container pre-advice), IFTMIN (transport instructions), and other UN/EDIFACT messages.']
  alliance_membership varchar [note: 'Strategic alliance or consortium membership of the carrier. Major alliances include 2M (Maersk/MSC), THE Alliance (Hapag-Lloyd/ONE/Yang Ming/HMM), Ocean Alliance (CMA CGM/COSCO/OOCL/Evergreen). Independent carriers operate outside alliances. Impacts slot-sharing agreements and vessel scheduling coordination.']
  api_integration_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier has active REST/SOAP API integration with the port\'s Terminal Operating System (TOS) for real-time data exchange beyond traditional EDI.']
  average_teu_per_call decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rolling 12-month average container volume in TEU handled per vessel call for this carrier. Used for yard planning and equipment allocation.']
  average_vessel_calls_per_month decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rolling 12-month average number of vessel calls made by this carrier at the port per month. Used for berth capacity planning and commercial forecasting.']
  bic_operator_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter carrier prefix followed by \'U\' (for container owner) as defined in ISO 6346. Used to identify container ownership and operator in global container tracking systems.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered name of the shipping line or ocean carrier as it appears on commercial contracts and vessel registration documents.']
  carrier_short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or trade name of the shipping line commonly used in operational communications, vessel schedules, and port documentation.']
  commercial_account_reference varchar [note: 'Internal account identifier linking this shipping line to the commercial billing and tariff management system. Used for invoice generation, credit management, and revenue tracking.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned by recognized rating agencies (Moody\'s, S&P, Fitch) reflecting the carrier\'s financial stability and creditworthiness. Used for credit limit determination and payment term negotiation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D|Not Rated — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  customs_broker_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the preferred customs broker or clearance agent used by the carrier for import/export documentation and customs clearance at this port.']
  dangerous_goods_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier is approved and certified to handle IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) cargo at this port. Requires specific documentation, training, and compliance with IMDG Code.']
  data_steward varchar [note: 'Business unit responsible for maintaining and validating the accuracy of this shipping line master record. Commercial: handles tariff and contract data. Marine Operations: handles vessel scheduling and operational data. Both: shared responsibility.']
  edi_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier has active EDI integration with the port for automated message exchange. True: EDI active. False: manual/email-based communication.']
  fleet_size_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier based on global fleet capacity measured in TEU. Mega Carrier: >1M TEU. Major Carrier: 500K-1M TEU. Regional Carrier: 100K-500K TEU. Niche Carrier: 10K-100K TEU. Feeder Operator: <10K TEU. Used for berth allocation priority and commercial negotiation.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the shipping line\'s global or regional headquarters is located.']
  iso_certification_status varchar [note: 'Highest level of ISO certification held by the carrier. ISO 9001: Quality Management. ISO 14001: Environmental Management. ISO 28000: Supply Chain Security. Multiple: holds more than one certification. Used for vendor assessment and partnership evaluation.']
  isps_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier maintains valid ISPS certification and compliance for all vessels calling at the port. Required for international vessel operations per SOLAS Chapter XI-2.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date when this shipping line master record was last reviewed and validated for accuracy by the data steward. Used for data quality monitoring and compliance.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the shipping line at this port. Active: currently operating vessel calls. Suspended: temporarily not calling at port. Ceased: permanently discontinued operations. Merged/Acquired: absorbed into another carrier entity.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days allowed for payment of port charges and terminal handling charges (THC) after invoice date. Typical values: 0 (prepaid), 7, 14, 30, 45, 60 days.']
  preferred_berth_window_hours varchar [note: 'Preferred advance notice window in hours between ETB (Estimated Time of Berthing) and vessel arrival that the carrier requests for berth allocation planning. Used by berth planning system to optimize scheduling.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for operational communications, vessel scheduling, and commercial matters.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the principal commercial or operations contact person at the shipping line for port coordination and service matters.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct telephone number of the primary contact including country and area codes for urgent operational coordination.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shipping line master record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system process name that last modified this shipping line master record. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shipping line master record was last modified. Updated on any field change.']
  reefer_capable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier operates reefer-capable vessels and handles refrigerated cargo at this port. Impacts berth allocation to reefer-equipped berths.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter unique identifier assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA) to identify ocean carriers in EDI transactions and shipping documentation. Required for all US-bound cargo per CBP regulations.']
  service_commencement_date date [note: 'Date when the shipping line first commenced vessel operations at this port facility.']
  service_termination_date date [note: 'Date when the shipping line ceased or suspended vessel operations at this port. Null for active carriers.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Primary type of shipping service the carrier provides at this port. Mainline: direct long-haul international routes. Feeder: short-sea distribution services. Transshipment Hub: hub-and-spoke operations. Regional: intra-regional services. Specialized: project cargo, heavy lift, or niche services.']
  tariff_group_code varchar [note: 'Internal code linking the carrier to a specific tariff schedule for port charges, THC (Terminal Handling Charges), wharfage, and other fees. Carriers in the same group receive identical pricing.']
  total_fleet_teu_capacity varchar [note: 'Total container capacity of the carrier\'s global fleet measured in TEU. Used for commercial assessment and berth planning.']
  vessel_count varchar [note: 'Total number of vessels (owned and chartered) in the carrier\'s operating fleet. Includes container ships, RoRo vessels, and specialized cargo vessels.']
  vgm_compliance_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for VGM verification per SOLAS Chapter VI Regulation 2. Method 1: weighing the packed container. Method 2: weighing all contents and adding tare weight. Both: carrier accepts either method. Required for all export containers since July 2016.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official corporate website URL of the shipping line for reference and public information access.']

  Note: 'Master record for all shipping lines (ocean carriers) operating vessel calls at the port. Captures SCAC code (Standard Carrier Alpha Code), BIC operator code (per ISO 6346), carrier name, alliance membership (2M, THE Alliance, Ocean Alliance, or independent), registered country (FK to country), principal contact, EDI partner reference (FK to edi_partner), preferred berth window, commercial account reference, fleet size indicator, and operational status (active/suspended/ceased). Data steward: Commercial / Marine Operations. Distinct from the broader port_community_participant in the customer domain — this is the operational SSOT for carrier identity used in vessel scheduling, BAPLIE stowage plans, BOL processing, tariff application, and alliance slot-sharing agreements.'
}

Table masterdata.service_code {
  service_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service code record. Primary key. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  parent_service_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to a parent service code if this service is a component of a bundled service. Null for standalone services.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for new or revised service codes. Only approved service codes are activated for billing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this service code for activation. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service code was approved. Null if not yet approved. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  billing_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which the service is billed (e.g., per TEU, per move, per hour, per day, per Gross Tonnage, per vessel call, flat rate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_TEU|per_FEU|per_move|per_hour|per_day|per_GT|per_vessel_call|per_CBM|per_ton|per_lift|per_container|flat_rate — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_gl_account varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code in SAP Controlling (CO) to which costs associated with delivering this service are posted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service code record was first created in the master data system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard rate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  data_steward varchar [note: 'Name or role of the data steward responsible for maintaining the accuracy and currency of this service code record. Typically Commercial or Tariff Management.']
  discount_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service is eligible for volume discounts, customer-specific discounts, or promotional pricing.']
  edi_service_code varchar [note: 'Service code used in Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) messages (e.g., IFTMIN, COPARN) exchanged with shipping lines, freight forwarders, and Port Community System (PCS) partners.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External or legacy service code from predecessor systems, partner port systems, or industry standard code lists (e.g., UN/EDIFACT service codes). Used for data migration and interoperability.']
  is_bundled_service boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service code represents a bundle of multiple underlying services sold as a package (e.g., full terminal handling package including discharge, storage, and gate-out).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this service code record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service code record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  maximum_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum charge cap for this service. Null if no cap applies.']
  minimum_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum charge amount for this service, regardless of quantity or usage. Null if no minimum applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to this service code. Used for operational guidance and exception handling.']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting any regulatory compliance requirements, International Maritime Organization (IMO) regulations, International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) requirements, or environmental regulations applicable to this service.']
  revenue_gl_account varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code in SAP FI to which revenue from this service is posted. Maps service billing to financial accounting.']
  sap_material_code varchar [note: 'SAP Materials Management (MM) material master code corresponding to this service, if the service is treated as a sellable material in SAP. Null if service is billed directly without material master.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the service type. THC = Terminal Handling Charge, IMDG = International Maritime Dangerous Goods, OOG = Out of Gauge. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: THC|wharfage|pilotage|towage|mooring|storage|reefer_monitoring|gate_lift|rail_lift|IMDG_surcharge|OOG_surcharge|demurrage|detention|berth_hire|crane_hire|lashing|unlashing — 17 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric service code used in tariff schedules, Terminal Operating System (TOS) billing triggers, and SAP Financial Accounting (FI) revenue posting. Unique business identifier for the service.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the service, including scope, applicability, and any special conditions or exclusions.']
  service_level_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service is governed by a Service Level Agreement with defined performance targets and penalties.']
  service_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the port service (e.g., Container Discharge, Pilotage Inbound, Reefer Monitoring, Wharfage).']
  service_owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department or division responsible for delivering this service and maintaining the service code definition.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service code. Active services are available for billing; inactive/deprecated services are retained for historical reference.']
  standard_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard published rate for this service in the base currency. Actual billing may vary based on customer agreements, volume discounts, or surcharges.']
  surcharge_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether surcharges (e.g., Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF), Currency Adjustment Factor (CAF), peak season surcharge) may be applied to this service.']
  tariff_schedule_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the owning tariff schedule document or version under which this service code is published. Used for tariff revision cycle tracking.']
  tax_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax (VAT, GST, or other applicable sales tax) is applicable to this service.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code used in SAP FI for tax calculation and posting. Null if tax_applicable_flag is False.']
  tos_billing_trigger varchar [note: 'Event or transaction type in NAVIS N4 or TOPS that triggers automatic generation of a billing line item for this service (e.g., container gate-in, vessel berthing, crane move completion).']
  uom_code varchar [note: 'Unit of measure applicable to this service. TEU = Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit, FEU = Forty-foot Equivalent Unit, GT = Gross Tonnage, CBM = Cubic Meter. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TEU|FEU|move|hour|day|GT|vessel_call|CBM|ton|lift|container — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Effective start date for this service code version. Part of tariff revision cycle management. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Effective end date for this service code version. Null indicates the service code is currently valid with no planned expiration. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this service code record.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all port service codes used in tariff schedules, TOS billing triggers, and SAP FI revenue posting. Captures service code (alphanumeric), service name, service category (THC, wharfage, pilotage, towage, mooring, storage, reefer monitoring, gate lift, rail lift, IMDG surcharge, OOG surcharge, etc.), applicable UOM code, billing basis (per TEU, per move, per hour, per day, per GT, per vessel call), revenue GL account mapping, tax applicability flag, owning tariff schedule reference, and effectivity dates (valid_from/valid_to) for tariff revision cycles. Data steward: Commercial / Tariff Management. SSOT for service code standardisation ensuring consistent billing line item generation across NAVIS N4 billing module and SAP FI/CO.'
}

Table masterdata.cargo_category {
  cargo_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cargo category. Primary key.']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cargo category is currently active and available for use in operational systems. Inactive categories are retained for historical reference.']
  applicable_equipment_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of equipment types suitable for handling this cargo category (e.g., STS crane, RTG, mobile harbour crane, reach stacker, forklift, AGV, bulk loader). References equipment master data.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Standardized short code identifying the cargo category (e.g., FCL, LCL, BULK-DRY, BULK-LIQ, RORO, REEFER, DG, OOG, EMPTY, BREAKBULK). Used across operational systems and EDI messaging.']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the cargo category (e.g., Full Container Load, Less than Container Load, Bulk Dry Cargo, Roll-on Roll-off, Refrigerated Cargo, Dangerous Goods).']
  category_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping cargo categories by handling paradigm: containerized (FCL/LCL), bulk (dry/liquid), breakbulk (general cargo), roro (wheeled), or specialized (project/OOG/reefer/DG).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cargo category record was first created in the master data system.']
  customs_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cargo in this category typically requires mandatory customs inspection or clearance procedures before release.']
  demurrage_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether demurrage charges apply to cargo in this category when dwell time exceeds free time allowances.']
  detention_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detention charges apply to equipment (containers, chassis) used for cargo in this category when held beyond free time.']
  dwell_time_standard_days varchar [note: 'Standard or average dwell time in days for cargo in this category from gate-in to gate-out. Used for yard capacity planning and demurrage calculation baselines.']
  edi_message_type varchar [note: 'Standard EDI message types used for electronic communication of cargo in this category (e.g., COPARN for container pre-announcement, BAPLIE for stowage plan, IFTMIN for instruction messages).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this cargo category definition becomes effective and available for operational use.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this cargo category definition remains effective. Null indicates no planned end date (open-ended).']
  environmental_risk_level varchar [note: 'Environmental risk classification for this cargo category based on potential for pollution, spillage, emissions, or ecological impact (low, medium, high, critical).']
  handling_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to load and discharge this cargo category: LoLo (Lift-on Lift-off via crane), RoRo (Roll-on Roll-off), bulk loader, conveyor, pipeline, or manual handling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lolo|roro|bulk_loader|crane|conveyor|pipeline|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  imdg_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cargo category is subject to IMDG Code regulations for dangerous goods transport and handling.']
  intermodal_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cargo in this category can be transferred to intermodal transport modes (rail, barge, truck) without repackaging or special handling.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cargo category record was last updated or modified.']
  oog_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cargo category accommodates out-of-gauge (oversized) cargo that exceeds standard container or cargo dimensions.']
  quarantine_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cargo in this category requires quarantine or biosecurity inspection (e.g., agricultural products, live animals, timber).']
  reefer_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cargo category includes or requires refrigerated (reefer) container handling capabilities and temperature-controlled storage.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification level required for handling this cargo category under ISPS Code and port security protocols (standard, elevated, high, restricted).']
  special_handling_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cargo category requires special handling procedures, certifications, or equipment beyond standard operations (e.g., dangerous goods, reefer monitoring, oversized cargo).']
  special_handling_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of special handling requirements for this cargo category, including certifications, temperature control, segregation rules, security protocols, or dimensional constraints.']
  storage_area_type varchar [note: 'Type of storage or staging area required for this cargo category: CY (Container Yard), CFS (Container Freight Station), bulk shed, liquid berth, open yard, covered warehouse, reefer yard, or dangerous goods depot. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: container_yard|container_freight_station|bulk_shed|liquid_berth|open_yard|covered_warehouse|reefer_yard|dangerous_goods_depot — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stowage_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for stowage planning and yard allocation (1=highest priority). Influences container stacking order and berth planning decisions.']
  tariff_group_code varchar [note: 'Code linking this cargo category to applicable tariff schedules and billing rate tables. Used by billing systems to determine terminal handling charges (THC) and wharfage rates.']
  teu_conversion_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Conversion factor to express cargo volume or capacity in TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Units). Standard containers: 20ft=1.0 TEU, 40ft=2.0 TEU. Used for capacity planning and billing.']
  un_locode_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether UN/LOCODE location references are required for cargo in this category for international trade documentation and EDI messaging.']
  weight_class varchar [note: 'Weight classification of typical cargo in this category: light (<10 tons), medium (10-30 tons), heavy (30-100 tons), or superheavy (>100 tons). Influences equipment selection and SWL requirements.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of cargo categories handled at the port — FCL (Full Container Load), LCL (Less than Container Load), bulk dry, bulk liquid, breakbulk, RoRo, project cargo, OOG (Out of Gauge), reefer, dangerous goods (IMDG), and empty containers. Captures category code, category name, handling method, applicable equipment types, storage area type (CY, CFS, bulk shed, liquid berth), and special handling requirements. Used across cargo, terminal, tariff, and compliance domains.'
}

Table masterdata.packaging_type {
  packaging_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the packaging type record. Primary key.']
  imdg_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.imdg_class. Business justification: Packaging types for dangerous goods reference IMDG hazard classifications to determine compatibility and handling requirements. Currently stores imdg_class_code as STRING; normalizing to FK allows joi']
  parent_packaging_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on packaging_type (parent_packaging_type_id)']
  applicable_transport_modes varchar [note: 'Transport modes for which this packaging type is approved (e.g., sea, road, rail, air, intermodal). Multiple modes may be comma-separated. Used for intermodal logistics coordination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this packaging type record was first created in the master data system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  customs_declaration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cargo in this packaging type requires specific customs declaration or documentation beyond standard manifest entries.']
  data_steward varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business unit or individual responsible for maintaining this packaging type master data. Typically Safety and Compliance department.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this packaging type classification becomes effective and can be used in operational systems. Used for regulatory update management.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this packaging type classification remains valid. Null for currently valid records. Used for managing regulatory updates and deprecated packaging codes.']
  fumigation_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging type can withstand fumigation treatment for quarantine and phytosanitary compliance without damage or contamination.']
  handling_equipment_type varchar [note: 'Recommended or required handling equipment for this packaging type (e.g., forklift, pallet jack, crane, Rubber Tyred Gantry (RTG), Mobile Harbour Crane (MHC)). Multiple types may be comma-separated.']
  height_mm varchar [note: 'External height dimension of the packaging in millimeters. Used for stowage planning and container loading optimization.']
  imdg_packing_group varchar [note: 'IMDG packing group compatibility indicating the degree of danger: I (high danger), II (medium danger), III (low danger), or not applicable for non-dangerous goods packaging.']
  iso_standard_version varchar [note: 'Version of the ISO standard this packaging type conforms to (e.g., ISO 3394:2012, ISO 9711:1990). Used for compliance verification and audit trails.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this packaging type record was last updated. Used for change tracking and synchronization with downstream systems.']
  length_mm varchar [note: 'External length dimension of the packaging in millimeters. Used for stowage planning and container loading optimization.']
  marpol_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging type meets MARPOL requirements for prevention of marine pollution, particularly relevant for hazardous substance packaging.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Primary material composition of the packaging (e.g., steel, aluminium, wood, fibreboard, plastic, textile, composite). Critical for IMDG compliance and handling requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: steel|aluminium|wood|plywood|fibreboard|plastic|textile|paper|composite|glass|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  maximum_capacity_liters decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity of the packaging in liters. Used for liquid and bulk cargo capacity planning and compliance verification.']
  maximum_gross_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable gross weight (packaging plus contents) in kilograms. Critical for Safe Working Load (SWL) compliance and handling equipment selection.']
  maximum_stack_height varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units that can be safely stacked vertically. Null if not stackable. Used for warehouse and yard stacking operations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks, clarifications, or operational notes related to this packaging type. Used for capturing context not covered by structured fields.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this packaging type in the master data system. Controls whether the packaging type can be used in new cargo bookings and manifests.']
  packaging_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the packaging type for operational grouping and handling instructions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drum|box|bag|crate|ibc|cylinder|jerrycan|pallet|composite|bulk|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  packaging_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the packaging type (e.g., Steel Drum, Fibreboard Box, Intermediate Bulk Container, Wooden Crate, Plastic Jerrycan, Gas Cylinder, Pallet).']
  reefer_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging type is suitable for use in refrigerated containers for temperature-controlled cargo.']
  regulatory_update_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency and schedule of regulatory updates for this packaging type (e.g., biennial UN update, annual IMDG amendment). Used for compliance calendar management.']
  segregation_group varchar [note: 'Segregation group code for dangerous goods stowage planning. Defines which other packaging types or cargo classes this packaging must be segregated from during transport and storage.']
  solas_vgm_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SOLAS VGM verification requirements apply to cargo shipped in this packaging type. Mandatory for containerized cargo under SOLAS Convention.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this packaging type data originates (e.g., NAVIS N4, Port Community System (PCS), SAP Materials Management (MM)).']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling, stowage, or operational instructions specific to this packaging type (e.g., keep upright, fragile, protect from moisture, segregation requirements).']
  stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging type can be safely stacked during storage and transport. Critical for yard management and Container Freight Station (CFS) operations.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Empty weight of the packaging itself in kilograms, excluding contents. Used for net weight calculations and cargo documentation.']
  tariff_classification_code varchar [note: 'Internal tariff code used for billing and Terminal Handling Charge (THC) calculation. Links packaging type to applicable port tariff schedules.']
  un_packaging_code varchar [note: 'Standard UN packaging code as defined by UN Recommendation 21 for dangerous goods transport. Two to four character alphanumeric code identifying the packaging type (e.g., 1A for steel drum, 4G for fibreboard box).']
  un_recommendation_version varchar [note: 'Version of UN Recommendation 21 or UN Model Regulations this packaging type is classified under. Updated biennially to reflect regulatory changes.']
  un_test_certificate_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this packaging type requires UN performance testing and certification for dangerous goods transport under IMDG regulations.']
  width_mm varchar [note: 'External width dimension of the packaging in millimeters. Used for stowage planning and container loading optimization.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of cargo packaging types as defined by UN Recommendation 21 and used in dangerous goods declarations, cargo manifests, and customs filings. Captures UN packaging code, packaging name (drum, bag, pallet, crate, IBC, cylinder, etc.), material type, IMDG packing group compatibility (FK to imdg_class for DG-rated packaging), maximum capacity, applicable transport regulations, and effectivity dates for regulatory updates. Data steward: Safety & Compliance. SSOT for packaging classification used in cargo documentation, IMDG compliance, and customs declarations.'
}

Table masterdata.resource {
  resource_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for resource']
  commodity_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.commodity_code. Business justification: resource has commodity_code (STRING) which matches commodity_code.hs_code business key. When resource represents cargo or commodity assets, it should FK to commodity_code master for HS classification.']
  container_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.container_type. Business justification: resource has iso_type_code (STRING) which matches container_type.iso_type_code business key. When resource represents a container asset, it should FK to container_type master for ISO type classificati']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'EDI partner identification code for electronic messaging and data exchange related to this resource.']
  flag_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: resource has flag_country_code (STRING) which should reference country master. When resource represents a vessel or flagged asset, the flag country should FK to country table. Using flag_country_id as']
  parent_resource_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on resource (parent_resource_id)']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: resource has location_code (STRING) which matches port_location.location_code business key. Resources are physically located at port locations (berths, yards, etc.). Adding port_location_id FK normali']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original purchase or acquisition cost of the resource in the base currency.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the capacity value (TEU=Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit, FEU=Forty-foot Equivalent Unit, CBM=Cubic Meter, DWT=Deadweight Tonnage, MT=Metric Ton, SQM=Square Meter).']
  capacity_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Principal quantitative measurement of the resource\'s capacity or capability (e.g., TEU for containers, DWT for vessels, square meters for terminals).']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the resource\'s current certification or compliance documentation.']
  commission_date date [note: 'Date when the resource was commissioned and put into operational service.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this resource record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values associated with the resource.']
  current_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current accounting book value of the resource after depreciation.']
  customs_bond_number varchar [note: 'Customs bond or guarantee number associated with the resource for international trade compliance.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the resource including specifications, features, and operational characteristics.']
  height_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical height dimension of the resource in meters.']
  home_port_code varchar [note: 'Primary or home port location for the resource using UN/LOCODE standard.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Insurance policy number covering the resource for liability, damage, or loss.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this resource record is currently active and available for use in operational systems.']
  is_hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the resource is certified for handling or transporting hazardous materials per IMDG Code.']
  is_reefer_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the resource has refrigeration capability for temperature-controlled cargo (applicable to containers, vessels, terminals).']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection or survey conducted on the resource.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this resource record was last updated or modified.']
  length_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length dimension of the resource in meters.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date when the resource was manufactured or built.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or builder of the resource (applicable to equipment, containers, vessels).']
  max_gross_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable gross weight (including cargo) for the resource in kilograms.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model or series identifier for the resource.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory inspection or survey of the resource.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to the resource.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity responsible for operating or managing the resource (may differ from owner).']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity that owns the resource.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or license number issued by regulatory authorities (e.g., vessel IMO number, equipment registration).']
  resource_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the resource used across operational systems and partner communications.']
  resource_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the resource for display and reporting purposes.']
  resource_subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing granular categorization within the resource type (e.g., container types: 20GP, 40HC, 45HC; vessel types: feeder, panamax, post-panamax).']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the resource indicating its functional category within maritime logistics operations.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the resource was retired from active service or decommissioned.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'Unique RFID tag identifier attached to the resource for automated tracking and identification.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer for individual resource identification and tracking.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the resource indicating its operational availability and state.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Empty weight of the resource in kilograms (applicable to containers, equipment, cargo units).']
  width_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical width dimension of the resource in meters.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for resource. Referenced by resource_id.'
}

Table security.facility_security_plan {
  facility_security_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Port Facility Security Plan (PFSP) record. Primary key for this entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the designated Port Facility Security Officer responsible for implementing and maintaining this security plan.']
  isps_facility_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port facility covered by this security plan.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Each port location (berth, terminal, facility) requires its own approved FSP under ISPS Code. FSPs are location-specific with unique approval certificates, PFSO assignments, and security measures tail']
  superseded_facility_security_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on facility_security_plan (superseded_facility_security_plan_id)']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: FSPs reference specific vessels for security assessments when high-risk vessels call. ISPS Code requires facility to assess vessel security history and characteristics before interface. New attribute ']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: FSPs define security measures by vessel type per ISPS Code. Tankers, container ships, and passenger vessels require different security protocols (MARSEC level measures, screening requirements, access ']
  access_control_system_type varchar [note: 'Type or model of the access control system specified in the Port Facility Security Plan (e.g., RFID, biometric, card-based).']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of amendments or revisions made to the Port Facility Security Plan since initial approval.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the flag state administration or Recognized Security Organization (RSO) that approved the PFSP.']
  approval_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate reference number issued by the approval authority upon PFSP approval.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the Port Facility Security Plan was officially approved by the designated authority.']
  cctv_coverage_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the port facility area covered by CCTV surveillance systems as specified in the security plan.']
  compliance_certificate_expiry date [note: 'Expiry date of the ISPS Code compliance certificate associated with this Port Facility Security Plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this Port Facility Security Plan record was first created in the system.']
  cybersecurity_measures_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Port Facility Security Plan includes specific cybersecurity measures for IT/OT systems protection.']
  document_classification varchar [note: 'Security classification level assigned to the Port Facility Security Plan document itself.']
  document_storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital location where the official Port Facility Security Plan document is securely stored.']
  drill_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which security drills and exercises must be conducted as specified in the plan.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the approved Port Facility Security Plan becomes operationally effective and binding.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current Port Facility Security Plan expires and requires renewal or reapproval. Nullable for plans with indefinite validity subject to periodic review.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or revision to the Port Facility Security Plan.']
  last_amendment_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of the changes made in the most recent amendment to the Port Facility Security Plan.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent internal or external audit conducted on the Port Facility Security Plan implementation.']
  last_audit_outcome varchar [note: 'Result or finding of the most recent audit of the Port Facility Security Plan.']
  last_drill_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent security drill or exercise conducted under this Port Facility Security Plan.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this Port Facility Security Plan record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  marsec_level_1_measures varchar [note: 'Summary of security measures and procedures defined in the plan for Maritime Security (MARSEC) Level 1 (normal operations).']
  marsec_level_2_measures varchar [note: 'Summary of heightened security measures and procedures defined in the plan for Maritime Security (MARSEC) Level 2 (heightened risk).']
  marsec_level_3_measures varchar [note: 'Summary of exceptional security measures and procedures defined in the plan for Maritime Security (MARSEC) Level 3 (probable or imminent security incident).']
  next_drill_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory security drill or exercise.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review or revalidation of the Port Facility Security Plan.']
  patrol_frequency_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Frequency in hours at which security patrols must be conducted as specified in the Port Facility Security Plan.']
  pfso_contact_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the designated Port Facility Security Officer.']
  pfso_email_address varchar [note: 'Official email address for the designated Port Facility Security Officer.']
  pfso_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated Port Facility Security Officer at the time of plan approval.']
  plan_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned to the Port Facility Security Plan by the approval authority. Externally-known unique identifier for the plan.']
  plan_scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope and coverage of the Port Facility Security Plan, including physical boundaries, operations covered, and exclusions.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the Port Facility Security Plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|active|under_review|suspended|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the approved Port Facility Security Plan following major.minor format (e.g., 2.1, 3.0).']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to the Port Facility Security Plan.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which the Port Facility Security Plan must be reviewed and updated (e.g., 12, 24, 36 months).']
  security_assessment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the Port Facility Security Assessment (PFSA) upon which this plan is based.']
  threat_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent threat assessment conducted for the port facility as part of the security plan maintenance.']
  training_program_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the security training and drill program defined in the Port Facility Security Plan.']

  Note: 'Master record for the Port Facility Security Plan (PFSP) as mandated by ISPS Code Part B. Captures the approved plan version, approval authority (flag state / RSO), plan scope covering all MARSEC levels (1, 2, 3), designated Port Facility Security Officer (PFSO), plan review cycle, last audit date, next review date, approval certificate reference, and amendment history. This is the foundational ISPS compliance document for each port facility.'
}

Table security.zone {
  zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the security zone within the port facility. Primary key for the security zone registry.']
  parent_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on zone (parent_zone_id)']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Security zones are physical subdivisions of port locations (restricted areas, public areas, cargo zones). Each zone maps to a specific berth, terminal, or yard location for access control, patrol rout']
  primary_zone_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the security officer or manager responsible for this zone. Links to workforce or security personnel registry.']
  zone_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  zone_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  access_control_requirement_marsec1 varchar [note: 'Detailed description of access control measures required for this zone when operating at MARSEC Level 1 (normal security). Includes badge requirements, escort policies, vehicle inspection protocols, and entry authorization procedures.']
  access_control_requirement_marsec2 varchar [note: 'Enhanced access control measures required for this zone when operating at MARSEC Level 2 (heightened security threat). Typically includes additional screening, reduced access lists, mandatory escorts, and enhanced vehicle searches.']
  access_control_requirement_marsec3 varchar [note: 'Maximum security access control measures required for this zone when operating at MARSEC Level 3 (imminent security threat). May include zone closure, armed security, biometric verification, and complete lockdown procedures.']
  access_point_count varchar [note: 'Number of controlled access points (gates, doors, turnstiles) into the security zone. Used for access control planning and security resource allocation.']
  area_square_meters decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total area of the security zone measured in square meters. Used for resource allocation, patrol planning, and CCTV coverage calculations.']
  boundary_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the physical boundaries of the security zone. Includes landmarks, fence lines, berth numbers, building identifiers, and geographic references for operational clarity.']
  boundary_gis_polygon varchar [note: 'GIS polygon coordinates defining the precise geographic boundaries of the security zone in WKT (Well-Known Text) format. Used for spatial analysis, mapping, and automated geofencing in access control systems.']
  cctv_camera_count varchar [note: 'Number of CCTV cameras actively monitoring this security zone. Used for surveillance coverage assessment and maintenance planning.']
  cctv_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security zone is covered by CCTV surveillance systems. True if cameras monitor the zone, false if no camera coverage exists.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this security zone record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  critical_infrastructure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this security zone contains critical infrastructure as defined by national security regulations. True for zones containing fuel storage, power generation, control centers, or other critical assets.']
  cybersecurity_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone includes IT/OT (Information Technology/Operational Technology) systems requiring cybersecurity controls. True for zones with control rooms, server facilities, or networked operational systems.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when this security zone designation became effective. Used for historical tracking and compliance documentation.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when this security zone designation expires or is scheduled for review. Null for indefinite zones. Used for temporary security zones or zones under periodic review.']
  emergency_assembly_point varchar [note: 'Designated emergency assembly point location for personnel evacuating from this security zone. Used for emergency response planning and safety drills.']
  incident_count_ytd varchar [note: 'Total number of security incidents recorded in this zone during the current calendar year. Used for security performance monitoring and resource allocation.']
  last_incident_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent security incident recorded in this zone. Used for risk assessment and security effectiveness analysis. Null if no incidents have occurred.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this security zone record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  last_risk_assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent security risk assessment was conducted for this zone. ISPS Code requires periodic reassessment of security zones.']
  lighting_adequacy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security zone has adequate lighting for security purposes as per ISPS Code requirements. True if lighting meets standards, false if lighting is insufficient or requires upgrade.']
